jobTitle: Accounts Payable Officer - Fixed Term Contract
jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










About the role:

The Accounts Payable Officer is responsible for the full end-to-end accounts payable process, ensuring supplier invoices are processed accurately and paid on time. This role plays a key part in maintaining financial integrity, supporting strong supplier relationships , and ensuring compliance with internal controls and reporting requirements.



Based at our Pōkeno factory and reporting to the Finance Manager, you will work in a high-volume environment managing invoice processing, reconciliations, and payment runs, while continuously improving AP processes and contributing to month-end and audit activities.



More specifically, you will be involved in:


- Managing the full AP lifecycle including supplier account setup and maintenance, raising purchase orders, invoice matching and coding, credit note processing and payment execution
- Performing monthly creditor reconciliations, resolving discrepancies and responding promptly to supplier queries via phone and email.
- Processing credit card and petty cash reconciliation and managing the GR/IR general ledger accounts
- Supporting month-end close, preparing spend analysis, assisting with audit documentation and contributing to continuous process improvements.

What you will bring:


- Minimum of 3+ years proven accounts payable experience in a high-volume environment
- Strong attention to detail and the ability to maintain a high level of accuracy and confidentiality
- Experience working with ERP systems (Microsoft Navision/MS Dynamics NAV highly regarded)
- Strong Excel skills and sound understanding of general ledger processes
- A customer-focused mindset with the ability to build strong relationships across the business and with external suppliers
- A Degree or Diploma in Accounting or Finance is preferred. Experience within consumer goods or manufacturing environments is highly regarded

jobTitle: Finance Business Partner - 12 month Fixed-term Contract - Southbank
jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet. The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



At The a2 Milk Company , we’re passionate about doing dairy differently. Guided by science and driven by a pioneering spirit, we exist to create a world where dairy nourishes all people and the planet.

We are now seeking a commercially astute Finance Business Partner to join our ANZ / International Finance team on a 12-month maximum term contract , based in our vibrant Southbank office .

Working closely with the Head of Commercial Finance and another Finance Business Partner, this role is responsible for managing month-end processes and preparing and analysing financial reports for the International business unit.

The position also holds responsibility for financial reporting, balance sheet reviews and ensuring compliance with audit requirements.



More about the Opportunity:

Responsibility for all management accounting activities for the international business unit including:


- EOM management and financial reports
- EOM journals including trade, marketing and distribution accruals
- P&L preparation for WD4 management review
- Balance sheet reconciliations
- Variance analysis between actual and budget/forecast
- Sales and trade spend tracking and management
- Prepare monthly sales reports and schedule forward sales at month end
- Administration of salesforce database and weekly out of stock reporting
- SG&A reporting and actuals for ANZ/International business units to ensure budgets are delivered
- Ensure marketing tracker is updated for international activities
- Perform monthly reconciliation of the Amazon and Online store P&L, including calculating sales volumes and ensuring the accurate reporting of revenue and COGS in Oracle
- Set up and update pricing in Oracle to ensure accurate and timely reflection of price changes.
- Conduct financial evaluation and analysis for pricing and NPD initiatives / business cases.
- Manage and respond to internal and external audit queries.

What We’re Looking For

We are seeking an experienced Finance professional who operates effectively in a fast-paced environment and is confident working independently while collaborating closely within the finance team.

You will bring:


- 3–5 years’ experience in a commercial accounting or finance role
- FMCG, consumer products or retail finance experience (highly regarded)
- Good analysis and reporting skills
- Advanced excel skills and exposure to large ERP system like Oracle/SAP
- Degree qualified in accounting (Bachelor of Commerce etc)
- CA/CPA qualified or working towards completion
- Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
- Resilience, flexibility and the ability to manage competing priorities You will be detail-oriented, analytically strong and comfortable working with senior finance stakeholders.

If you are a capable finance professional who takes ownership and delivers accurate, timely insights, we welcome your application.



Why Join Us?

The a2 Milk Company Australia is a certified Family Inclusive Workplace committed to improving our team members work life wellbeing with leading policies and practices on flexible work, parental leave, family care and family wellbeing. This role can be worked flexibly, allowing us to support our team members in balancing their personal and professional lives. Our family-friendly culture aims to foster workforce participation, career development, and mental health and wellbeing

Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



The role:

As the Food Safety Manager, based in Auckland, you will work closely with cross-functional teams, factories, and third-party manufacturers to strengthen and elevate our food safety culture. You will champion best-practice standards and guide teams to meet and exceed a2MC’s food safety expectations.



You will play a pivotal role in shaping and leading the food safety strategy across The a2 Milk Company. This includes providing expert leadership on food safety requirements for all our products, projects, and installations, and driving programs and continuous improvement initiatives that support our goal of achieving zero food-safety-related defects across our supply chain and partner network.



More specifically, you will:


- Establish and maintain Food Safety standards and compliance requirements across a2MC.
- Develop and implement food safety strategies, programs, and governance frameworks that ensure zero food safety defects.
- Provide technical expertise on food safety requirements for all products and projects.
- Lead the development of pre-HACCP assessments for innovations, renovations, and improvement initiatives to mitigate allergen, chemical, microbiological, nutritional, and physical hazards.
- Support factories and third-party manufacturers (3PMs) in resolving food safety issues using structured problem-solving methodologies.
- Drive continuous improvement initiatives across the supply chain and operations.
- Institutionalise standard routines and pre-requisite programs and verify effective implementation with factories and 3PMs.
- Lead and verify the implementation of the HACCP Plan across all relevant operations.
- Design and deliver training programs to uplift food safety capability across the organisation.
- Analyse results, trends, and insights to develop ongoing improvement plans in food safety performance.

What you will bring:


- Broad knowledge of food processing, food safety systems, and current GMP
- A tertiary qualification in Food Science, Food Technology, Microbiology, or a related discipline is preferred.
- Expertise in food science, food microbiology, and applied food safety principles.
- Strong capability with problem-solving tools and structured investigation methods.
- Proven leadership skills with the ability to influence, guide, and uplift capability across teams.
- Excellent communication and presentation skills, with strong stakeholder-engagement experience.
- High attention to detail and the ability to work collaboratively across diverse teams and external partners.
- Experience in a food manufacturing environment (mandatory).
- Experience in infant nutrition or closely regulated nutrition categories (mandatory).
- Demonstrated experience establishing and leading HACCP studies, and strong working knowledge of Pre-requisite Programs, Zoning, and Pathogen Monitoring.

We are committed to enhancing our team members' work-life wellbeing with leading policies and practices on flexible work, parental leave, family care, and family wellbeing. This role can be worked flexibly, ensuring a healthy balance between work and personal life. Our family-friendly culture is designed to support workforce participation, career development, and mental health and wellbeing.



Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



About the role:

Based at our Pokeno manufacturing site, you will play a critical role in implementing food safety strategies and ensuring consistent compliance across the site. You will be a key driver of proactive food safety controls and continuous improvement within the factory.



More specifically, you will be involved in:


- Implementing and verifying factory food safety programs including cGMP, HACCP, zoning, pathogen management and foreign matter control
- Developing and delivering food safety training and building capability across the site
- Conducting GMP and food safety audits, and driving corrective and preventative actions
- Verifying the effective execution of the HACCP system and pre-requisite programs
- Analysing trends, data and results to identify risks and drive continuous improvement initiatives
- Supporting investigation and resolution of food safety incidents using structured problem-solving tools
- Driving a proactive food safety culture with the goal of zero food safety defects

What you will bring:




- Experience in a food manufacturing environment, ideally within infant nutrition
- Strong working knowledge of food safety systems, HACCP, cGMP and regulatory requirements
- Background in food science, food microbiology or food processing
- Experience establishing, leading or supporting HACCP studies
- Knowledge of zoning principles and pathogen monitoring programs
- Confident communication and training delivery skills, with the ability to influence on the factory floor
- A proactive, detail-focused mindset with strong problem-solving capability

What we offer


- Medical, Life & Income Protection Insurance.
- Annual Bonus.
- A supportive team culture with learning and development opportunities.
- Free breakfast and lunch provided in our onsite cafeteria.
- Subsidised gym membership.
- Social club.


Apply now

If this sounds like you, please submit your CV. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for a phone screening.



Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

We’re excited to share that our business has recently joined the a2 Milk Company family. Formerly known as Yashili Dairy NZ Ltd, we are now operating as a2 Nutritionals NZ Limited, based in Pokeno.



The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



About the role:

The Logistics Manager plays a critical leadership role in overseeing end-to-end site logistics operations, ensuring the safe, compliant, timely and cost-effective movement of ingredients and finished goods. Based at Pokeno and reporting to the Supply Chain Manager, this role leads all on-site warehousing functions while managing the interface with third-party logistics (3PL) providers.



You will be responsible for safeguarding service levels, inventory accuracy and export documentation quality, while partnering closely with Manufacturing, Quality, Procurement, Planning and Finance teams to ensure seamless operational execution.



More specifically, you will be involved in:


- Managing inbound and outbound logistics flows for raw materials, packaging and finished goods between site, suppliers and 3PL warehouses
- Acting as the primary operational contact for 3PL providers, monitoring SLA and KPI performance (OTIF, stock accuracy, damages, cycle counts) and driving issue resolution
- Leading inventory control processes including reconciliations, cycle counts, stocktakes, investigations and root cause analysis
- Overseeing export documentation and finished goods invoicing to ensure compliance and accuracy

What you will bring:




- 10+ years’ experience in logistics, warehousing or supply chain roles within FMCG, manufacturing or food processing environments
- Demonstrated experience managing third-party logistics (3PL) providers and performance outcomes
- Strong understanding of inventory control, export logistics and regulatory compliance requirements
- Proven ability to drive continuous improvement, optimise logistics costs and enhance service performance
- Tertiary qualification in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business or related discipline (or equivalent experience)
- Experience in dairy, infant formula or other regulated food environments will be highly regarded.

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description













- Work with one of Australia’s most disruptive dairy brands
- Influence category strategy, not just execution
- Join a high-performing ANZ Sales team
- Grow your career in a fast-moving global business.

Join a business that does dairy differently.

At The a2 Milk Company, we’re driven by science, a pioneering spirit and a bold ambition to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes people and the planet.

As we continue to grow across ANZ and globally, we’re looking for a commercially sharp, data-savvy and relationship-driven Senior National Account Manager to lead key national customers and unlock the next phase of category growth.

If you’re a high-performing FMCG Account Manager ready to step into bigger ownership, deeper strategic influence and broader commercial impact this could be your move.




About the Opportunity:

Reporting to the Head of Sales, this role owns delivery of volume, net sales, profit and gross margin targets across assigned national customers.

This is a pivotal role where you’ll be the primary commercial contact, leading strategic customer engagement, driving best-in-class Joint Business Planning and ensuring flawless in-store execution across the a2 portfolio. This role is more than account management, it’s about shaping category growth, influencing senior buyers and partnering cross-functionally to deliver sustainable, profitable outcomes.

What you will be responsible for.


- Owning full P&L delivery across national accounts
- Leading end-to-end customer strategy and Joint Business Planning
- Identifying ranging, pricing, distribution and promotional opportunities to accelerate growth
- Delivering compelling, data-driven category reviews
- Managing trade investment with strong ROI discipline
- Driving forecasting accuracy and collaborating closely with Supply, Marketing and Finance
- Contributing to the broader Senior Retail Sales Leadership Team


More About You

You are most likely here because you are looking for something different. We think you will love this role If you are a rising FMCG star with:


- Experience managing national or major grocery accounts
- Strong commercial and analytical capability (Excel, PowerPoint, Circana)
- Current experience with influencing category strategies
- You are a BIG picture thinker with a growth mindset and ideas-sharer!
- You are relationship driven, with proven negotiation skills and confidence engaging senior buyers
- A results-driven, resilient and collaborative mindset
- You are a team player but can work autonomously with an ear open for new ideas and developing your own capabilities.
- If you’re ready to step into a bigger commercial platform and help shape the future of dairy in ANZ we’d love to hear from you


You don't have to have 100% of the points above to apply. We are looking for a relationship's driver and culture-champion! Someone with the energy, attitude and mindset to grow with our a2MC team. If this sounds like you then we encourage you to hit the apply button below.




Why Join Us?

The a2 Milk Company Australia is a certified Family Inclusive Workplace committed to improving our team members work life wellbeing with leading policies and practices on flexible work, parental leave, family care and family wellbeing. This role can be worked flexibly, allowing us to support our team members in balancing their personal and professional lives. Our family-friendly culture aims to foster workforce participation, career development, and mental health and wellbeing.

Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

We’re excited to share that our business has recently joined the a2 Milk Company family. Formerly known as Yashili Dairy NZ Ltd, we are now operating as a2 Nutritionals NZ Limited, based in Pokeno.



The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



About the role:

Reporting to the Head of Operations and based at Pokeno, you will be responsible for leading and managing the execution of projects from a supply chain and operations perspective. Projects will include new product creation and development (NPD), supply chain network optimisation, manufacturing capability uplift, cost improvement initiatives and broader a2MC strategic programmes.



You will work cross-functionally with Operations, Planning, Procurement, Quality, R&D and Commercial teams to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget and in line with quality and compliance requirements.



More specifically, you will be involved in:


- Leading end-to-end project management for NPD launches including planning, risk management, stakeholder coordination and go-live execution
- Driving supply chain optimisation initiatives including inventory management, S&OP alignment, network planning and cost reduction projects
- Developing and maintaining detailed project plans, RAID logs, budgets and executive reporting dashboards
- Facilitating cross-functional workshops and ensuring alignment across manufacturing, logistics and commercial teams

About you:


- Minimum of 8+ years proven experience in OT/automation roles within dairy, food, pharma, or FMCG, with 3+ years leading teams
- Strong expertise in PLC, SCADA, HMI, MES, historians, and industrial networks (EtherNet/IP, Profinet), with the ability to troubleshoot and optimise OT systems
- Experience leading CAPEX, digital transformation, and continuous improvement projects from URS through qualification
- Demonstrated ability to manage vendors, contractors, and cross-functional stakeholders effectively
- Exposure to industrial cybersecurity, IT/OT integration, and regulated manufacturing environments is highly regarded
- Tertiary qualification in Automation, Electrical Engineering, Control Systems, Mechatronics, or related discipline preferred
jobDescriptionHtml:
Position Description

Our Story:

We’re excited to share that our business has recently joined the a2 Milk Company family. Formerly known as Yashili Dairy NZ Ltd, we are now operating as a2 Nutritionals NZ Limited, based in Pokeno.

 

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy. 

 

About the role:

Reporting to the Head of Operations and based at Pokeno, you will be responsible for leading and managing the execution of projects from a supply chain and operations perspective. Projects will include new product creation and development (NPD), supply chain network optimisation, manufacturing capability uplift, cost improvement initiatives and broader a2MC strategic programmes.

 

You will work cross-functionally with Operations, Planning, Procurement, Quality, R&D and Commercial teams to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget and in line with quality and compliance requirements.

 

More specifically, you will be involved in:

  • Leading end-to-end project management for NPD launches including planning, risk management, stakeholder coordination and go-live execution
  • Driving supply chain optimisation initiatives including inventory management, S&OP alignment, network planning and cost reduction projects
  • Developing and maintaining detailed project plans, RAID logs, budgets and executive reporting dashboards
  • Facilitating cross-functional workshops and ensuring alignment across manufacturing, logistics and commercial teams

 

About you: 

  • Minimum of 8+ years proven experience in OT/automation roles within dairy, food, pharma, or FMCG, with 3+ years leading teams
  • Strong expertise in PLC, SCADA, HMI, MES, historians, and industrial networks (EtherNet/IP, Profinet), with the ability to troubleshoot and optimise OT systems
  • Experience leading CAPEX, digital transformation, and continuous improvement projects from URS through qualification
  • Demonstrated ability to manage vendors, contractors, and cross-functional stakeholders effectively
  • Exposure to industrial cybersecurity, IT/OT integration, and regulated manufacturing environments is highly regarded
  • Tertiary qualification in Automation, Electrical Engineering, Control Systems, Mechatronics, or related discipline preferred

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet. We seek to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by promoting a more sustainable approach to dairy.

Opportunities for Production Operators for our newly structured shift times at our Smeaton Grange Site - Immediate Start!


- Day shift: 5.00 am to 1.06 pm
- Afternoon Shift: 1.00 pm to 9.06 pm At The a2 Milk Company, we’re not just about making dairy differently we’re about creating a workplace where people feel safe, supported, and part of something bigger. Guided by our BOLD values and team culture we believe success comes from collaboration, respect, and bringing out the best in each other.

The Role - what you will be doing.

We are seeking dedicated Production Operators to join our Smeaton Grange team in permanent full-time roles offering varied work across production and warehouse operations. Smeaton Grange is our liquid milk bottling plant where we bottle over 850,000 litres of our delicious a2 Milk per week. This role will give you the opportunity to learn, develop and grow a rewarding career in an inclusive and supportive environment.

In this role, you’ll:


- Work side by side with an experienced, collaborative, and close-knit team.
- Operate and monitor equipment to keep our lines safe and efficient to ensure smooth production.
- Rotate across different tasks – filling, packing, processing, dispatch and more – keeping each day varied.
- Help uphold our safety-first culture and take pride in producing high-quality products.
- Work across rotating day and afternoon shifts (variety and increased pay rate) Why You’ll Love Working Here


- Team culture at its best : We’re a collaborative group that celebrates wins together and supports each other through challenges.
- Long-term security : These are permanent roles with clear career growth opportunities in a strong global company with a local presence.
- New Static Shifts: No night shifts! When applying for this role please highlight your preferred shift of either morning or afternoon shifts (allowances/penalties attached to hours outside of the ordinary hours)
- Inclusive environment : Diversity is embraced, and every voice is valued.
- Learning every day : Ongoing training and development to help you grow your skills.
- Making an impact : Be part of a company that is pioneering positive change in the dairy industry across wellbeing, sustainability and farm welfare. What We’re Looking For


- Team players who thrive in a collaborative environment with great communication skills.
- FMCG or food manufacturing experience is essential - dairy experience highly regarded
- Experience operating filling machines and related equipment
- Strong written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills
- Safety-conscious, reliable, and willing to learn.
- A forklift licence will open additional opportunities, though it’s not required. Apply Now

If you want to be part of a team where people truly look out for one another while building a long-term career - we would love to hear from you.




Why Join The a2 Milk Company

The a2 Milk Company Australia is a certified Family Inclusive Workplace, dedicated to enhancing our team members' work-life wellbeing through leading policies and practices on flexible work, parental leave, family care, and family wellbeing. This role can be worked flexibly, allowing us to support our team members in balancing their personal and professional lives. Our family-friendly culture aims to foster workforce participation, career development, and mental health and wellbeing
Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










At The a2 Milk Company , we’re doing dairy differently. Backed by science and driven by a pioneering spirit, our vision is bold: to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes people and the planet. As we continue to grow across ANZ and globally, we’re strengthening our production leadership team at Smeaton Grange.

We’re now looking for an experienced Production / Manufacturing Team Leader from a food or beverage environment who is ready to step into a key leadership role within a high-performing dairy operation.

More about the Role;

This role is a lot more than shift supervision. Reporting to the Production & Continuous Improvement Manager, you’ll lead a team of approximately 20 team members (including two Leading Hands) across processing, packaging and warehouse operations. You will have the opportunity to not only grow a success-driven career but get involved in strategy with a key focus on quality across the site.

You will:

• Lead daily production delivery, scheduling and execution
• Drive safety, quality and compliance standards (HACCP, GMP)
• Partner closely with Maintenance, Quality and Supply Chain
• Own labour utilisation, performance and engagement outcomes
• Coach and develop future leaders within your team

This is a salaried leadership role , offering flexibility and requiring management across shifts when required. Our Leading Hands manage shift coverage, and we value leaders who are adaptable and outcome-focused to drive leadership, collaboration and productivity.

What you’ll bring

We’re looking for a self-driven, hands-on operational leader who understands what great looks like on the floor.

Must have:
• Experience in food manufacturing (FMCG) – ideally bottling or beverage environments
• Experience leading teams in a unionised site
• Strong safety leadership and continuous improvement mindset
• Proven accountability for quality outcomes
• Experience managing labour allocation and operational performance

Highly regarded:
• Lean Manufacturing knowledge (5 Why, DMAIC, CI projects)
• Exposure to high-volume FMCG environments (e.g. dairy, beverage, or similar)
• Experience working across multiple shifts in dynamic production settings

This role would suit a people-driven leader who leads constructively, holds high standards and is passionate about building capability across your people, while uplifting processes and quality by leading from the floor.

Why join a2 Milk?

The a2 Milk Company Australia is a certified Family Inclusive Workplace committed to improving our team members work life wellbeing with leading policies and practices on flexible work, parental leave, family care and family wellbeing. This role can be worked flexibly, allowing us to support our team members in balancing their personal and professional lives. Our family-friendly culture aims to foster workforce participation, career development, and mental health and wellbeing

Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



Your Role:

We are seeking a Purchasing Coordinator to join our team at our Pōkeno manufacturing site, reporting to the Purchasing Manager.



In this role, you will be responsible for executing purchasing activities for direct materials, ensuring business requirements are met in terms of ordering, availability, stockholding and quality. You will also support supplier performance monitoring and the management of quality-related incidents, while working closely with internal teams and external partners to support production requirements.



This role suits someone who thrives in a hands-on, site-based environment , enjoys working closely with manufacturing, quality and warehouse teams, and takes pride in accuracy, follow-up and strong supplier relationships.



More specifically you will be involved in:


- Preparing and processing purchase orders in line with MRP requirements, lead times and purchasing parameters
- Monitoring delivery schedules , managing overdue or delayed orders and maintaining shipment schedules for imported materials
- Maintaining appropriate inventory levels , safety stocks and rolling forecasts for suppliers
- Working with Finance and Warehouse teams to manage goods receipting, returns, credits, GRNI and GIRN
- Coordinating with suppliers, logistics providers and customs agents to support timely material availability
- Monitoring and reporting on supplier performance , documentation compliance and quality non-conformances
- Supporting cross-functional collaboration with Manufacturing, Quality, Planning and Scheduling teams

What you will bring:


- 2–3 years’ experience in a purchasing, procurement or supply chain environment
- Understanding of MRP, inventory control and purchasing processes
- Experience working with ERP systems (NAV / Business Central advantageous)
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications
- Experience in manufacturing, FMCG, food or dairy environments (preferred)
- Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively across teams and with suppliers

We are committed to enhancing our team members' work-life wellbeing with leading policies and practices on flexible work, parental leave, family care, and family wellbeing. This role can be worked flexibly, ensuring a healthy balance between work and personal life. Our family-friendly culture is designed to support workforce participation, career development, and mental health and wellbeing.





Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



Your Role:

At The a2 Milk Company, we believe in doing dairy differently. Our brand is built on care – for our consumers, our partners, and the quality of every drop we produce. As we continue to grow, we are looking for a Supply Chain Co-ordinator to join our New Zealand-based Supply Chain team in Auckland.



Reporting to the Group Supply Chain Manager, this role plays a critical part in the day-to-day execution of our supply chain, ensuring product flows smoothly from source to market. You’ll be responsible for purchase order management, replenishment execution, and logistics coordination across domestic and export markets, working closely with internal stakeholders, third-party manufacturers and logistics partners.



This is a hands-on, detail-driven role suited to someone who enjoys processing, accuracy and keeping things moving — while still being connected to the bigger supply chain picture.



More specifically you will be involved in:


- Creating, issuing and maintaining purchase orders in the ERP system to support production and replenishment plans
- Monitoring PO status , following up with suppliers and internal teams to ensure on-time delivery and accurate fulfilment
- Executing domestic and export replenishment activities , ensuring stock is delivered in full and on time to global distribution centres (NZ, Australia, China)
- Processing goods receipting , resolving delivery or invoice discrepancies, and maintaining data integrity
- Preparing and checking export and trade documentation , ensuring compliance with origin and destination requirements
- Performing inventory reconciliations for assigned warehouses and reporting outcomes in line with agreed timelines
- Providing clear, proactive updates on replenishment progress, risks and exceptions to key stakeholders
- Supporting continuous improvement initiatives across supply chain processes and ways of working



What you will bring:




- Experience in supply chain, logistics, procurement or replenishment coordination roles
- Strong processing discipline, attention to detail and confidence working with high volumes of transactional data
- Experience using ERP systems (Oracle or similar) and strong Excel skills
- Understanding of purchase order management, goods receipting and inventory reconciliation processes
- Exposure to export logistics and international trade documentation (advantageous but not essential)
- A collaborative working style and the ability to build effective relationships across teams and with external partners


We are committed to enhancing our team members' work-life wellbeing with leading policies and practices on flexible work, parental leave, family care, and family wellbeing. This role can be worked flexibly, ensuring a healthy balance between work and personal life. Our family-friendly culture is designed to support workforce participation, career development, and mental health and wellbeing.





Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Position Description










Our Story:

The a2 Milk Company, driven by science and a pioneering spirit, disrupted the dairy and infant milk formula industry. Our vision is to create an A1-free world where dairy nourishes all people and our planet.

The a2 Milk Company seeks to achieve this through the naturally occurring a2 Milk® difference and by bringing a more sustainable approach to dairy.



About the role:

Reporting to the Warehouse Manager and based at our Pokeno site, you will play a critical role in maintaining high quality standards across our warehouse operations. You’ll be responsible for ensuring all inbound and outbound goods meet quality, compliance, and regulatory requirements, while supporting production and inventory accuracy in a fast-paced manufacturing environment.



More specifically, you will be involved in:


- Inspecting inbound and outbound goods to ensure they are free from damage and foreign matter (quality control, warehouse quality, GMP)
- Completing and maintaining accurate documentation for all warehouse movements, including receipting, dispatch paperwork, and seal control (warehouse administration, compliance)
- Creating, checking, and confirming picks against production orders and shipping documentation (order accuracy, production support)
- Updating scheduling systems with booking statuses and movement information (ERP systems, warehouse systems)
- Conducting quality checks, label verification, inventory enquiries, and registering improvements or investigations in Promapp (continuous improvement, inventory accuracy)
- Maintaining exceptional cleanliness and housekeeping standards across the warehouse and facilities (5S, hygiene, food safety)
- Supporting the wider warehouse team with daily tasks, enquiries, data entry, and workload coordination

What you will bring


- At least 1 year’s experience in a warehousing or logistics environment
- Strong attention to detail with a quality-focused mindset
- Good written and spoken English skills
- Strong organisational and time-management skills
- Knowledge of quality systems, compliance, and health & safety practices
- A hands-on approach and physical capability for warehouse tasks
- A willingness to learn and contribute to a high-performance team

Preferred but not essential:


- Experience in a manufacturing or food production environment
- Forklift licence
- Experience using ERP systems such as Microsoft Dynamics NAV (or similar)
- Familiarity with health & safety standards

Find out more about our great benefits on our Careers Page .

jobDescriptionText:
Principal - Software Engineer


.































General information









Career area



Technology




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




City



Charlotte, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



20983




Posted Date



10-23-25




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










Lead the design, development, and scaling of a self-hosted AI Agent Platform on AWS. Drive platform architecture, AI agent orchestration, security, and enterprise adoption. Blend hands-on engineering with strategic technical leadership. At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.












The Work Itself











- Architect and evolve a self-hosted, AWS-based AI Agent Platform using ECS/Fargate, Lambda, API Gateway, ALB, S3, KMS, CloudWatch.
- Design scalable, modular infrastructure supporting multiple agent frameworks (LangGraph, OpenAI Agent SDK, Autogen).
- Define patterns for agent deployment, orchestration, and multi-tenant scaling in containerized environments.
- Build intelligent multi-agent workflows with complex reasoning and tool orchestration.
- Develop reusable SDKs, templates, and developer tooling for faster adoption.
- Integrate agents with enterprise systems such as ServiceNow, Salesforce, Snowflake, Confluence, Okta, GRC platforms.
- Implement authentication and authorization using Okta OIDC, JWT, and fine-grained tool policies.
- Establish observability standards including logging, metrics, tracing, and governance dashboards.
- Ensure compliance with enterprise AI governance and risk management frameworks.
- Mentor senior engineers and act as technical thought leader across teams.
- Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to drive enterprise-wide platform adoption.












The Skills You Bring











- Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, AI/ML, Data Science, or related experience preferred
- 5+ years of software engineering experience required
- 3+ years in senior or principal roles preferred
- Proven experience architecting and scaling distributed systems and AI platforms on AWS.
- Strong Python skills and familiarity with async frameworks like FastAPI and asyncio.

jobDescriptionText:
Principal - Security Architect - Artificial Intelligence (AI/LLM)


.































General information









Career area



Technology




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21117




Posted Date



11-14-25




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










As the Principal - Security Architect - Artificial Intelligence (AI/LLM), you will lead the design, implementation, and governance of security architectures for AI-driven systems and platforms. You will ensure that AI solutions are secure by design, compliant with regulatory requirements, and resilient against evolving threats. Your expertise will guide cross-functional teams in adopting best practices, developing secure patterns, and shaping the technology strategy and roadmap for AI security across the organization. This is a mid-level to senior-level highly technical role that will serve as a mentor to more junior architects and will report to Head of Security Architecture.

At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself











- Lead comprehensive security reviews of AI systems, applications, and infrastructure to identify and mitigate risks.
- Conduct threat modeling for AI and machine learning solutions, ensuring robust protection against adversarial attacks and data breaches.
- Evaluate vendors and emerging technologies for security posture, integration capabilities, and alignment with organizational standards.
- Develop and maintain technology strategy and security roadmaps for AI initiatives, ensuring alignment with business objectives and regulatory requirements.
- Establish and promote secure architecture patterns, frameworks, and guidelines for AI development and deployment.
- Collaborate with engineering, data science, product, and compliance teams to embed security throughout the AI lifecycle.
- Provide expert guidance on network security, data security, cloud security, and identity & access management (IAM) in the context of AI systems.
- Present complex security concepts and recommendations to technical and non-technical stakeholders, including executive leadership.
- Build and nurture relationships with internal and external partners to advance the organization’s AI security posture.
- Stay abreast of emerging threats, technologies, and regulatory changes impacting AI security.












The Skills You Bring











- 5+ years of experience in security architecture, with significant exposure to AI/ML systems and cloud environments.
- Experience with Model Context Protocol (MCP) and securing the infrastructure that supports it.
- Ability to mentor and coach a technical team, leading by example.
- Deep knowledge of network security, data security, cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP), and IAM principles.
- Proven experience conducting security reviews, threat modeling, and vendor/technology evaluations.
- Demonstrated ability to develop technology strategies, roadmaps, and secure architecture patterns based on evolving security threats and industry trends.
- Strong understanding of financial industry regulatory frameworks (such as is FFIEC, PCI DSS, and SOX) and cybersecurity frameworks (such as NIST and CIS)
- Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey complex concepts to diverse audiences.
- Exceptional relationship-building and collaboration skills across technical and business teams.
- Relevant certifications (e.g., CISSP, CCSP, AWS/Azure Security, TOGAF) are highly desirable.
- Bachelor’s or equivalent experience in Computer Science, Information Security, or related field.
#LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

.

Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










This role is Detroit Hybrid preferred; however, remote option possible.

The Senior Actuarial Analyst is responsible for developing and supporting a robust reserving framework at Ally Insurance. The Senior Actuarial Analyst will be expected to independently lead projects from start to finish. This includes brainstorming, accurately executing advanced analytics, and effectively communicating results to Management and Senior Leadership. This role will have high visibility to Leadership and future advancement opportunities. Ideal candidates will have demonstrated leadership, communication, and technical skills. Programming skills a must . SQL/R/Python preferred.













The Work Itself











- Support a reserving framework for insurance business, including both standard actuarial techniques and advanced modeling
- Lead, develop, and communicate advanced analytics to Management and Senior Leadership
- Foster innovation within the team and across the broader organization
- Work collaboratively as a part of the product development, pricing, and/or reserving processes
- Develop reserve indications, produce reserve ranges, and perform advanced analytics
- Support development of themselves and other associates in an actuarial capacity and according to Ally vision and mission statement












The Skills You Bring











- ACAS or near ACAS preferred; 5 or more Casualty Actuarial Society exams required
- 4+ years of experience in actuarial or data science roles; exposure to advanced ratemaking and/or reserving a plus
- Strong communication and relationship building skills
- Programming skills in SQL & R
- Ability to adapt to change and adjust priorities quickly, as necessary and appropriate
- High level of problem solving and critical thinking capabilities
- Well-organized and detail-oriented
- Highly motivated and self-directed
- Minimum of Bachelor’s degree in risk management, finance, economics, actuarial science, mathematics, statistics, or related field
- Must be authorized to work in the US on a permanent basis #LI-Remote #LI-DFS













How We'll Have Your Back

Ally and Your Career

Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

This role is Detroit Hybrid preferred; however, remote option possible.


The Senior Actuarial Analyst is responsible for developing and supporting a robust reserving framework at Ally Insurance. The Senior Actuarial Analyst will be expected to independently lead projects from start to finish. This includes brainstorming, accurately executing advanced analytics, and effectively communicating results to Management and Senior Leadership.  This role will have high visibility to Leadership and future advancement opportunities. Ideal candidates will have demonstrated leadership, communication, and technical skills. Programming skills a must. SQL/R/Python preferred.

The Work Itself

  • Support a reserving framework for insurance business, including both standard actuarial techniques and advanced modeling
  • Lead, develop, and communicate advanced analytics to Management and Senior Leadership
  • Foster innovation within the team and across the broader organization
  • Work collaboratively as a part of the product development, pricing, and/or reserving processes
  • Develop reserve indications, produce reserve ranges, and perform advanced analytics
  • Support development of themselves and other associates in an actuarial capacity and according to Ally vision and mission statement

The Skills You Bring

  • ACAS or near ACAS preferred; 5 or more Casualty Actuarial Society exams required
  • 4+ years of experience in actuarial or data science roles; exposure to advanced ratemaking and/or reserving a plus
  • Strong communication and relationship building skills
  • Programming skills in SQL & R
  • Ability to adapt to change and adjust priorities quickly, as necessary and appropriate
  • High level of problem solving and critical thinking capabilities
  • Well-organized and detail-oriented
  • Highly motivated and self-directed
  • Minimum of Bachelor’s degree in risk management, finance, economics, actuarial science, mathematics, statistics, or related field
  • Must be authorized to work in the US on a permanent basis
#LI-Remote
#LI-DFS

  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.

The Opportunity











- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the Greater Pittsburgh/Erie, PA area requiring local travel throughout the surrounding Grove City, PA to Jamestown, NY sales market. Approximately 2-4 overnights expected monthly. Company vehicle provided.
- An Account Executive for Ally Auto Finance acts as a field sales representative, promoting the full breadth of Ally’s auto finance products and services to dealerships and equipping them with the tools necessary to grow their business and support their customers. They identify, pursue, and maximize business opportunities within their local market and create solid working relationships with existing and new dealers.












The Work Itself











- Manage the full sales lifecycle for Ally’s auto finance product offerings and act as a singular point-of-contact for existing and new dealerships in a designated market.
- Demonstrate a thorough knowledge of all Ally’s auto finance products, plans, and programs such as floorplan financing, Dealer Products & Services, and SmartAuction.
- Market and promote the full breadth of Ally's products and services including commercial banking products.
- Communicate and liaise with designated Consumer Underwriters and peer Account Executives across Ally Auto and Insurance.
- Coordinate with key product specialists on the team to provide assistance, training, and consultative services to all assigned dealers.
- Manage risk as a Dealership Performance Consultant.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 3+ years of experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- 3+ years in a dealer-interfacing role within the auto finance space.
- Ability to leverage industry expertise and relationships to contribute to business strategy and drive profitability.
- Full sales lifecycle experience with a strong foundation in business management, analytics, presentation, and relationship selling skills.
- Active problem solver with an aptitude for managing conflicting priorities. #LI-Remote #DFS

jobDescriptionText:

.

KY, TN




Remote?



Yes

Full time

The Opportunity











- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the South-Central Kentucky or Central Tennessee area, preferably the Greater Nashville, TN or Bowling Green, KY area, requiring local travel throughout the surrounding Greater Nashville & Louisville sales market. Company vehicle provided.
- Successful account executives love to play. Creativity drives them – they throw new ideas against the wall and watch them stick. In this role, you’ll expand product offerings in existing business through cross-selling and increase market share with new clients. You’ll balance your compassion and empathy for everyone involved in the sales process. We see our account executives as problem solvers who are resilient, can face adversity, aren’t afraid of rejection and emerge as champions when times are tough. If you want flexibility, training to improve your skills and a management team that champions you and backs you up, Ally is the place to be.












The Work Itself











- You’ll sell finance & insurance (F&I) product lines to meet targeted goals by explaining corporate programs and service capabilities, while effectively overcoming objections.
- You’ll service current customers by maintaining rapport, making regular visits (there’s a lot of travel involved with this role), evaluating customer's needs for new opportunities, and replying to requests.
- You’ll also identify non-F&I opportunities and coordinate with the Insurance Specialist (identify new customers, estimate growth volume and qualify prospects).
- To ensure customers are fully knowledgeable with Ally’s products, you’ll assist in training F&I Managers within our dealerships.
- To sustain your success, you’ll maintain current knowledge about our competitors, market threats and challenges, maintain professional and technical knowledge by participating in training, and remain involved in the industry, respective area and trade associations.












The Skills You Bring











- 3+ years of experience required.
- The ability to develop relationships with dealer principals and decision makers.
- Retail dealership experience including finance or sales management is highly preferred.
- You have well-developed business management, presentation and relationship selling skills.
- Holding yourself accountable is important to you. Being effective at prioritizing tasks is second nature.
- You’ve branded yourself as a problem solver and you objectively manage conflicting priorities.
- High school diploma or GED equivalent required. Bachelors degree preferred. #LI-Remote #DFS












How We'll Have Your Back

- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

  • Holding yourself accountable is important to you. Being effective at prioritizing tasks is second nature. 
  • You’ve branded yourself as a problem solver and you objectively manage conflicting priorities.
  • High school diploma or GED equivalent required. Bachelors degree preferred. 
#LI-Remote
#DFS

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals.   • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.

jobDescriptionText:
Accountant II - Corporate Accounting and Shared Services


.































General information









Career area



Accounting




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI




Remote?



No




Ref #



21769




Posted Date



02-20-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










The Accountant II position is within the Controller’s Group reporting to a Manager within the Corporate Accounting and Shared Services Team (CASST). The candidate would be primarily responsible for supporting the work needed to reconcile the enterprise’s cash, cash clearing, and suspense accounts. This person would also work closely with Ally’s Enterprise Functions and Consolidations Accounting teams (also divisions of Corporate Accounting) on journal entries, internal reporting, and reconciliation activities. Finally, this person will support any other ad-hoc or project work within the Corporate Accounting Team, including ongoing automation efforts.












The Work Itself











- Prepare certain cash, cash clearing, and suspense journal entries and account reconciliations (monthly and daily) including complex accounts which require heavy collaboration with business partners across the organization to identify, research, and correct discrepancies.
- Prepare journal entries, internal reporting, and reconciliations associated with enterprise functions and consolidation-adjacent processes.
- Support the preparation of the quarterly and year-end financial statements (10-Q/10-K) and regulatory reports.
- Identify and implement improvements and automation opportunities in areas of responsibility.
- Coordinate with several groups across the enterprise in gathering all the necessary materials.
- Assist in analytical accounting support to the internal business partners.
- Assists in the resolution of accounting problems through the appropriate channels.
- Manage and improve the processes around the assigned accounting activities.
- Drive audit compliance within the areas for which you are responsible.
- Contribute to ad hoc analytical projects as necessary both within and beyond your primary area of focus.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 1+ years of relevant experience
- Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent
Preferred Qualifications
- Highly preferred: Bachelor's degree in Accounting major or equivalent.
- Highly preferred: 1+ years previous work experience in Accounting including experience preparing cash and other account reconciliations and with preparing and posting journal entries.
- Highly preferred: Advanced MS Excel skills (Power Query, pivot tables, x-lookups, etc.).
- Workday (including OfficeConnect) experience.
- CPA (or equivalent) is preferred (or working toward CPA or equivalent license).
- Possess general technical accounting skills.
- Exhibit a strong work ethic and high degree of self-motivation.
- Must possess good oral and written communication skills.
- Ability to organize data, perform independent analysis, and provide explanations/conclusions to management.
- Ability to work with limited supervision while applying previous experience to execute moderately complex tasks.
- Ability to contribute meaningfully to projects.
- Comfortable interacting with other personnel in groups or from other departments; may interact with other internal and external personnel on specific projects or routine matters, usually under supervision. #LI-Hybrid













How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.

jobDescriptionText:
Analyst II - BL Risk Testing and Monitoring - Deposits


.































General information









Career area

1100 Virginia Drive, PA




Remote?



No

21665

Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










The Testing and Monitoring Analyst II operates independently and plays a critical role in evaluating the design and operating effectiveness of key processes and controls within the Deposits Risk Control Self Assessment (RCSA). This role is responsible for executing risk based testing and monitoring activities, identifying operational risks and control deficiencies, and partnering with business stakeholders to drive sustainable process improvements. Additional responsibilities may include special projects like process reviews, Issue Validations, and continuous monitoring. The ideal candidate executes efficient and effective tests relative to risk, ensuring accordance with Testing and Monitoring methodology and other applicable policies and standards. This role will be hands on requiring someone who is comfortable working in a fast-paced environment across multiple cross-functional areas.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position. In office role with hybrid schedule. Ally work location is Fort Washington PA













The Work Itself











- Independently conduct risk based testing and ongoing monitoring of Deposits processes to assess control design and operating effectiveness.
- Develop and execute appropriate testing strategies, including defining scope, selecting samples, and determining testing methods.
- Lead walkthroughs with Deposits teams to gain a better and comprehensive understanding of processes, risks, and controls.
- Clearly document testing results and findings in complete and accurate work papers that meet testing and monitoring standards.
- Identify, communicate, and escalate control deficiencies, emerging risks, and trends in a timely and professional manner.
- Build and maintain strong working relationships with Deposits leadership, business partners, and cross functional teams.
- Collaborate with managers and Deposits leaders to address control issues.
- Perform special projects and ad hoc reviews outside of standard RCSA testing, including but not limited to targeted process reviews, Issue Validation and closure testing, continuous monitoring initiatives, preparation for internal or external audits and regulatory exams.
- Assist management with duties as necessary.













The Skills You Bring











- 1+ years of experience in banking operations, risk, compliance, audit, or quality required
- Understanding of the Financial Services industry, with an emphasis on Risk Management; as well as related laws, rules, and regulations.
- Project management experience preferred
- Prior experience in an Analyst role preferred
- Ability to adapt to change quickly.
- High level of problem-solving capability and research-oriented skills.
- Well-organized and thorough work ethic
- Strong Communication skills both written and verbal.
- Strong skills in MS Excel and PowerPoint













How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.
