jobTitle: Lead Auditor - Accounting and Financial Reporting
jobDescriptionText:
Lead Auditor - Accounting and Financial Reporting


.































General information









Career area



Audit




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI




City



Detroit, MI




Remote?



No




Ref #



21606




Posted Date



01-30-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










Hardworking auditors are constant learners. Curiosity drives them – it’s why they began their audit journey in the first place. Using a growth mindset is what fuels their fascination by understanding the financial services industry and its regulations and how it applies to each business unit at Ally. We get that. In this role, you’ll work within a collaborative agile environment and use your critical thinking skills for completing assigned audit activities. Also, An individual would need to be flexible and adaptable to constantly evolving demands and bring to the role a results-oriented approach and a strong ability to learn.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.












The Work Itself











- You will supervise team members assigned to audit projects; typically leading one audit at a time and including work paper review. Area of audit focus will primarily cover the controller's organization (accounting and financial reporting) and external audit assistance.
- You will proactively lead work assignments to promote execution within established budgets and achievements.
- You will own the development, execution, and documentation of audit tests and issue validations in accordance with Audit Services policies and procedures.
- You will facilitate the vetting of potential audit findings, identifying root causes, and articulating their relevance.
- You will coordinate timely execution of issues validations in accordance with Audit Services policies and procedures.
- You will partner with Ally management cross-functionally to enhance the audit experience and promote visibility and partnership.
- Your meaningful, constructive feedback on work performed will assist in the training and/or mentoring of less experienced staff members.
- Your use of data literacy and analytics will provide consultation to management regarding emerging control practices.
- Your proactive communication skills with management and peers will drive alignment to mitigate customer service issues before they occur.
- Your technical knowledge, creativity and improved mentality will be crucial when performing projects as assigned to assist with analyzing Ally’s risk and control environment.
- Your participation in Audit Transformation initiatives will be essential to the continued improvement to the enterprise and its continuing role within Ally business.












The Skills You Bring











- Relevant 4-year degree or comparable combination of education and experience (including military service). Strong preference to candidates with a bachelor’s degree.
- Core certification (CIA, CPA, CISA, CAMS, CFE, etc.), desired.
- Experience using ACL and Teammate or other Computer Assisted Audit Techniques (CAATs), again a plus.
- 4+ years of work experience in audit or financial services.
- Experience auditing accounting and financial reporting is strongly preferred.
- Strong experience in leading others and performing reviews over control, substantive, and validation testing.
- Strong and effective project management and problem-solving skills and abilities.
- Good writing and verbal communications.
- Demonstrated ability to collaborate and work cooperatively to build trust and promote consensus with others.
- A desire to creatively identify problems that potentially were not known to exist and provide solutions and/or recommendations.
- Lead in a way that promotes a positive image of Audit Services and builds rapport with clients and risk partners.
- Strong understanding of risk mitigation to ensure achievement of industry/regulatory standards, identify root causes, and conduct project risk assessments.
- Eye for business and understanding of the industry, company, and products to promote achievement of objectives and goals.
- Demonstrate curiosity, passion, and strong tech/analytical skills: think before you leap, actively listen, stay open to feedback, and pursue continuous learning with self-awareness of strengths and weaknesses.
- Proactively challenge the status quo while staying adaptable to change and ambiguity, remaining resilient, composed, and focused under pressure. #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

jobDescriptionHtml:
Lead Auditor - Accounting and Financial Reporting .

General information

Career area
Audit
Work Location(s)
500 Woodward Avenue, MI
City
Detroit, MI
Remote?
No
Ref #
21606
Posted Date
01-30-26
Working time
Full time

Ally and Your Career

Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

Hardworking auditors are constant learners. Curiosity drives them – it’s why they began their audit journey in the first place. Using a growth mindset is what fuels their fascination by understanding the financial services industry and its regulations and how it applies to each business unit at Ally. We get that. In this role, you’ll work within a collaborative agile environment and use your critical thinking skills for completing assigned audit activities. Also, An individual would need to be flexible and adaptable to constantly evolving demands and bring to the role a results-oriented approach and a strong ability to learn.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

The Work Itself

  • You will supervise team members assigned to audit projects; typically leading one audit at a time and including work paper review.  Area of audit focus will primarily cover the controller's organization (accounting and financial reporting) and external audit assistance.
  • You will proactively lead work assignments to promote execution within established budgets and achievements.
  • You will own the development, execution, and documentation of audit tests and issue validations in accordance with Audit Services policies and procedures.
  • You will facilitate the vetting of potential audit findings, identifying root causes, and articulating their relevance.
  • You will coordinate timely execution of issues validations in accordance with Audit Services policies and procedures.
  • You will partner with Ally management cross-functionally to enhance the audit experience and promote visibility and partnership.
  • Your meaningful, constructive feedback on work performed will assist in the training and/or mentoring of less experienced staff members.
  • Your use of data literacy and analytics will provide consultation to management regarding emerging control practices.
  • Your proactive communication skills with management and peers will drive alignment to mitigate customer service issues before they occur.
  • Your technical knowledge, creativity and improved mentality will be crucial when performing projects as assigned to assist with analyzing Ally’s risk and control environment.
  • Your participation in Audit Transformation initiatives will be essential to the continued improvement to the enterprise and its continuing role within Ally business.

The Skills You Bring

  • Relevant 4-year degree or comparable combination of education and experience (including military service). Strong preference to candidates with a bachelor’s degree.
  • Core certification (CIA, CPA, CISA, CAMS, CFE, etc.), desired.
  • Experience using ACL and Teammate or other Computer Assisted Audit Techniques (CAATs), again a plus.
  • 4+ years of work experience in audit or financial services.
  • Experience auditing accounting and financial reporting is strongly preferred.
  • Strong experience in leading others and performing reviews over control, substantive, and validation testing. 
  • Strong and effective project management and problem-solving skills and abilities.
  • Good writing and verbal communications.
  • Demonstrated ability to collaborate and work cooperatively to build trust and promote consensus with others.
  • A desire to creatively identify problems that potentially were not known to exist and provide solutions and/or recommendations.
  • Lead in a way that promotes a positive image of Audit Services and builds rapport with clients and risk partners.
  • Strong understanding of risk mitigation to ensure achievement of industry/regulatory standards, identify root causes, and conduct project risk assessments. 
  • Eye for business and understanding of the industry, company, and products to promote achievement of objectives and goals.
  • Demonstrate curiosity, passion, and strong tech/analytical skills: think before you leap, actively listen, stay open to feedback, and pursue continuous learning with self-awareness of strengths and weaknesses.
  • Proactively challenge the status quo while staying adaptable to change and ambiguity, remaining resilient, composed, and focused under pressure.
#LI-Hybrid

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.

jobDescriptionText:
Director Consumer Bank Strategy & Planning


.































General information









Career area



Deposits Products & Marketing




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21716




Posted Date



02-23-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










In office role with hybrid schedule, Ally work location is Charlotte NC. At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.
The Director – Business Strategy & Planning, Consumer Bank is a trusted advisor and operational leader supporting the President of the Consumer Bank, as well as business support to the Strategic Delivery Leader and the Customer Advocacy Leader. This role drives critical management deliverables, executive communications, cross-functional alignment, and strategic planning. The ideal candidate combines strategic thought leadership, exceptional Board and CEO executive-level storytelling, strong stakeholder management, and is disciplined and execution focused to help shape the business agenda and deliver outcomes that protect the bank, our customers, and the financial system.













The Work Itself










Executive Partnership and Strategic Planning
- Serve as a thought leader on strategy, priorities, and organizational effectiveness.
- Lead annual and quarterly strategic planning, annual strategic objectives and key metric establishment and monitoring; translate goals into measurable execution plans.
- Anticipate risks and opportunities; prepare decision frameworks and recommendations that enable rapid, well-informed executive action. Messaging, Storytelling, and Executive Communications
- Craft compelling narratives for the Board of Directors, Executive Committee, Regulators, and internal leadership presentations such as Consumer Banking monthly business reviews.
- Drive the content development for executive dialogues, town halls, and stakeholder updates, ensuring clarity, cohesion, and alignment to strategic priorities.
- Develop content for speeches, briefing materials, and talking points; oversee executive reporting cadences and scorecards/dashboards. Cross-Functional Collaboration and Stakeholder Management
- Build strong partnerships with other members of the Business Support team, other members of the leadership team, and key partners such as Finance, HR, Technology, Risk, Compliance, Legal, and Operations to synchronize priorities and resources.
- Facilitate governance routines, steering committees, and working groups to maintain alignment and momentum. Business Strategy and Insights
- Shape the Consumer Bank business strategy, its operating model, and roadmap by leading strategic analysis and competitive market assessments routinely.
- Partner closely with the Finance and Analytics teams to pull data upwards—synthesizing metrics, trends, and model outputs into executive-ready scorecards & insights.
- Translate analytics into clear conclusions, recommended actions, and measurable outcomes; track impact against KPIs and regulatory expectations.
- Execute on standardized KPI reporting Execution and Operational Discipline
- Oversee executive routines: business reviews, performance meetings, strategy updates, and board/regulatory prep.
- Manage special projects and strategic workstreams on an as-needed basis; remove obstacles and escalate issues proactively. Regulatory and Risk Awareness
- Support preparation for regulatory exams, independent testing, internal audit, and board risk committee updates.
- Ensure messaging and materials accurately reflect risk posture, remediation progress, model validation, and control effectiveness.
- Maintain awareness of emerging risks, regulatory changes, and industry best practices; integrate learnings into strategy and communications.















The Skills You Bring











Minimum Qualifications:
- 9+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High School Diploma or GED equivalent
Preferred:
- Bachelor’s degree; advanced degree (MBA, JD, MS in Data/Analytics, or related field) highly preferred.
- 9+ years in financial services industry is preferred.
- At least 7 years leadership experience preferred.
- Proven track record as a strategy lead, management consultant, program director, or similar role supporting senior executives or executive clientele.
- Exceptional strategic thinking, synthesis, and storytelling skills—able to convert complex topics and data into a clear, compelling narrative.
- Critical thinking and experience in situations requiring sound business judgment.
- Significant financial services experience in consumer banking.
- Creativity stemming from fact-based research.
- Ability to bring vision / strategies to life.
- Strong presentation skills, including ability to articulate complex problems in concise simple manner.
- Comfortable speaking with and presenting to senior leadership.
- Flexible and adaptive to changing situations.
- Excellent written and verbal skills.
- High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office products.
#LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Director - Responsible AI


.































General information









Career area



Technology




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21865




Posted Date



03-06-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










As a leader in digital financial services, our customer and employee experiences are powered by data and AI. Our cloud-based data, analytics, and GenAI platform enables thousands of employees to make data-driven decisions every day. We are seeking a Director, Responsible AI to operationalize and advance AI governance at scale across machine learning, generative AI, and agent-based systems. This leader drives the implementation of RAI standards and oversees governance enablement mechanisms that embed Responsible AI into the AI development lifecycle—translating policy and risk requirements into practical, reusable engineering patterns, tooling, and metrics that accelerate safe adoption. This role also leads direct governance support for Ally’s key AI initiatives.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.












The Work Itself











- Advance responsible AI practices by integrating AI governance best practices into every stage of model development and deployment.
- Collaborate closely with risk and other partners to evolve enterprise-wide AI governance standards and processes.
- Drive AI adoption at scale and on schedule across the enterprise, within the governance framework.
- Deliver governance support for strategic AI projects and initiatives.
- Drive end-to-end operational excellence in AI governance in predictive models, GenAI and agentic systems, from model development, evaluation, deployment to operational performance monitoring.













The Skills You Bring










Minimum qualifications
- 9+ years of relevant experience.
- Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent.
Preferred qualifications:
- Highly preferred: Demonstrated expertise in model risk areas and mitigations (e.g., bias/fairness, explainability, PII handling, monitoring) and in supporting full model lifecycle.
- Highly preferred: Exceptional ability to translate Risk Management and Model Risk expectations into practical development standards for AI systems in banking or regulated environments.
- Highly preferred: Strong communication skills and executive influence. Drive alignment across diverse stakeholders, create clarity and build consensus.
- 7+ years of experience in applied machine learning / data science delivering measurable business impact in real-world systems.
- 3+ years of experience in risk management, regulatory compliance or similar functions.
- Master's degree.
- Third-party/vendor AI governance experience.
- Governance experience in state-of-the-art ML/GenAI topics such as agents, RAG, LLM observability, etc.
- People management experience. #LI-Hybrid

General information

Ally and Your Career

Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

As a leader in digital financial services, our customer and employee experiences are powered by data and AI. Our cloud-based data, analytics, and GenAI platform enables thousands of employees to make data-driven decisions every day. We are seeking a Director, Responsible AI to operationalize and advance AI governance at scale across machine learning, generative AI, and agent-based systems. This leader drives the implementation of RAI standards and oversees governance enablement mechanisms that embed Responsible AI into the AI development lifecycle—translating policy and risk requirements into practical, reusable engineering patterns, tooling, and metrics that accelerate safe adoption. This role also leads direct governance support for Ally’s key AI initiatives. 

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position. 

The Work Itself

  • Advance responsible AI practices by integrating AI governance best practices into every stage of model development and deployment. 
  • Collaborate closely with risk and other partners to evolve enterprise-wide AI governance standards and processes. 
  • Drive AI adoption at scale and on schedule across the enterprise, within the governance framework. 
  • Deliver governance support for strategic AI projects and initiatives. 
  • Drive end-to-end operational excellence in AI governance in predictive models, GenAI and agentic systems, from model development, evaluation, deployment to operational performance monitoring. 

The Skills You Bring

Minimum qualifications
  • 9+ years of relevant experience.
  • Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent.

Preferred qualifications: 
  • Highly preferred: Demonstrated expertise in model risk areas and mitigations (e.g., bias/fairness, explainability, PII handling, monitoring) and in supporting full model lifecycle. 
  • Highly preferred: Exceptional ability to translate Risk Management and Model Risk expectations into practical development standards for AI systems in banking or regulated environments. 
  • Highly preferred: Strong communication skills and executive influence. Drive alignment across diverse stakeholders, create clarity and build consensus. 
  • 7+ years of experience in applied machine learning / data science delivering measurable business impact in real-world systems. 
  • 3+ years of experience in risk management, regulatory compliance or similar functions. 
  • Master's degree.
  • Third-party/vendor AI governance experience. 
  • Governance experience in state-of-the-art ML/GenAI topics such as agents, RAG, LLM observability, etc. 
  • People management experience.
#LI-Hybrid

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

We are seeking a Director to lead Software Engineering in support of our Supply Chain organization, providing strategic and people leadership across multiple development teams with full accountability for execution, quality, and outcomes. This position reports to a Senior Technology Director and includes direct leadership of multiple resources, along with responsibility for delivery, resource planning, and budgets.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself











- Define and execute the vision, strategy, and roadmap for Supply Chain technology platforms aligned to business objectives and success metrics.
- Build, lead, and mentor a team of engineers and platform specialists, establishing strong engineering standards, delivery discipline, and a culture of ownership. Lead architecture and design for supply chain systems in a secure, cloud first environment.
- Drive digital, AI, and automation initiatives, including Generative AI, analytics, RPA, and low code solutions.
- Act as a trusted strategic advisor and right-hand partner to Product, Data, and Lines of Business, driving innovative solutions through Generative AI, advanced analytics, and automation.
- Partner closely with Supply Chain Technology Product Owners to operationalize strategy, scale delivery, and ensure day-to-day technology execution aligns with long-term transformation objectives
- Oversee delivery of complex programs, ensuring outcomes are achieved on time and within budget.
- Build, lead, and develop high performing technology teams.
- Continuously optimize the supply chain technology stack in partnership with Product and business teams.
- Communicate clearly and effectively with senior management, internal stakeholders, and external partners.
- Continuously evolve the transformation strategy to ensure the organization remains competitive, compliant, and future ready.













The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications:
- 9+ years of relevant experience
- Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- At least 7+years of experience in Supply Chain technology transformation.
- Proven leadership experience designing and managing Supply Chain technology architecture for large, complex, and regulated enterprises (preferably financial services).
- Implementation experience on Coupa/Source-to-Pay modules, Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM); experience with Workday HCM/ERP integration strongly preferred.
- Strong functional knowledge across Accounts Payable/P2P, vendor risk management, integrations, and related Coupa modules.
- Ability to build and develop API integrations
- Strong problem-solving skills with a proactive, outcome driven mindset.
- Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree preferred. #LI-Hybrid

.































General information









Career area



Technology




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC

No

Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech

We are seeking a Director, Head of Innovation Labs to establish and lead Ally’s next-generation innovation engine. This role will define and evolve a portfolio of innovation labs focused on rapid experimentation, emerging technologies, and new business models that drive material impact across the enterprise.

Operating at the intersection of strategy, technology, and venture-style execution, this role emphasizes disciplined experimentation, scalable incubation, and strong governance. You will set the vision and operating model for how ideas move from concept to pilot to scaled product, while partnering closely with business leaders, product, engineering, design, data, security, compliance, and legal teams to ensure innovations are bold, responsible, and commercially viable.

As a senior technical and innovation leader, you will identify external trends, build strategic partnerships, and cultivate internal talent to continuously expand Ally’s innovation capacity. You will balance speed with rigor, creativity with accountability, and exploration with measurable business outcomes.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself











- Own and evolve the technical vision and architecture for Innovation Labs, establishing a scalable ecosystem for rapid experimentation, prototyping, and incubation across emerging technologies.
- Continuously evaluate and pilot emerging technologies and architectural patterns, including generative AI, autonomous agents, machine learning platforms, advanced analytics, cloud-native, and automation, and drive strategic adoption where they create material business value.
- Define technical standards, patterns, and reference architectures for building AI-enabled MVPs, proofs of concept, and pilots that can transition efficiently into enterprise-grade products.
- Design and oversee shared innovation tooling, sandbox environments, and cloud-based experimentation platforms for model development, agent orchestration, data access, and rapid application delivery.
- Establish lab-level approaches for data architecture, model lifecycle management (MLOps/LLMOps), integration, security, identity, and observability to support fast, safe experimentation.
- Ensure innovation solutions meet enterprise requirements for security, privacy, reliability, accessibility, responsible AI, and regulatory compliance.
- Partner with product, engineering, data, and infrastructure teams to modernize core platforms and introduce AI-first and automation-first capabilities.
- Drive technical due diligence for innovation investments, build-vs-buy decisions, and external partnerships related to AI platforms, tooling, and emerging ecosystems.
- Identify systemic technical risks and scalability constraints (e.g., data quality, model drift, cost, latency) and lead cross-team architecture initiatives to address them.
- Influence and guide technical decision-making across the organization by leading architecture reviews, setting engineering best practices, and mentoring senior technologists in modern AI-enabled system design.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 9+ years of relevant experience
- Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- 9+ years of distinguished experience in software engineering, advanced systems architecture, or applied research, with a track record of shaping technical strategy at enterprise or industry scale.
- 5+ years of experience mentoring principal- and staff-level engineers and cultivating next-generation technical leaders.
- Recognized technical authority in emerging and advanced technologies (e.g., generative AI, autonomous agents, advanced analytics, distributed systems, cloud-native platforms), with evidence of original contributions or thought leadership.
- Demonstrated ability to reason about highly abstract problem spaces and translate novel concepts into durable architectural patterns and scalable platforms.
- Proven experience leading and influencing highly technical, senior-level engineers, architects, and researchers in exploratory and innovation-driven environments.
- Deep expertise designing large-scale, resilient, and evolvable architectures, including data platforms, AI/ML lifecycle systems, and automation frameworks.
- Track record of establishing technical standards, reference architectures, and engineering excellence practices adopted broadly across organizations.
- Exceptional systems thinking, with the ability to connect long-term technology horizons to near-term product and platform investments.
- Experience operating in regulated, security-conscious environments while enabling responsible adoption of frontier technologies.
- Ability to influence executive leadership, set technical agenda, and drive alignment without direct authority. #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. - Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech

We are seeking a Director, Head of Innovation Labs to establish and lead Ally’s next-generation innovation engine. This role will define and evolve a portfolio of innovation labs focused on rapid experimentation, emerging technologies, and new business models that drive material impact across the enterprise.

Operating at the intersection of strategy, technology, and venture-style execution, this role emphasizes disciplined experimentation, scalable incubation, and strong governance. You will set the vision and operating model for how ideas move from concept to pilot to scaled product, while partnering closely with business leaders, product, engineering, design, data, security, compliance, and legal teams to ensure innovations are bold, responsible, and commercially viable.

As a senior technical and innovation leader, you will identify external trends, build strategic partnerships, and cultivate internal talent to continuously expand Ally’s innovation capacity. You will balance speed with rigor, creativity with accountability, and exploration with measurable business outcomes.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

The Work Itself

  • Own and evolve the technical vision and architecture for Innovation Labs, establishing a scalable ecosystem for rapid experimentation, prototyping, and incubation across emerging technologies.
  • Continuously evaluate and pilot emerging technologies and architectural patterns, including generative AI, autonomous agents, machine learning platforms, advanced analytics, cloud-native, and automation, and drive strategic adoption where they create material business value.
  • Define technical standards, patterns, and reference architectures for building AI-enabled MVPs, proofs of concept, and pilots that can transition efficiently into enterprise-grade products.
  • Design and oversee shared innovation tooling, sandbox environments, and cloud-based experimentation platforms for model development, agent orchestration, data access, and rapid application delivery.
  • Establish lab-level approaches for data architecture, model lifecycle management (MLOps/LLMOps), integration, security, identity, and observability to support fast, safe experimentation.
  • Ensure innovation solutions meet enterprise requirements for security, privacy, reliability, accessibility, responsible AI, and regulatory compliance.
  • Partner with product, engineering, data, and infrastructure teams to modernize core platforms and introduce AI-first and automation-first capabilities.
  • Drive technical due diligence for innovation investments, build-vs-buy decisions, and external partnerships related to AI platforms, tooling, and emerging ecosystems.
  • Identify systemic technical risks and scalability constraints (e.g., data quality, model drift, cost, latency) and lead cross-team architecture initiatives to address them.
  • Influence and guide technical decision-making across the organization by leading architecture reviews, setting engineering best practices, and mentoring senior technologists in modern AI-enabled system design.

The Skills You Bring

Minimum Qualifications

  • 9+ years of relevant experience
  • Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent
Preferred Qualifications
  • 9+ years of distinguished experience in software engineering, advanced systems architecture, or applied research, with a track record of shaping technical strategy at enterprise or industry scale.
  • 5+ years of experience mentoring principal- and staff-level engineers and cultivating next-generation technical leaders.
  • Recognized technical authority in emerging and advanced technologies (e.g., generative AI, autonomous agents, advanced analytics, distributed systems, cloud-native platforms), with evidence of original contributions or thought leadership.
  • Demonstrated ability to reason about highly abstract problem spaces and translate novel concepts into durable architectural patterns and scalable platforms.
  • Proven experience leading and influencing highly technical, senior-level engineers, architects, and researchers in exploratory and innovation-driven environments.
  • Deep expertise designing large-scale, resilient, and evolvable architectures, including data platforms, AI/ML lifecycle systems, and automation frameworks.
  • Track record of establishing technical standards, reference architectures, and engineering excellence practices adopted broadly across organizations.
  • Exceptional systems thinking, with the ability to connect long-term technology horizons to near-term product and platform investments.
  • Experience operating in regulated, security-conscious environments while enabling responsible adoption of frontier technologies.
  • Ability to influence executive leadership, set technical agenda, and drive alignment without direct authority.
#LI-Hybrid

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Base Pay Range: $135000 - $235000 USD
An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer. 
Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

The Opportunity











- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the Greater Eau Claire, WI or surrounding area requiring local travel throughout the surrounding Eau Claire, WI, La Crosse, WI, Rochester, MN and Northwest Wisconsin sales market. Company vehicle provided.
- An Account Executive for Ally Auto Finance acts as a field sales representative, promoting the full breadth of Ally’s auto finance products and services to dealerships and equipping them with the tools necessary to grow their business and support their customers. They identify, pursue, and maximize business opportunities within their local market and create solid working relationships with existing and new dealers.












The Work Itself











- Manage the full sales lifecycle for Ally’s auto finance product offerings and act as a singular point-of-contact for existing and new dealerships in a designated market.
- Demonstrate a thorough knowledge of all Ally’s auto finance products, plans, and programs such as floorplan financing, Dealer Products & Services, and SmartAuction.
- Market and promote the full breadth of Ally's products and services including commercial banking products.
- Communicate and liaise with designated Consumer Underwriters and peer Account Executives across Ally Auto and Insurance.
- Coordinate with key product specialists on the team to provide assistance, training, and consultative services to all assigned dealers.
- Manage risk as a Dealership Performance Consultant.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 3+ years of experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- Dealer-interfacing or dealership experience.
- Ability to leverage industry expertise and relationships to contribute to business strategy and drive profitability.
- Full sales lifecycle experience with a strong foundation in business management, analytics, presentation, and relationship selling skills.
- Active problem solver with an aptitude for managing conflicting priorities. #LI-Remote #DFS

jobDescriptionText:
Fellow Software Engineer – UI Platform (Web & Mobile)


.































General information









Career area



Technology

500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21549




Posted Date



01-23-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career

We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech
We are seeking a Fellow Software Engineer to help lead our UI Platforms across web and mobile applications using React and React Native. This role is responsible for defining and evolving the shared foundations that enable product teams to deliver consistent, secure and high-quality user experiences at enterprise scale.
Operating within a regulated environment, this role emphasizes platform reliability, governance, and developer enablement. You will set technical direction for UI frameworks, tooling and patterns while partnering closely with product, design, security, compliance and infrastructure teams to ensure our platform balances speed to market with stability and control.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.












The Work Itself











- Own and evolve the UI platform architecture supporting web and mobile applications.
- Continuously evaluate emerging technologies, industry trends, and best practices through research, conferences. and professional networks, and drive the strategic adoption of innovative solutions within the organization’s technology stack.
- Define standards, patterns and best practices for React and React Native used across product teams.
- Design and maintain shared libraries, design systems, and foundational components.
- Establish platform-level approaches for state management, navigation, authentication and cross-cutting concerns.
- Ensure platform solutions meet enterprise requirements for security, privacy, accessibility and regulatory compliance.
- Enable product teams through documentation, reference implementations and developer tooling.
- Own and evolve the UI Test automation strategy across web and mobile platforms, ensuring it scales with the platform and supports rapid, safe delivery.
- Drive platform modernization efforts, including framework upgrades, technical debt reduction, and performance improvements.
- Partner with DevOps and infrastructure teams to improve CI/CD pipelines, testing frameworks, and release strategies.
- Identify systemic risks and scalability issues and lead cross-team initiatives to address them.
- Influence and guide technical decision-making across multiple teams by leading design and architecture reviews, establishing and maintaining engineering standards and best practices and providing mentorship and technical leadership to ensure high-quality, scalable, and maintainable solutions aligned with organization goals.
- Familiarity with native mobile development concepts (iOS/Android) and their interaction with React Native.












The Skills You Bring












- 7+ years of professional software engineering experience with deep UI specialization.
- Expert-level experience with React, React Native, TypeScript and modern front-end architecture.
- Proven experience designing and owning shared platforms, frameworks, and core libraries.
- Experience operating in large-scale regulated environments with formal governance and compliance requirements.
- Strong understanding of performance, accessibility, and maintainability at scale.
- Ability to influence across teams without direct authority. #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

  • Establish platform-level approaches for state management, navigation, authentication and cross-cutting concerns.
  • Ensure platform solutions meet enterprise requirements for security, privacy, accessibility and regulatory compliance.
  • Enable product teams through documentation, reference implementations and developer tooling.
  • Own and evolve the UI Test automation strategy across web and mobile platforms, ensuring it scales with the platform and supports rapid, safe delivery.
  • Drive platform modernization efforts, including framework upgrades, technical debt reduction, and performance improvements.
  • Partner with DevOps and infrastructure teams to improve CI/CD pipelines, testing frameworks, and release strategies.
  • Identify systemic risks and scalability issues and lead cross-team initiatives to address them.
  • Influence and guide technical decision-making across multiple teams by leading design and architecture reviews, establishing and maintaining engineering standards and best practices and providing mentorship and technical leadership to ensure high-quality, scalable, and maintainable solutions aligned with organization goals.
  • Familiarity with native mobile development concepts (iOS/Android) and their interaction with React Native.

The Skills You Bring


  • 7+ years of professional software engineering experience with deep UI specialization.
  • Expert-level experience with React, React Native, TypeScript and modern front-end architecture.
  • Proven experience designing and owning shared platforms, frameworks, and core libraries.
  • Experience operating in large-scale regulated environments with formal governance and compliance requirements.
  • Strong understanding of performance, accessibility, and maintainability at scale.
  • Ability to influence across teams without direct authority.
#LI-Hybrid

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Base Pay Range: $135000 - $235000 USD
An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer. 
Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech

Join Ally's Identity Shield and Consumer Fraud team to build mission-critical data platforms and APIs at the intersection of data engineering, security, and real-time fraud prevention. You'll develop systems that aggregate and enrich behavioral, transactional, and contextual signals across Ally's digital banking ecosystem, building intelligent, AI-enabled services that power adaptive authorization and fraud detection protecting millions of customers every day.



At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself










As an engineer on this team, you'll tackle fascinating challenges at the convergence of fast data and security - processing massive volumes of streaming events, enriching signals with contextual intelligence, and serving insights with millisecond latency. You'll work with modern data technologies to build resilient, scalable platforms where performance and reliability directly impact our ability to keep customers safe. This is your opportunity to shape Ally’s next‑gen identity and fraud defense stack, AI-assisted development workflows, honing your skills in security-first design principles, distributed systems, streaming architectures, and cloud‑native development. This role offers the rare combination of high-impact work, cutting-edge technology, and meaningful outcomes. You'll collaborate across engineering, data science and security teams to solve complex problems that matter. Whether you're developing intelligent identity management capabilities, designing event schemas and platforms for real-time fraud detection, or building APIs that serve critical signals, your work will be visible and valued.

On this team, we believe in relentless progress balanced with sustainable pace. We support each other, celebrate wins, learn from setbacks, and foster an environment where everyone can do their best work. Our #1 goal is team success, and our people are above all else.

Key Responsibilities:


- Design and build scalable data pipelines that ingest, transform, and enrich signals from diverse sources across Ally's ecosystem
- Develop streaming applications that process high-volume event streams with low latency requirements
- Create robust data models and schemas optimized for both analytical queries and real-time serving
- Write clean, tested Python code following engineering best practices and team standards
- Collaborate with fraud analysts and data scientists to understand requirements and deliver solutions that drive real outcomes
- Participate in architectural decisions, technical design reviews, and code reviews
- Build and maintain infrastructure-as-code for data platform components
- Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize platform performance and reliability
- Contribute to documentation, runbooks, and knowledge sharing across the team
- Experiment with emerging technologies and propose innovations that enhance platform capabilities
- Mentor team members and contribute to a culture of continuous learning and improvement












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications:


- 7+ years of relevant experience
- Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent Preferred Qualifications:


- 5+ years of professional software development experience with strong fundamentals in data structures and algorithms
- Proficiency in Python, NodeJS, TypeScript for building data pipelines, APIs, and backend services
- Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with PostgreSQL including query optimization and data modeling
- Experience working with both relational databases (PostgreSQL) and NoSQL databases (DynamoDB) and Graph DB (Neptune) in production environments
- Experience designing and implementing ETL/ELT workflows for data transformation and enrichment
- Passion for writing maintainable code, automated tests, and clear documentation
- Collaborative mindset with excellent communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences
- Self-motivated learner who stays curious about new technologies and approaches
- BSc/BA in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
- Experience building or working with streaming applications and event-driven architectures
- Familiarity with AWS data services such as Flink, Glue, Kinesis, and ElasticCache
- Understanding of API design and microservices architectures
- Knowledge of RESTful API Design, GraphQL and AWS AppSync for building scalable APIs
- Knowledge of serverless patterns and technologies (Lambda, ECS/Fargate, etc.)
- Knowledge of observability tools and distributed tracing (Dynatrace, OpenTelemetry, CloudWatch, X-Ray)
- Familiarity with caching strategies for authentication state (ElastiCache, Redis)
- Experience designing and deploying systems across multi-region architectures
- Experience with Infrastructure as Code tools like Terraform or similar
- Understanding of distributed systems concepts and data engineering principles
- Background or interest in cybersecurity, fraud detection, or risk management domains
- Exposure to distributed messaging systems or event streaming platforms
- Exposure to AI-assisted development tools and workflows












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.






Base Pay Range : $110000 - $180000 USD An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.
