Identity Shield & Consumer Fraud - Shield (Backend)


.































General information









Career area



Technology




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No

21748




Posted Date



02-20-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech

Join Ally's Identity Shield and Consumer Fraud team to build authentication and identity infrastructure that protects our digital banking customers and enables intelligent fraud prevention. You'll develop systems that implement fine-grained access control, manage authentication and authorization flows across Ally's digital banking ecosystem, and generate the behavioral and contextual signals that power adaptive fraud detection.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself










As a backend engineer on this team, you'll tackle fascinating challenges implementing identity protocols at scale—building OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect flows, developing authorization engines that evaluate complex policies with millisecond latency, and serving authentication decisions that directly impact our ability to keep customers safe and prevent fraud. You'll work with modern cloud technologies to build resilient, scalable platforms where performance and reliability are non-negotiable. This is your opportunity to shape Ally's next-generation identity and authentication infrastructure, mastering security-first design principles, distributed systems, serverless architectures, and AI-assisted development workflows.

This role offers the rare combination of high-impact work, cutting-edge technology, and meaningful outcomes. You'll collaborate across engineering, product, security, and data science teams to solve complex problems that matter. Whether you're developing authentication services that adapt to risk signals, implementing passwordless flows using passkeys and biometrics, building integrations with enterprise identity providers, or designing event streams that carry authentication signals to fraud detection systems, your work will be visible and valued.

On this team, we believe in relentless progress balanced with sustainable pace. We support each other, celebrate wins, learn from setbacks, and foster an environment where everyone can do their best work. Our #1 goal is team success, and our people are above all else.



Key Responsibilities:


- Design and develop authentication and authorization services implementing modern identity protocols (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, SAML, JWT)
- Build scalable APIs for identity management, user registration, verification, and account recovery
- Implement session management and token lifecycle systems across web and mobile platforms
- Develop fine-grained authorization engines and policy evaluation services for access control
- Build integrations with distributed identity providers, enterprise SSO platforms, and social login services
- Implement passwordless authentication flows using passkeys, biometrics, and device attestation
- Develop adaptive authentication logic that adjusts requirements based on risk signals and context
- Instrument authentication events with rich contextual metadata that power fraud detection and analytics
- Implement comprehensive observability using OpenTelemetry across identity services
- Write clean, tested code in Node.js, TypeScript, and Python following engineering best practices and team standards
- Collaborate with frontend engineers, security teams, and data scientists to deliver solutions that drive real outcomes
- Participate in architectural decisions, technical design reviews, and code reviews
- Build and maintain infrastructure-as-code for identity platform components using Terraform
- Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize platform performance, reliability, and security posture
- Contribute to documentation, runbooks, and knowledge sharing across the team
- Experiment with emerging technologies and propose innovations that enhance platform capabilities
- Mentor team members and contribute to a culture of continuous learning and improvement
- Work in an agile environment with sprint planning, story elaboration, and iterative delivery












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications:


- 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High School Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications:


- 7+ years of professional software development experience with strong fundamentals in data structures and algorithms
- Strong experience with RESTful API design, GraphQL, and microservices architectures
- Hands-on experience with AWS serverless technologies (Lambda, API Gateway, AppSync, ECS/Fargate)
- Solid understanding of authentication and authorization concepts, patterns, and best practices
- Experience working with both relational databases (PostgreSQL) and NoSQL databases (DynamoDB) in production environments
- Strong grasp of security principles including encryption, token management, secret handling, and threat modeling
- Passion for writing maintainable code, automated tests, and clear documentation
- Collaborative mindset with excellent communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences
- Self-motivated learner who stays curious about new technologies and approaches
- BSc/BA in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
- Deep knowledge of identity protocols and standards (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, SAML, JWT, PKCE, WebAuthn)
- Experience building authentication or authorization systems from scratch or at scale
- Familiarity with identity providers and platforms (Auth0, Okta, Cognito etc.)
- Understanding of passwordless authentication patterns (passkeys, FIDO2, WebAuthn)
- Experience with mobile authentication patterns (biometric authentication, device binding, push notifications)
- Knowledge of zero-trust architecture principles and continuous verification patterns
- Experience designing adaptive or risk-based authentication systems
- Familiarity with session management patterns and token rotation strategies
- Understanding of cryptographic concepts (signing, encryption, key management, certificate handling)
- Experience with Infrastructure as Code tools like Terraform
- Knowledge of observability tools and distributed tracing (Dynatrace, OpenTelemetry, CloudWatch, X-Ray)
- Experience designing and deploying systems across multi-region architectures
- Familiarity with caching strategies for authentication state (ElastiCache, Redis)
- Understanding of compliance requirements (SOC2, PCI-DSS, GDPR, CCPA)
- Experience with CI/CD pipelines and automated security testing (SAST, DAST, dependency scanning)
- Background or interest in cybersecurity, fraud detection, or identity and access management domains
- Knowledge of event-driven architectures and how authentication signals power fraud detection systems
- Exposure to AI-assisted development tools and workflows #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).

jobDescriptionHtml:
Identity Shield & Consumer Fraud - Shield (Backend) .

General information

Career area
Technology
Work Location(s)
601 S. Tryon Street, NC
Remote?
No
Ref #
21748
Posted Date
02-20-26
Working time
Full time

Ally and Your Career

Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech

Join Ally's Identity Shield and Consumer Fraud team to build authentication and identity infrastructure that protects our digital banking customers and enables intelligent fraud prevention. You'll develop systems that implement fine-grained access control, manage authentication and authorization flows across Ally's digital banking ecosystem, and generate the behavioral and contextual signals that power adaptive fraud detection.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

The Work Itself

As a backend engineer on this team, you'll tackle fascinating challenges implementing identity protocols at scale—building OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect flows, developing authorization engines that evaluate complex policies with millisecond latency, and serving authentication decisions that directly impact our ability to keep customers safe and prevent fraud. You'll work with modern cloud technologies to build resilient, scalable platforms where performance and reliability are non-negotiable. This is your opportunity to shape Ally's next-generation identity and authentication infrastructure, mastering security-first design principles, distributed systems, serverless architectures, and AI-assisted development workflows.

This role offers the rare combination of high-impact work, cutting-edge technology, and meaningful outcomes. You'll collaborate across engineering, product, security, and data science teams to solve complex problems that matter. Whether you're developing authentication services that adapt to risk signals, implementing passwordless flows using passkeys and biometrics, building integrations with enterprise identity providers, or designing event streams that carry authentication signals to fraud detection systems, your work will be visible and valued.

On this team, we believe in relentless progress balanced with sustainable pace. We support each other, celebrate wins, learn from setbacks, and foster an environment where everyone can do their best work. Our #1 goal is team success, and our people are above all else.

 

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design and develop authentication and authorization services implementing modern identity protocols (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, SAML, JWT)
  • Build scalable APIs for identity management, user registration, verification, and account recovery
  • Implement session management and token lifecycle systems across web and mobile platforms
  • Develop fine-grained authorization engines and policy evaluation services for access control
  • Build integrations with distributed identity providers, enterprise SSO platforms, and social login services
  • Implement passwordless authentication flows using passkeys, biometrics, and device attestation
  • Develop adaptive authentication logic that adjusts requirements based on risk signals and context
  • Instrument authentication events with rich contextual metadata that power fraud detection and analytics
  • Implement comprehensive observability using OpenTelemetry across identity services
  • Write clean, tested code in Node.js, TypeScript, and Python following engineering best practices and team standards
  • Collaborate with frontend engineers, security teams, and data scientists to deliver solutions that drive real outcomes
  • Participate in architectural decisions, technical design reviews, and code reviews
  • Build and maintain infrastructure-as-code for identity platform components using Terraform
  • Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize platform performance, reliability, and security posture
  • Contribute to documentation, runbooks, and knowledge sharing across the team
  • Experiment with emerging technologies and propose innovations that enhance platform capabilities
  • Mentor team members and contribute to a culture of continuous learning and improvement
  • Work in an agile environment with sprint planning, story elaboration, and iterative delivery

The Skills You Bring

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
  • High School Diploma or GED equivalent

Preferred Qualifications:

  • 7+ years of professional software development experience with strong fundamentals in data structures and algorithms
  • Strong experience with RESTful API design, GraphQL, and microservices architectures
  • Hands-on experience with AWS serverless technologies (Lambda, API Gateway, AppSync, ECS/Fargate)
  • Solid understanding of authentication and authorization concepts, patterns, and best practices
  • Experience working with both relational databases (PostgreSQL) and NoSQL databases (DynamoDB) in production environments 
  • Strong grasp of security principles including encryption, token management, secret handling, and threat modeling
  • Passion for writing maintainable code, automated tests, and clear documentation
  • Collaborative mindset with excellent communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences
  • Self-motivated learner who stays curious about new technologies and approaches
  • BSc/BA in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
  • Deep knowledge of identity protocols and standards (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, SAML, JWT, PKCE, WebAuthn)
  • Experience building authentication or authorization systems from scratch or at scale
  • Familiarity with identity providers and platforms (Auth0, Okta, Cognito etc.)
  • Understanding of passwordless authentication patterns (passkeys, FIDO2, WebAuthn)
  • Experience with mobile authentication patterns (biometric authentication, device binding, push notifications)
  • Knowledge of zero-trust architecture principles and continuous verification patterns
  • Experience designing adaptive or risk-based authentication systems
  • Familiarity with session management patterns and token rotation strategies
  • Understanding of cryptographic concepts (signing, encryption, key management, certificate handling)
  • Experience with Infrastructure as Code tools like Terraform
  • Knowledge of observability tools and distributed tracing (Dynatrace, OpenTelemetry, CloudWatch, X-Ray)
  • Experience designing and deploying systems across multi-region architectures
  • Familiarity with caching strategies for authentication state (ElastiCache, Redis)
  • Understanding of compliance requirements (SOC2, PCI-DSS, GDPR, CCPA)
  • Experience with CI/CD pipelines and automated security testing (SAST, DAST, dependency scanning)
  • Background or interest in cybersecurity, fraud detection, or identity and access management domains
  • Knowledge of event-driven architectures and how authentication signals power fraud detection systems
  • Exposure to AI-assisted development tools and workflows
#LI-Hybrid

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










This role is remote in any of the 50 states. However, if you live near an Ally office, expectation is a hybrid role - 3 days in office / 2 days remote.

The Ally Auto Dealer Training Team/Ally Academy supports Ally’s core Auto Finance business by providing virtual and in-person instructor-led training to our dealer customers nationwide. We are seeking an automotive professional to join our team as an L&D Senior Specialist. This individual will be responsible for facilitating training for retail automobile dealers on all topics related to dealership operations, ranging from Variable and Fixed Ops management training, compliance training, sales & lease training, etc. and will have experience in the automotive retail environment. The L&D Senior Specialist will also facilitate and execute learning experiences in support of building Ally employee dealer customer acumen and will observe and coach Auto L&D facilitators to ensure the ongoing development and success of our team.

The right candidate must be willing to travel extensively, must be tech savvy, and must be willing and able to work with dealers and internal matrix partners to identify and support needs for training.













The Work Itself











- Participate in Train the Trainer (T3) events to learn and understand how to deliver each course
- Manage logistics regarding training events ranging from travel to classroom supplies, room setup, pre & post communications, etc.
- Facilitate Ally Academy Training Courses effectively and within established guidelines
- Leverage Zoom, PowerPoint, Turning Technologies, Salesforce, Teams, and other technologies to facilitate training and document training information/statistics
- Manage time/calendar to support overall objectives, while maintaining schedule to keep up with supporting tasks when not facilitating
- Maintain 95%+ Top 2 Box Survey Results from End-of-Course Surveys
- Function as a SME during development of new courses or adjustments to existing material
- Evaluate course effectiveness through end of course evaluations, participant comprehension, and operational metrics, recommending necessary content revisions
- Network internally with matrix teams and externally with dealer customer to identify opportunities/needs for training
- Attend matrix meetings to stay in touch and align with their needs
- Work within the team to support the business and overall objectives
- Align with Ally’s culture, core values, and manage risk appropriately













The Skills You Bring











Minimum Qualifications

- 3+ years of experience

Preferred Qualifications

- 5+ years retail automotive experience including extensive experience with dealership financials and financial reports

- Ability to travel 75%

Ally and Your Career

Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

This role is remote in any of the 50 states. However, if you live near an Ally office, expectation is a hybrid role - 3 days in office / 2 days remote.

The Ally Auto Dealer Training Team/Ally Academy supports Ally’s core Auto Finance business by providing virtual and in-person instructor-led training to our dealer customers nationwide. We are seeking an automotive professional to join our team as an L&D Senior Specialist. This individual will be responsible for facilitating training for retail automobile dealers on all topics related to dealership operations, ranging from Variable and Fixed Ops management training, compliance training, sales & lease training, etc. and will have experience in the automotive retail environment. The L&D Senior Specialist will also facilitate and execute learning experiences in support of building Ally employee dealer customer acumen and will observe and coach Auto L&D facilitators to ensure the ongoing development and success of our team. 

The right candidate must be willing to travel extensively, must be tech savvy, and must be willing and able to work with dealers and internal matrix partners to identify and support needs for training.

The Work Itself

  • Participate in Train the Trainer (T3) events to learn and understand how to deliver each course
  • Manage logistics regarding training events ranging from travel to classroom supplies, room setup, pre & post communications, etc.
  • Facilitate Ally Academy Training Courses effectively and within established guidelines
  • Leverage Zoom, PowerPoint, Turning Technologies, Salesforce, Teams, and other technologies to facilitate training and document training information/statistics
  • Manage time/calendar to support overall objectives, while maintaining schedule to keep up with supporting tasks when not facilitating
  • Maintain 95%+ Top 2 Box Survey Results from End-of-Course Surveys
  • Function as a SME during development of new courses or adjustments to existing material
  • Evaluate course effectiveness through end of course evaluations, participant comprehension, and operational metrics, recommending necessary content revisions
  • Network internally with matrix teams and externally with dealer customer to identify opportunities/needs for training
  • Attend matrix meetings to stay in touch and align with their needs
  • Work within the team to support the business and overall objectives
  • Align with Ally’s culture, core values, and manage risk appropriately

 

The Skills You Bring


Minimum Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience
  • HS Diploma or GED Equivalent

Preferred Qualifications 

  • 5+ years retail automotive experience  including extensive experience with dealership financials and financial reports
  • Experience working with Microsoft Suite, Outlook, Zoom, CRMs, DMSs, and other technologies
  • Ability to travel 75%
  • Effectively practices high stakes conversation tactics.
  • Ability to effectively facilitate large groups, in face-to-face or virtual environments, leveraging different facilitation methods and techniques.
  • Strong content organization skills: presentations are well-structured, informative, and use visuals effectively.

#LI-Remote 

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.

This position reviews and completes the fulfillment of tasks within policy and procedure and requires the identification and escalation of potential risks within each process. Job functions may include the investigation of payment history, reviewing accounts for repossession approval, and partnership with the multiple lines of business to resolve consumer issues. You will utilize strong organizational, data entry, analysis skills and the ability to escalate identified risks in your area of responsibility. This position may require the agent to be on call or work from home during building outages, catastrophic weather conditions, and business emergencies.













The Work Itself











- Receive and fulfill requests from other departments within Ally.
- Complete a variety of control-based tasks.
- Assist in partnership with complaints.
- Utilizing multiple systems simultaneously.
- Adherence to laptop security, and work from home policies.
- Communicates clearly and concisely via written or verbal communication to provide exceptional customer service regarding routine and escalated requests.
- Proficient computer skills.
- Knowledge of Ally systems preferred.
- Ability to research and solve operational problems while adhering to company guidelines.
- Perform qualitative review of individual situations, identify trends, and make recommendations to resolve issues.
- Ability to work in a high-volume environment with accuracy while achieving service level agreements requirements.
- Support various business and/or department level projects.
- Works closely with Special Handling groups on complex accounts (Bankruptcy, SCRA, Replevin, Deceased, Fraud, etc.).
- Meet internal goal metrics and be self-driven to exceed those goals.
- Meet compliance and QC requirements.
- Identify and provide appropriate training as needed.












The Skills You Bring











- Previous experience in Collections or Recovery operations preferred.
- HS diploma or equivalent required
- Performs clerical, administrative and technically based activities and/or supporting tasks.
- Requires intermediate knowledge of Microsoft Office programs, high proficiency preferred.
- Excellent written and oral communication skills.
- Comprehension and judgment are required regarding applicability of guidelines or policies to specific situations; correlational intelligence strongly preferred when applying lessons learned from one experience to the next opportunity.
- Able to work a flexible schedule which may include weekends, holidays, and overtime as necessary to meet business needs.
- Ingenuity and initiative required to collect information, coordinate activities with others, and resolve routine and/or factual problems.

jobDescriptionText:
Account Executive, F&I Dealer Products & Services - Greater Los Angeles, CA Sales Market


.































General information









Career area



Sales




Country



United States




State



California




City



Los Angeles

CA




Remote?



Yes




Ref #



21521




Posted Date



01-14-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity











- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the Greater Los Angeles, CA area requiring local travel throughout the surrounding Los Angeles sales market. Company vehicle provided.
- Successful account executives love to play. Creativity drives them – they throw new ideas against the wall and watch them stick. In this role, you’ll expand product offerings in existing business through cross-selling and increase market share with new clients. You’ll balance your compassion and empathy for everyone involved in the sales process. We see our account executives as problem solvers who are resilient, can face adversity, aren’t afraid of rejection and emerge as champions when times are tough. If you want flexibility, training to improve your skills and a management team that champions you and backs you up, Ally is the place to be.












The Work Itself











- You’ll sell finance & insurance (F&I) product lines to meet targeted goals by explaining corporate programs and service capabilities, while effectively overcoming objections.
- You’ll service current customers by maintaining rapport, making regular visits (there’s a lot of travel involved with this role), evaluating customer's needs for new opportunities, and replying to requests.
- You’ll also identify non-F&I opportunities and coordinate with the Insurance Specialist (identify new customers, estimate growth volume and qualify prospects).
- To ensure customers are fully knowledgeable with Ally’s products, you’ll assist in training F&I Managers within our dealerships.
- To sustain your success, you’ll maintain current knowledge about our competitors, market threats and challenges, maintain professional and technical knowledge by participating in training, and remain involved in the industry, respective area and trade associations.
- Through creative, strategic thinking and hard work, establishing yourself as a trusted advisor to the dealer principal and dealership staff. Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

  • Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the Greater Los Angeles, CA area requiring local travel throughout the surrounding Los Angeles sales market. Company vehicle provided. 
  • Successful account executives love to play. Creativity drives them – they throw new ideas against the wall and watch them stick. In this role, you’ll expand product offerings in existing business through cross-selling and increase market share with new clients. You’ll balance your compassion and empathy for everyone involved in the sales process. We see our account executives as problem solvers who are resilient, can face adversity, aren’t afraid of rejection and emerge as champions when times are tough. If you want flexibility, training to improve your skills and a management team that champions you and backs you up, Ally is the place to be.

The Work Itself

  • You’ll sell finance & insurance (F&I) product lines to meet targeted goals by explaining corporate programs and service capabilities, while effectively overcoming objections.
  • You’ll service current customers by maintaining rapport, making regular visits (there’s a lot of travel involved with this role), evaluating customer's needs for new opportunities, and replying to requests.
  • You’ll also identify non-F&I opportunities and coordinate with the Insurance Specialist (identify new customers, estimate growth volume and qualify prospects).
  • To ensure customers are fully knowledgeable with Ally’s products, you’ll assist in training F&I Managers within our dealerships.
  • To sustain your success, you’ll maintain current knowledge about our competitors, market threats and challenges, maintain professional and technical knowledge by participating in training, and remain involved in the industry, respective area and trade associations.
  • Through creative, strategic thinking and hard work, establishing yourself as a trusted advisor to the dealer principal and dealership staff. Regular analysis of performance metrics, F&I Training, and monthly roundtable meetings with dealership management are expected requirements of the role.

The Skills You Bring

  • 3+ years of experience required. 
  • Dealership and/or dealer-interfacing sales or F&I consulting experience. 
  • The ability to develop relationships with dealer principals and decision makers.
  • You have well-developed business management, presentation and relationship selling skills.
  • Holding yourself accountable is important to you. Being effective at prioritizing tasks is second nature. 
  • You’ve branded yourself as a problem solver and you objectively manage conflicting priorities.
  • High school diploma or GED equivalent required. Industry designations don’t hurt.

.































General information









Career area



Deposits Products & Marketing

601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No

21708




Posted Date

Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! The Opportunity










In office role with hybrid schedule, Ally work location is Charlotte NC. At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

Are you driven to make a meaningful difference for both customers and employees? As part of our Customer Advocacy and Insights team, the Manager of the Associate Voice & Experience Team will champion initiatives that shape experiences, drive strategic change, and amplify voices across the organization.

This role will continuously listen to our associates, translate their insights into meaningful action, and hold the organization accountable for improving the customer experience because Ally believes the best experiences start from the inside out.

If you’re seeking a role where your contributions are recognized, you and your teammates voices matter, and your work drives real change, we invite you to join us and help shape the future of our organization.













The Work Itself











- Set the vision and strategy for enterprise idea generation and associate feedback, translating insight into action in close partnership with stakeholders
- Turn feedback into results by identifying experience improvement opportunities, prioritizing them with cross-functional partners, and driving execution that improves both associate and customer outcomes.
- Build deep, trusted partnerships across lines of business to gather input, increase adoption, and deliver measurable, high-impact change.
- Design and continuously improve processes, tools, and deliverables that enable clear communication, effective enablement, and meaningful recognition at all levels of the organization.
- Scale and evolve listening channels to expand participation, engage new functional areas, and foster a culture of innovation and ownership.
- Lead executive level storytelling through concise communications and presentations that articulate the vision, track progress to outcomes, show business alignment, and highlight impact.
- Create clear, safe pathways for associates to surface challenges and insights, strengthening Ally’s ability to proactively improve employee and customer experiences.












The Skills You Bring











Minimum Qualifications:
- 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High School Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications:
- 5+ years of relevant experience in financial services industry is preferred.
- 5+ years leadership experience preferred.
- Bachelor's degree in related field strongly preferred, MBA is ideal.
- Proven ability to lead and prioritize multiple, concurrent initiatives, delivering measurable outcomes in a fast-paced, matrixed environment.
- Strong strategic thinking and analytical thinking skills, with demonstrated experience taking ambiguous problems from insight through execution.
- Exceptional organizational skills and program management skills, with the ability to oversee complex, multi-phase initiatives across teams and timelines.
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex insights into clear, compelling narratives for all levels of the organization.
- Deep understanding of human-centered and empathy-driven approaches, with a proven ability to convert insights into prioritized, actionable solutions.
- Proven ability to drive accountability across business partners and influence outcomes without direct ownership.
- Experience synthesizing and structuring large volumes of feedback into clear themes, priorities, and recommendations.
- Comfort building and leveraging technology-enabled listening, feedback, or insights platforms to scale engagement and decision-making.
#LI-Hybrid

- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

  • Set the vision and strategy for enterprise idea generation and associate feedback, translating insight into action in close partnership with stakeholders
  • Turn feedback into results by identifying experience improvement opportunities, prioritizing them with cross-functional partners, and driving execution that improves both associate and customer outcomes.
  • Build deep, trusted partnerships across lines of business to gather input, increase adoption, and deliver measurable, high-impact change.
  • Design and continuously improve processes, tools, and deliverables that enable clear communication, effective enablement, and meaningful recognition at all levels of the organization.
  • Scale and evolve listening channels to expand participation, engage new functional areas, and foster a culture of innovation and ownership.
  • Lead executive level storytelling through concise communications and presentations that articulate the vision, track progress to outcomes, show business alignment, and highlight impact.
  • Create clear, safe pathways for associates to surface challenges and insights, strengthening Ally’s ability to proactively improve employee and customer experiences.

The Skills You Bring


Minimum Qualifications:
  • 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
  • High School Diploma or GED equivalent 
Preferred Qualifications:
  • 5+ years of relevant experience in financial services industry is preferred.
  • 5+ years leadership experience preferred.
  • Bachelor's degree in related field strongly preferred, MBA is ideal.
  • Proven ability to lead and prioritize multiple, concurrent initiatives, delivering measurable outcomes in a fast-paced, matrixed environment.
  • Strong strategic thinking and analytical thinking skills, with demonstrated experience taking ambiguous problems from insight through execution.
  • Exceptional organizational skills and program management skills, with the ability to oversee complex, multi-phase initiatives across teams and timelines.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex insights into clear, compelling narratives for all levels of the organization.
  • Deep understanding of human-centered and empathy-driven approaches, with a proven ability to convert insights into prioritized, actionable solutions.
  • Proven ability to drive accountability across business partners and influence outcomes without direct ownership.
  • Experience synthesizing and structuring large volumes of feedback into clear themes, priorities, and recommendations.
  • Comfort building and leveraging technology-enabled listening, feedback, or insights platforms to scale engagement and decision-making.

#LI-Hybrid

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

jobDescriptionText:
Manager, Deposits Product Management - Sales and Onboarding


.































General information









Career area



Deposits Products & Marketing




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21596




Posted Date



02-05-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










This role will be responsible for building and managing a best-in-class deposit account opening experience and driving alignment amongst key partners (Finance, Sourcing, Channel, Technology, and Operations) to deliver on strategic business goals. Specific focus on maturing newly defined processes, successfully managing change in a dynamic work environment, and constantly analyzing how to make the account opening process better. Improvements to be measured across multiple dimensions (cost, efficiency, customer experience, and risk exposure). A premium is placed on relationship building, developing followership and collaboratively create solutions. Additionally, ability to successfully work with vendors and external partners is a critical component of success. Role has a deep understanding of the deposits business, strong financial acumen, synthesize information and data to form opinions that will help shape the business. This individual will need to have a thorough understanding of customer segments, consumer behaviors, product economics, and digital banking capabilities. Should have a deep understanding of deposit product specifics, deposit operations, account application processes, general analytics, and customer experience. Also necessary is a strong understanding and working knowledge of key risk management practices, risk framework, legal and compliance appropriate for transactional deposit products.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position. In office role with hybrid schedule - Ally work location is Charlotte NC













The Work Itself











- Responsible for developing innovative solutions using data, observations, and concepts to improve customer experience and deliver value
- Ensure business, technology, channel, marketing, and analytics partners have a clear understanding of product value (economics) and business objectives
- Responsible for improving customer satisfaction and shaping end-to-end customer experience for deposit account opening
- Identify and align necessary resources to define, scope, prioritize and execute work
- Actively work as a solution team member, helping balance priorities, financial impact, and the end-to-end customer experience
- Responsible for reviewing monthly business performance, assessing trends, opportunities, and developing forward looking outlook (expenses, account quality, and customer growth)
- Work closely with vendors to manage performance, SLA’s, contract dynamics, and overall relationship
- Engage new vendors to assess capability sets and ability to further advance Ally’s account open decision-making processes.
- Responsible for first line risk, compliance, regulatory and legal aspects of transactional deposit products













The Skills You Bring











- 5+ years of deposits experience with direct exposure to customer insights, analytics and or strategy
- Strong partnership focus; prior success with indirect leadership of all levels - up, down, and peer partners. Proven ability to work well with banking operations and information technology teams
- Customer-centric focus to product management; puts a priority on creating a premium customer experience
- Strategic thinking; able to link disparate concepts/ideas to develop a coherent approach solving for business performance and unlocking value
- Ability to work at a very detailed level, as well as synthesize and present key action items / insights
- Excellent presentation and written communication skills, proficient in MS office tools including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
- Strong aptitude for data driven hypotheses, analysis, and outcome-based recommendations. Can leverage data visualization tools (e.g., PowerBi) and self-serve data access and analysis.
- Thrives in a dynamic environment - no standard day
- High aptitude for new skills, flexibility around work assignments, and strong desire to work as part of a team
- Bachelor’s degree preferred, advanced degree preferred
#LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

jobDescriptionText:
Manager, HR Business Partner


.































General information









Career area



Human Resources




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21856




Posted Date



03-05-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










This role is on a hybrid schedule in our Charlotte or Detroit office, in a few days each week.

As an HRBP Manager, you will serve as a strategic talent partner to a defined business unit or function, working in close partnership with a Senior Director HRBP. This individual contributor role will support our c ontrol functions including legal , corporate affairs, and Audit.

You will facilitate the human capital strategy while taking ownership to lead select initiatives within your client group. You will use an analytical mindset, industry knowledge, and strong business acumen to address talent opportunities and challenges, enabling operational efficiency and growth in a technology- and AI-enabled environment. You will operate with minimal to moderate oversight, balancing independent problem-solving with collaboration across HR Centers of Excellence (COEs). You will regularly interact with mid-to-senior-level leaders (including Executive leadership), building trust and influence to drive outcomes that align to business strategy and the Ally “Do It Right” culture.













The Work Itself











- Serve as a trusted advisor to mid-to-senior leaders, aligning human capital priorities to business strategy and performance goals.
- Translate business needs into HR plans in partnership with HRBP leadership; own delivery and outcomes within your client portfolio.
- Lead or co-lead components of organizational design initiatives aligned to strategy, growth priorities, and operational efficiency.
- Assess organizational health, including spans and layers, decision rights, role clarity, and operating mechanisms to improve agility, speed, and accountability.
- Guide workforce assessment and enablement, including reskilling, redeployment, and role design/redesign in partnership with Talent, L&D, and TA COEs.
- Partner with Total Rewards and leaders on retention and rewards insights; provide inputs to workforce planning and succession/talent reviews.
- Lead moderate-scale change initiatives for your client groups and support larger enterprise changes led by HRBP leadership.
- Ensure alignment across performance, rewards, learning, and talent programs to reinforce desired behaviors and outcomes.
- Partner with workforce analytics and leverage HR data to diagnose issues and trends (e.g., turnover, engagement, performance distribution); convert insights into recommendations and actions hikers with leaders and COEs.
- Coach leaders on org health, performance, engagement, and team effectiveness; navigate nuanced situations such as conflict resolution and mediation, applying effective challenge, empathy, and risk awareness.
- Consult on annual talent processes (e.g., performance management, compensation, engagement, talent management, talent acquisition, and leadership development).
- Translate leadership needs into COE requirements and provide feedback to shape future content, programs, and policies.
- Proactively identify practical opportunities to enable HR support using AI and evolving technologies; pilot and champion data-driven, tech-enabled practices where appropriate.
- Apply relevant employment laws, regulations, policies, and procedures to business decisions.
- Role-model Ally’s values and “Do It Right” culture in all interactions.












The Skills You Bring











​Minimum Requirements
- 7 years of relevant experience or equivalent
- High School Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- 7+ years of progressive HR experience across HRBP and/or HR COE disciplines (e.g., Talent, Total Rewards, Employee Relations, L&D) OR 5+ years of progressive HR experience and a bachelor’s degree in HR or a related field
- At least 2 years in a consulting role
- Demonstrated experience supporting and influencing mid-to-senior leaders, with evidence of leading moderate-scale org design or change initiatives.
- Experience operating in complex, fast-changing, or technology-driven environments; consulting experience a plus
- Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business, or related field preferred.
- PHR, SHRM-CP (or higher) recommended.
- Business acumen and systems thinking; ability to connect human capital levers to business outcomes.
- Strong coaching, facilitation, and communication skills; capable of presenting complex ideas to groups and influencing diverse stakeholders.
- Data literacy: analyze, interpret, and translate data into insights and action plans; comfort partnering with analytics teams.
- Change management skills; able to plan, execute, and sustain change with cross-functional partners.
- Relationship management and credibility-building with leaders; sound judgment, creativity, and problem-solving in ambiguous situations.
- Strong command of employment law and risk management.
- Curiosity and baseline fluency in AI with a practical orientation toward integrating tech into HR deliverables.












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

  • Serve as a trusted advisor to mid-to-senior leaders, aligning human capital priorities to business strategy and performance goals.
  • Translate business needs into HR plans in partnership with HRBP leadership; own delivery and outcomes within your client portfolio.
  • Lead or co-lead components of organizational design initiatives aligned to strategy, growth priorities, and operational efficiency.
  • Assess organizational health, including spans and layers, decision rights, role clarity, and operating mechanisms to improve agility, speed, and accountability.
  • Guide workforce assessment and enablement, including reskilling, redeployment, and role design/redesign in partnership with Talent, L&D, and TA COEs.
  • Partner with Total Rewards and leaders on retention and rewards insights; provide inputs to workforce planning and succession/talent reviews.
  • Lead moderate-scale change initiatives for your client groups and support larger enterprise changes led by HRBP leadership.
  • Ensure alignment across performance, rewards, learning, and talent programs to reinforce desired behaviors and outcomes.
  • Partner with workforce analytics and leverage HR data to diagnose issues and trends (e.g., turnover, engagement,  performance distribution); convert insights into recommendations and actions hikers with leaders and COEs.
  • Coach leaders on org health, performance, engagement, and team effectiveness; navigate nuanced situations such as conflict resolution and mediation, applying effective challenge, empathy, and risk awareness.
  • Consult on annual talent processes (e.g., performance management, compensation, engagement, talent management, talent acquisition, and leadership development).
  • Translate leadership needs into COE requirements and provide feedback to shape future content, programs, and policies.
  • Proactively identify practical opportunities to enable HR support using AI and evolving technologies; pilot and champion data-driven, tech-enabled practices where appropriate.
  • Apply relevant employment laws, regulations, policies, and procedures to business decisions.
  • Role-model Ally’s values and “Do It Right” culture in all interactions.

The Skills You Bring


​Minimum Requirements
  • 7 years of relevant experience or equivalent
  • High School Diploma or GED equivalent
Preferred Qualifications
  • 7+ years of progressive HR experience across HRBP and/or HR COE disciplines (e.g., Talent, Total Rewards, Employee Relations, L&D) OR 5+ years of progressive HR experience and a bachelor’s degree in HR or a related field
  • At least 2 years in a consulting role
  • Demonstrated experience supporting and influencing mid-to-senior leaders, with evidence of leading moderate-scale org design or change initiatives.
  • Experience operating in complex, fast-changing, or technology-driven environments; consulting experience a plus
  • Bachelor’s degree in HR, Business, or related field preferred.
  • PHR, SHRM-CP (or higher) recommended.
  • Business acumen and systems thinking; ability to connect human capital levers to business outcomes.
  • Strong coaching, facilitation, and communication skills; capable of presenting complex ideas to groups and influencing diverse stakeholders.
  • Data literacy: analyze, interpret, and translate data into insights and action plans; comfort partnering with analytics teams.
  • Change management skills; able to plan, execute, and sustain change with cross-functional partners.
  • Relationship management and credibility-building with leaders; sound judgment, creativity, and problem-solving in ambiguous situations.
  • Strong command of employment law and risk management.
  • Curiosity and baseline fluency in AI with a practical orientation toward integrating tech into HR deliverables.

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Base Pay Range: $90000 - $150000 USD
An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer. 
Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.
