jobDescriptionText:
Manager - Software Engineering


.































General information









Career area



Technology




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21721




Posted Date



02-19-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech

Responsible for managing the results of software development teams, to ensure software solutions are built according to company standards and are compliant with business requirements. Reviews and evaluates the performance of the development teams and of the individuals on the teams. Ultimately accountable for project deliverables, budgets, schedules and quality. Serves as the primary interface with IT and business partners on software development projects. This position will report to a Director of Software Development and may have responsibility for directly managing one or more development resources.

The ideal candidate will possess technical leadership experience on the Salesforce Service cloud platform and is passionate about delivering quality user experience to millions of Ally customers across delivery channels and lines of business. Candidates should have experience working in the Customer Care/Contact Center space and possess attention to detail and a track record of flawless execution



At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself










This position will be the team champion for all Salesforce implementations and participate/lead the modernization of customer care platforms through automation, agentic AI, workflow optimization and integration. The tech lead will also support the rollout of additional Salesforce products like – FSC, Data Cloud, Agentforce, etc. In addition, the manager will -


- Partner with business, UX design and back-end API teams to stand up contact center capabilities across delivery channels of voice, chat, SMS, and push notifications
- Guide development teams to review business requirements, design documents, test plans/test cases
- Partner with architecture and external development teams to design technical solutions
- Lead the development and deployment of new features with your team
- Recommend optimizations for improved efficiency and speed to market for new alerting capabilities
- Enforce performance & code quality standards through peer code reviews and automated testing
- Assist with hiring, onboarding and mentoring of new developers for your team












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications:
- 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High School Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications:
- 5+ years of Salesforce experience
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or other related discipline preferred.
- 5+ years of experience with Service Cloud, Data Cloud, Agentforce, Financial Services Cloud, Experience Cloud, etc.?
- 3+ years of experience with custom development within Salesforce and integrations with other applications?
- 2+ years of experience with solution designing and User Experience for digital and contact center agents?
- 2+ years of experience managing small to large Salesforce engineering teams?
- Ability to supervise staff and make personnel decisions.
- Ability to manage multiple complex software development projects.
- Ability to articulate the company's business requirements and objectives and translate those into software development solutions.
- Ability to effectively communicate software development issues and recommendations to senior level management within and outside of IT.
- Knowledge of all phases of software development. Technical Skills Required:


- Must have Salesforce certifications like - Platform Developer 1 or 2, App Builder, Administrator/Advanced Administrator or Service Cloud.
- Technical architecture or development certification preferred
- Must have experience with Eclipse IDE, Force.com ANT, Developer Console for development, debugging and query
- Must have experience working with integration features such as callouts, future methods, batch and scheduled apex, REST and SOAP APIs
- GraphQL experience is preferred.
- Must have experience with SFDC Developer, Apex data loader for data loads (inserts/updates) in Salesforce
- Hands-on experience with both point-and-click configuration as well as programmatic customization
- Experience with tools and techniques to ensure compatibility of rendering of content across browsers, clients and form factors.
- Solid understanding of JavaScript and OAuth is required #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

The Opportunity










The Executive Transportation Specialist is responsible for providing safe, discreet and secure transportation for company executives, board members, and VIP quests. The role requires exceptional driving skills, sound judgement, and a deep commitment to safety, confidentiality, and service excellence. Occasional travel and support in other geographic locations may be required on a short-term, temporary basis.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position. This role reports to the office on d aily basis #LI-Onsite












The Work Itself











- Provide security, comfortable, and punctual transportation for executives.
- Identify and mitigate potential hostile situations to protect executives.
- Communicate effectively with executives and security personnel.
- Conduct route planning to ensure the safety of executives, company information, property, and the company's reputation.
- Provide security for the executive floor and at events in the specified area.
- Learn to conduct site security assessments and liaise with law enforcement and other security professionals to ensure a safe environment for executive travel and events.
- Assist in development of threat assessments and completion of security plans and briefs for events and executive travel.
- Support Physical Security Program Administration team to complete quality assurance reviews
- Respond to emergencies during executive transportation as needed.
- Be available to work nights and weekends as required.
- Handle sensitive and confidential information with the utmost discretion and always maintain discretion and confidentiality.












The Skills You Bring











- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Criminal Justice, Security Management, or a related field is preferred.
- +1 years of proven experience in executive protection, law enforcement, or military protective services required.
- Experience supporting C-suite executive movements required.
- Must have a valid state driver license.
- Executive Protection certification from a recognized training program preferred.
- Strong knowledge of protective security principles, advance work, and understanding of security threats associated with C-suite executives.
- Current certifications in CPR, First Aid, and AED highly recommended
- Defensive and evasive driving training highly desirable.
- Proven ability to apply good judgment and promptly raise a critical issue to leadership when necessary.
- Excellent knowledge of local and regional roadways, airports, and traffic patterns.
- Strong situational awareness, discretion, emotional intelligence and communication skills.
- Ability to adapt to a dynamic security environment.
- Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information.
- Availability to work nights and weekends.
- Basic computer knowledge and use.

Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

The Executive Transportation Specialist is responsible for providing safe, discreet and secure transportation for company executives, board members, and VIP quests. The role requires exceptional driving skills, sound judgement, and a deep commitment to safety, confidentiality, and service excellence. Occasional travel and support in other geographic locations may be required on a short-term, temporary basis.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.
This role reports to the office on daily basis  #LI-Onsite

The Work Itself

  • Provide security, comfortable, and punctual transportation for executives.
  • Identify and mitigate potential hostile situations to protect executives.
  • Communicate effectively with executives and security personnel.
  • Conduct route planning to ensure the safety of executives, company information, property, and the company's reputation.
  • Provide security for the executive floor and at events in the specified area.
  • Learn to conduct site security assessments and liaise with law enforcement and other security professionals to ensure a safe environment for executive travel and events.
  • Assist in development of threat assessments and completion of security plans and briefs for events and executive travel.
  • Support Physical Security Program Administration team to complete quality assurance reviews
  • Respond to emergencies during executive transportation as needed.
  • Be available to work nights and weekends as required.
  • Handle sensitive and confidential information with the utmost discretion and always maintain discretion and confidentiality.

The Skills You Bring

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Criminal Justice, Security Management, or a related field is preferred.
  • +1 years of proven experience in executive protection, law enforcement, or military protective services required.
  • Experience supporting C-suite executive movements required.
  • Must have a valid state driver license.
  • Executive Protection certification from a recognized training program preferred.
  • Strong knowledge of protective security principles, advance work, and understanding of security threats associated with C-suite executives.
  • Current certifications in CPR, First Aid, and AED highly recommended
  • Defensive and evasive driving training highly desirable.
  • Proven ability to apply good judgment and promptly raise a critical issue to leadership when necessary.
  • Excellent knowledge of local and regional roadways, airports, and traffic patterns.
  • Strong situational awareness, discretion, emotional intelligence and communication skills.
  • Ability to adapt to a dynamic security environment.
  • Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information.
  • Availability to work nights and weekends.
  • Basic computer knowledge and use.

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Base Pay Range: $55000 - $95000 USD
An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer. 
Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech.


Ally is committed to transforming our data governance and management capabilities to drive business value and uphold our “do it right” approach. We are seeking a goal-oriented Director who is passionate about data governance and management to lead the execution and continuous improvement of Ally’s data governance and management policies, enablement services, and compliance measurement within a function or business line. This pivotal role will lead business-line data governance and management efforts, while collaborating closely with the Enterprise Data Governance & Management (EDGM) team, and senior business and technology leaders across the organization to ensure data quality, integrity, compliance, and accessibility. If you are a forwarding-thinking leader with a passion for data governance and management, and you are ready to drive impactful change, we encourage you to apply.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself










Enablement & Policy Adoption:
- Design and oversee business line enablement services (e.g., execution, training, tooling, communication and reporting) to help your business line to understand and successfully adopt a consistent and comprehensive approach in adhering to the data governance and management policy, standards, and procedures
- Oversee the execution of business line activities to enable data governance and management adherence across requirements for stewardship, metadata, data dictionaries, data lineage, data quality, and data issue management
- Partner with business line leadership, business data leads, and business line risk partners to drive alignment and accountability across data management expectations
- Act as a subject matter expert across the data domains and data products within the aligned business line Culture & Change Leadership:
- Champion a culture of data accountability, stewardship, and data-driven decision making within the business line
- Lead change management efforts to embed data governance and management practices into daily business operations, routines, and decision-making and enable the enterprise’s strategic objective of successful adoption of data governance practices across the organization
- Stay current with industry data management and data analytics trends, including embedding AI practices Executive Communication:
- Develop and deliver executive-level updates on business line performance in adhering to data governance and management requirements, reporting regularly to senior stakeholders within the business line and across the enterprise on progress and the data agenda within the business line as necessary Data Issue Management:
- Support the business line in identification, prioritization, tracking, and remediation of data defect and data issue management processes
- Understand the regulatory requirements and frameworks that are applicable to your business line, especially as these requirements relate to identifying the critical data and data risks within your business line
- Work with business data leads, business risk partners, and second-line stakeholders, to monitor business line compliance with regulatory requirements and proactively manage data risks
- Serve as the primary point of escalation for any such identified risks to senior leadership Execution Excellence:
- Establish performance targets, key performance indicators (KPIs), and success metrics for the business line in line with senior leadership and enterprise data governance expectations
- Partner with the enterprise data governance team to measure effectiveness and identify opportunities to improve data governance maturity and operational consistency for your business line Team Leadership:
- Lead and mentor a team of data governance professionals and other key business line team members (i.e., business report owners, data owners)
- Provide coaching and monitor adoption and adherence to data governance and management best practices within your business line Enterprise Collaboration:
- Serve as the primary data lead for the business line, partnering with diverse business, technology, and risk teams across the enterprise, including the enterprise data governance team, as each business line begins to take accountability for managing their data













The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications:
9+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience​ High School Diploma or GED equivalent

Strongly Preferred Qualifications:
- 7+ years of experience in data governance, data management, data analysis or related roles, with at least 3 years in a leadership/management capacity driving technology project initiatives
- 2+ years of hands-on data management execution (e.g., writing data quality rules, creating data dictionaries, identifying lineage, remediating data errors)
- 2+ years of practical experience writing effective SQL queries to retrieve and analyze data accurately and efficiently
- Proficient in Power BI; capable of dissecting reports, verifying data lineage and business logic, and remediating data quality issues without relying solely on data owners
Preferred Qualifications:

- Experience : 7+ years of experience in data governance, data management, data analysis or related roles, with at least 3 years in a leadership / management capacity; Financial Services industry experience preferred
- Experience: 2+ years of practical experience writing SQL queries (e.g., joins, filters, aggregations, CTEs/window functions) or related querying languages to retrieve data for analysis, assess data integrity, and/or trace data flows across systems
- Expertise : Understanding of data governance frameworks, policies, and standards (e.g., DCAM), proven experience with data management concepts (e.g., data catalogs, data lineage, data quality), and familiarity with data governance tooling (e.g., Alation, Collibra, Manta, Ab Initio)
- Education : Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analysis, Mathematics, Economics, or related discipline
- Leadership : Proven experience in leading large-scale data initiatives, cross-functional teams, and a team of data governance professionals
- Technical Skillset : Understanding of programming languages (e.g., Python, SQL, DBT) and data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Oracle, AWS)
- Communication : Strong communication, influencing, and stakeholder management skills, including ability to navigate complex organizational environments
- Passion : Understands the tremendous impact foundational data governance and management can make for an organization and is enthusiastic about supporting the organization through the journey
- Approach : Strategic mindset paired with pragmatic execution capabilities
- Risk Management: Ability to identify, assess, and mitigate data-related risks within the organization
- Collaboration : Strong collaboration skills to work effectively with diverse teams and colleagues at all levels
- Presence : Ability to communicate effectively to executive leadership in both business and technology roles
- Decision Making : Ability to manage complexity, prioritize team deliverables, and make or influence effective decisions
- Industry experience : Financial Services / highly regulated industry experience strongly preferred #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.






Base Pay Range : $135000 - $235000 USD An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

Ally is committed to transforming our data governance and management capabilities to drive business value and uphold our “do it right” approach. - Promote a culture of data accountability, stewardship, and data-driven decision making across your aligned business line
- Provide support in planning and executing change management efforts to ensure successful adoption of the enterprise data governance agenda across the organization
- Stay current with industry data management and data analytics trends, including embedding AI practices Data Issue Management:
- Support the business line in identification, prioritization, tracking, and remediation of data defect and data issue management processes
- Understand the regulatory requirements and frameworks that are applicable to your business line, especially as these requirements relate to identifying the critical data and data risks within your business line
- Work with business data leads, business risk partners, and second-line stakeholders, to monitor business line / function compliance with regulatory requirements, proactively manage data risks, and coordinate remediation of open business line data defects and data issues Execution Excellence:
- Manage the day-to-day execution of data governance and management requirements within the business line / function
- Support documentation of asset-level and element-level data lineage, support business and technical metadata management activities for data dictionary and business glossary creation and maintenance, identify and maintain data quality rules and controls, and identify, collate, and drive remediation of improvement opportunities
- Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure effectiveness and drive continuous improvement Team Management:
- Provide oversight to junior data governance analysts, supporting their development and ensuring quality execution
- Work closely with senior leadership and enterprise data governance partners to manage the day-to-day execution of data governance-related activities within your business line Enterprise Collaboration:
- Work with diverse business, technology, and risk teams across the enterprise, including the enterprise data governance team, as each business line begins to take accountability for managing their data Policy Accountability:
- Help interpret data governance and management policies, standards, procedures, and related documentation for your business line












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications:
7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience​ High School Diploma or GED equivalent

Strongly Preferred:
- 5+ years of experience in data governance, data management, data analysis or related roles, with at least 1-2 years in a team lead, senior analyst, or supervisory role
- 2+ years of hands-on data management execution (e.g., writing data quality rules, creating data dictionaries, identifying lineage, remediating data errors)
- 2+ years of practical experience writing effective SQL queries to retrieve and analyze data accurately and efficiently
- Proficient in Power BI; capable of dissecting reports, verifying data lineage and business logic, and remediating data quality issues without relying solely on data owners
Preferred Qualifications:
- Expertise : Understanding of data governance frameworks, policies, and standards (e.g., DCAM) and familiarity with data management concepts (e.g., data catalogs, data lineage, data quality) and data governance tooling (e.g., Alation, Collibra, Manta, Ab Initio)
- Education : Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analysis, Mathematics, Economics, or related discipline
- Leadership : Demonstrated ability to guide junior staff, coordinate cross-functional work, and support large-scale data initiative
- Technical Skillset : Understanding of programing languages (e.g. Python, SQL, DBT) and data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Oracle, AWS)
- Communication : Strong communication and stakeholder-management skills; an ability to translate data governance requirements into actionable steps for business line / function partners
- Facilitation: Strong ability to facilitate meetings and discussions with cross-functional stakeholders to drive alignment and decision-making
- Passion : Understands the tremendous impact good data governance and management can make for an organization and is enthusiastic about supporting the organization through the journey
- Approach : Strategic mindset paired with pragmatic execution capabilities
- Risk Management : Ability to identify, assess, and mitigate data-related risks within the organization
- Collaboration : Strong collaboration skills to work effectively with diverse teams and colleagues at all levels
- Presence : Ability to communicate effectively to senior leadership in both business and technology roles
- Decision Making : Ability to manage complexity, prioritize team deliverables, and make effective decisions
- Industry Experience : Financial Services / highly regulated industry experience preferred #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

Full time

Ally and Your Career

Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that has worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We are always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions. To read more about what our tech team does, be sure to visit our tech blog at ally.tech. 

Ally is hiring for four Manager - Data Governance positions. Each role will be aligned to either the Auto, Technology, Risk, or Consumer Banking organization. 

Ally is committed to transforming our data governance and management capabilities to drive business value and uphold our “do it right” approach. We are seeking a results-oriented Manager who brings hands on experience in data governance and management to support the execution and continuous improvement of Ally’s data governance and management policies, enablement services, and compliance measurement within a function or business line. This pivotal role will manage the day-to-day business-line data governance and management efforts, while collaborating closely with the Enterprise Data Governance & Management (EDGM) team, and business and technology leaders across the organization to ensure data quality, integrity, compliance, and accessibility. If you are a forwarding-thinking manager with a passion for data governance and management, and you are ready to drive impactful change, we encourage you to apply.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position. 

The Work Itself

Enablement Services:
  • Manage data governance, data domain, and data product execution activities within your business line
  • Coordinate with and support business line data owners, business data leads, and business risk partners with data governance-related enablement activities to help your business line understand and successfully adopt enterprise data management requirements (e.g., data quality, data dictionaries, data lineage, data stewardship, data classification, data privacy, etc.)
  • Conduct business line training and awareness campaigns for data governance leveraging enterprise materials
  • Provide data tooling and data pipelines support, embedding data quality checks, determining data quality rule thresholds, tracking lineage
  • Track the status of key data governance activities and initiatives
Executive Communication:
  • Coordinate the preparation of reporting inputs, dashboards, and status summaries to be used by senior leadership in providing executive-level updates on progress with adhering to data governance and management requirements
  • Monitor and address results of enterprise dashboards, metrics, and summaries that reflect business line / function performance and alignment with expectations and key risk indicators
Culture Change:
  • Promote a culture of data accountability, stewardship, and data-driven decision making across your aligned business line
  • Provide support in planning and executing change management efforts to ensure successful adoption of the enterprise data governance agenda across the organization
  • Stay current with industry data management and data analytics trends, including embedding AI practices
Data Issue Management:
  • Support the business line in identification, prioritization, tracking, and remediation of data defect and data issue management processes
  • Understand the regulatory requirements and frameworks that are applicable to your business line, especially as these requirements relate to identifying the critical data and data risks within your business line
  • Work with business data leads, business risk partners, and second-line stakeholders, to monitor business line / function compliance with regulatory requirements, proactively manage data risks, and coordinate remediation of open business line data defects and data issues
Execution Excellence:
  • Manage the day-to-day execution of data governance and management requirements within the business line / function
  • Support documentation of asset-level and element-level data lineage, support business and technical metadata management activities for data dictionary and business glossary creation and maintenance, identify and maintain data quality rules and controls, and identify, collate, and drive remediation of improvement opportunities
  • Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure effectiveness and drive continuous improvement
Team Management:
  • Provide oversight to junior data governance analysts, supporting their development and ensuring quality execution
  • Work closely with senior leadership and enterprise data governance partners to manage the day-to-day execution of data governance-related activities within your business line
Enterprise Collaboration:
  • Work with diverse business, technology, and risk teams across the enterprise, including the enterprise data governance team, as each business line begins to take accountability for managing their data
Policy Accountability:
  • Help interpret data governance and management policies, standards, procedures, and related documentation for your business line
 

The Skills You Bring

Minimum Qualifications:
7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience​
High School Diploma or GED equivalent

Strongly Preferred: 
  • 5+ years of experience in data governance, data management, data analysis or related roles, with at least 1-2 years in a team lead, senior analyst, or supervisory role
  • 2+ years of hands-on data management execution (e.g., writing data quality rules, creating data dictionaries, identifying lineage, remediating data errors)
  • 2+ years of practical experience writing effective SQL queries to retrieve and analyze data accurately and efficiently
  • Proficient in Power BI; capable of dissecting reports, verifying data lineage and business logic, and remediating data quality issues without relying solely on data owners

Preferred Qualifications: 
  • Expertise: Understanding of data governance frameworks, policies, and standards (e.g., DCAM) and familiarity with data management concepts (e.g., data catalogs, data lineage, data quality) and data governance tooling (e.g., Alation, Collibra, Manta, Ab Initio) 
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analysis, Mathematics, Economics, or related discipline 
  • Leadership: Demonstrated ability to guide junior staff, coordinate cross-functional work, and support large-scale data initiative 
  • Technical Skillset: Understanding of programing languages (e.g. Python, SQL, DBT) and data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Oracle, AWS)
  • Communication: Strong communication and stakeholder-management skills; an ability to translate data governance requirements into actionable steps for business line / function partners 
  • Facilitation: Strong ability to facilitate meetings and discussions with cross-functional stakeholders to drive alignment and decision-making
  • Passion: Understands the tremendous impact good data governance and management can make for an organization and is enthusiastic about supporting the organization through the journey 
  • Approach: Strategic mindset paired with pragmatic execution capabilities 
  • Risk Management: Ability to identify, assess, and mitigate data-related risks within the organization 
  • Collaboration: Strong collaboration skills to work effectively with diverse teams and colleagues at all levels 
  • Presence: Ability to communicate effectively to senior leadership in both business and technology roles 
  • Decision Making: Ability to manage complexity, prioritize team deliverables, and make effective decisions 
  • Industry Experience: Financial Services / highly regulated industry experience preferred 
#LI-Hybrid

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. jobDescriptionText:
Office Claims Adjuster - ADI Claims


.































General information









Career area



Insurance




Work Location(s)



1450 American Lane, IL




Remote?



No




Ref #



21522




Posted Date



01-20-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










We’re looking for people interested in claim adjudication and who are focused on helping our customers while maintaining Ally’s interest. You will be responsible for the investigation, valuation, establishment of exposure, negotiation and settlement property and casualty claims within a predetermined settlement authority. Ensures that all assigned claims are concluded promptly, equitably and economically within the provisions of the policy contract and in accordance with the policy. If you’d like to learn more about what we believe makes this role - and this place - so phenomenal, get in touch.












The Work Itself











- Handles intake of first notice of loss reports.
- Conducts investigations independently and at the direction of management utilizing sound investigative procedures to establish liability as
- quickly as possible.
- Negotiates settlement of claims with insureds while following established, authorized settlement authority.
- Handles questions on coverage.
- Maintain a complete and thoroughly documented claim file ensuring claim files are properly documented and all supporting documents are
- attached.
- Uses compassionate communication and persuasive negotiation to ensure a positive customer experience
- Treat customers in a courteous, friendly and professional manner providing timely, thorough, accurate information and assistance.
- Work closely with the field appraisers to ensure accurate and timely damage evaluations.
- Adjusts reserves to accurately reflect the exposure and issue payments to the appropriate parties.
- Identify subrogation and/or salvage situations in a timely manner to protect the interests of the Corporation and request documents needed
- to process titles and salvage of vehicles.
- Handles all claims within the guidelines of the state’s Fair Claims Practices Acts and other Regulations while also ensuring timeliness and KPIs are being met.
- Maintain an up-to-date dairy.
- Maintain necessary licensing and continuing education.












The Skills You Bring











- HS diploma or equivalent required. Associate / Bachelor's degree in business related discipline preferred.
- 1+ years in customer service or financial services industry is preferred.
- Multi-state adjuster licenses preferred but not required.
- 1+ year of prior property and casualty experience preferred.
- The ability to take detail statements and demonstrate the ability to interpret and apply written coverage accurately to establish claim and determine an action plan
- Ability to resolve conflicts and empathize with customers is critical, treating customers in a courteous, friendly and professional manner.
- Demonstrate an understanding of insurance law as it relates to claims
- High degree of analytical, negotiation and organizational skills
- Typically requires advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office Programs.
- Leveraging technical expertise and relationships to contribute to strategy and drive business results.

#DFS
#LI-Hyrbid

- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. jobTitle: Account Executive, Auto Finance - Greater Portland/Salem/Albany, OR Sales Market
jobDescriptionText:
Account Executive, Auto Finance - Greater Portland/Salem/Albany, OR Sales Market


.































General information









Career area



Sales




Country



United States




State



Oregon




Work Location(s)



OR




Remote?



Yes




Ref #



21831




Posted Date



03-05-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity











- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the Greater Portland, Salem or Albany Oregon area requiring local travel throughout the surrounding sales market. Company vehicle provided.
- An Account Executive for Ally Auto Finance acts as a field sales representative, promoting the full breadth of Ally’s auto finance products and services to dealerships and equipping them with the tools necessary to grow their business and support their customers. They identify, pursue, and maximize business opportunities within their local market and create solid working relationships with existing and new dealers.












The Work Itself











- Manage the full sales lifecycle for Ally’s auto finance product offerings and act as a singular point-of-contact for existing and new dealerships in a designated market.
- Demonstrate a thorough knowledge of all Ally’s auto finance products, plans, and programs such as floorplan financing, Dealer Products & Services, and SmartAuction.
- Market and promote the full breadth of Ally's products and services including commercial banking products.
- Communicate and liaise with designated Consumer Underwriters and peer Account Executives across Ally Auto and Insurance.
- Coordinate with key product specialists on the team to provide assistance, training, and consultative services to all assigned dealers.
- Manage risk as a Dealership Performance Consultant.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 3+ years of experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- 3+ years in a dealer-interfacing role within the auto finance space.
- Ability to leverage industry expertise and relationships to contribute to business strategy and drive profitability.
- Full sales lifecycle experience with a strong foundation in business management, analytics, presentation, and relationship selling skills.
- Active problem solver with an aptitude for managing conflicting priorities.












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.






Base Pay Range : $85000 - $120000 USD An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in a variable incentive plan.

jobTitle: Director of Sales, Ally Dealership Insurance (ADI) - Northeast Region / Greater Pittsburgh, PA Area
jobDescriptionText:
Director of Sales, Ally Dealership Insurance (ADI) - Northeast Region / Greater Pittsburgh, PA Area


.

Career area



Sales




Country



United States




State



Pennsylvania




City



Pittsburgh




Work Location(s)



PA




Remote?



Yes




Ref #



21923




Posted Date



03-06-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity











- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the Greater Pittsburgh, PA area. Approximately 75% travel throughout the Northeast region will be required for this role, with approximately 7 overnights per month expected. Company vehicle provided.
- The Director of Sales - Property & Casualty Insurance (DOS) leads, trains, motivates, and supports a team of Account Executives (AE).
- The DOS develops or enables the development of new and expanding business opportunities.
- The DOS is responsible for the acquisition of new accounts as well as the retention and service to existing accounts.
- The DOS will facilitate the integration of AE's into a sales operating area for all the P&C products.
- The DOS must manage the overall aggregate and individual AE production based upon our acquisition model and the use of our business planning processes and tools.
- Additional responsibilities include handling special projects, such as assisting the National Accounts prospecting efforts or working cross functionally to support the Ally brand growth.












The Work Itself











- Manage the activities of the Sales team within assigned area, providing support and guidance as needed.
- Supervise, train, evaluate and provide ongoing professional development to staff and maintain superior product knowledge and provide training to Sales team on P&C product-related topics and industry trends.
- Co-travel with assigned producers on a regular basis and attend important meetings and symposiums with sales personnel, deliver sales presentations to key clients in coordination with sales representatives.
- Work collaboratively with Ally Insurance account teams including the Sales teams, Director of Sales, Senior Director of Sales (SDOS), and all other Ally field personnel in identifying new customers, conquesting new business and strategically managing the sales opportunity pipeline.
- Coordinate sales activities with all Ally Insurance channel partners and ensure recording and reporting of sales results are completed.
- Participates at account planning meetings and Area Sales meetings as appropriate.
- Maintain superior product knowledge and awareness of competitive property & casualty plans and sales procedures.
- Produce monthly, quarterly, and yearly analysis of region and assigned Sales team.
- Utilize Salesforce reports to track sales plans and continuously review all sales activities, targets, and strategies in order to maximize results.
- Monitor and analyze results to identify significant trends impacting goal achievement and then implement the appropriate corrective actions.
- Participate in the sale of P&C products as required. Actively apply Situational Leadership in coaching.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 9+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- Automotive retail, insurance, banking, and/or financial services experience.
- Property & Casualty insurance experience.
- Property and Casualty producer license required; if not currently licensed, Ally will provide training and licensing within 3 months of hire as required for the role. Ally will facilitate non-resident licensing in remaining 50 U.S. jurisdictions.
- High school diploma or GED equivalent required. Bachelor's Degree in related field is preferred as well as industry designations. i.e. Chartered Property Casualty Underwriter (CPCU), Associate in Risk Management (ARM) etc.
- Property & Casualty Insurance experience, auto or dealer financial services industry knowledge, sales training experience and experience in managing others preferred.
- Ability to develop business relationships with dealer principles and decision makers.
- Excellent communication, interpersonal and organizational skills.
- Well-developed business management, presentation and relationship selling skills.
- High level of accountability and ability to effectively prioritize tasks.
- Strong problem-solving skills and ability to objectively manage conflicting priorities.
- Ability to function in a matrix organization.
- Dealership operations and insurance product knowledge preferred.
- Ability to work with PC applications.
- Ability to work independently.
- Assist with local marketing campaigns. #LI-Remote #DFS












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

.































General information









Career area



Portfolio Management




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21715




Posted Date



02-25-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










This role is on a hybrid schedule in our Charlotte, NC office with a few days in office weekly.

Ally is seeking a Portfolio Manager to help establish and develop a new portfolio management function, supporting the newly formed centralized Consumer Banking Project Management Office (PMO) division. This individual contributor role provides an exciting opportunity for an experienced professional to define the approaches and standards that will shape the future of Ally’s portfolio management capabilities. The Portfolio Manager will engage directly with business, digital, and technology partners across all levels of the organization.













The Work Itself











- Own and manage processes related to capacity management, including the development and maintenance of reporting tools and dashboards to monitor progress against plan
- Identify and implement processes to address project issues, mitigate risks, and provide proactive insights to stakeholders related to the Consumer Banking roadmap
