Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

At Ally, you get a startup feel, but experience the benefits of a company that’s worked out the kinks and is fulfilling its purpose. We’re always evolving and see that as a good thing. From owning our work to seeing its impact in the real world, our team is relentless in finding new ways technology can help make experiences better and help people. We are problem solvers, we value diverse thinking, we support one another, and we challenge ourselves to think bigger in the journey to deliver customer-obsessed tech solutions.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

 

The Work Itself

  • Design and implement highly available, scalable infrastructure systems that support mission-critical production services, including automated deployment pipelines, observability platforms, and disaster recovery                                                                                                                         
  • Lead incident response and postmortem processes, diving deep into complex distributed systems failures to identify root causes and drive systemic reliability improvements across engineering teams           
  • Develop and maintain service level objectives (SLOs) and error budgets, using data-driven approaches to balance feature velocity with system reliability and guide organizational decision-making             
  • Build tooling and automation to eliminate toil, improve operational efficiency, and enable engineering teams to safely deploy and operate services with minimal manual intervention

The Skills You Bring

Minimum Qualifications
  • 7+ years of relevant experience 
  • Bachelor's degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent

Preferred Qualifications
  • 5+ years of experience in site reliability engineering, systems engineering, or DevOps roles with a proven track record of maintaining large-scale production systems                                                   
  • Deep expertise in cloud AWS including infrastructure as code tools like Terraform, CloudFormation, or Pulumi 
  • Experience defining and measuring SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets, and using them to drive reliability improvements and inform product decisions
  • Proficiency in AI development                                                                                   
  • Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, Go, or Node with the ability to write production-quality code for automation, tooling, and system integration                                          
  • Extensive experience with container orchestration (ECS or similar) and microservices architectures in production environments                                                          
  • Proficiency with observability and monitoring tools such as: Dynatrace, Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, New Relic, or similar and experience building comprehensive monitoring and alerting systems                               
  • Solid understanding of networking concepts, load balancing, CDNs, DNS, and distributed systems principles including consensus algorithms and failure modes
  • Hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines and GitOps workflows using tools like Jenkins, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, or CircleCI
  • Strong incident management and troubleshooting skills with the ability to quickly diagnose and resolve complex production issues under pressure
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills with the ability to influence technical direction across multiple teams and mentor engineers at various levels

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Base Pay Range: $110000 - $180000 USD
An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer. 
Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

The Opportunity










Ally’s model hosting Platform team is looking for an experienced software engineer to design, build and contribute to our AI/ML model hosting platform that enables modeling teams across the organization to host their AI/ML models as APIs in easy, self-serve, safe and performant way. We aim to operate like a startup in an enterprise environment, moving quickly and iteratively working with our partners to get to the best solution. This role is focused on end-to-end Cloud Native Application Development with Python. Proven expertise in developing secure and robust software applications, organizational leadership, collaboration and communication skill is required.




At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position













The Work Itself










o Work closely with technical and business partners to understand and translate technical requirements into technical specifications for delivering new features and capabilities

o Design and implement clean, quality, and testable applications that supports rapid delivery

o Deploy cloud infrastructure with Infrastructure as code, as well as build/maintain CI/CD pipelines making updates as required.

o Find creative and innovative approaches to complex problems while working, learning, and collaborating in a cross-functional agile team.

o Be passionate and continue to advance software craftsmanship within Ally

o Size/estimate user stories/tasks and assist in the development of a continuous release strategy.

o Contribute to technical documentation and specifications, as required.

o Contribute/ lead code reviews.













The Skills You Bring











- At least 5+ years of experience in Python programming including application service development.
- Ability to design and build serverless and stateful systems on AWS, that are scalable and highly available.
- At least 5 years of AWS experience with a variety of services, in priority order but not limited to:
- Lambda, ECS, S3, Dynamo DB, API gateway, Step Functions, SQS, IAM, Security Groups.
- Experience with Infrastructure as code (Terraform preferred).
- Experience with snowflake
- Experience with microservice development patterns.
- Experience working in an agile environment.
- Experience/ Working knowledge configuring and deploying CI/CD pipelines
- Experience with version control like GitLab
- AI/ML experience/certification is a plus.
- Experience with Apigee is a plus.

jobTitle: Regional Growth Leader, F&I Dealer Products & Services - Quebec Sales Region
jobDescriptionText:
Regional Growth Leader, F&I Dealer Products & Services - Quebec Sales Region


.































General information









Career area



Insurance




Country



Canada




State



Quebec




Work Location(s)



QC




Remote?



Yes




Ref #



21765




Posted Date



02-27-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity











- Must be eligible to work in Canada, and reside in, or be willing to relocate to, within a one-hour drive of Quebec City or Montreal, Quebec, requiring approximately 50% travel servicing dealers throughout Quebec. Company vehicle provided.
- Create leads and sell F&I product line to targeted goals; close sales by building rapport with key decision makers within target accounts, explaining corporate program and service capabilities, while effectively overcoming objections.
- Develop sales plan and effectively manage resources to maximize return on expenditures.
- Operate within the growth channel which encompasses all new and used car franchised dealerships within the Canada.
- Work collaboratively with Ally Insurance's account teams and all other Ally field personnel in identifying new customers, conquest new business and strategically managing the sales opportunity pipeline.
- Successfully maintaining the account for servicing and income development activities include working with all car dealership personnel who are involved with F&I products to improve dealer business performance.
- The consultation, creation and sharing of sales strategies to appropriate dealership staff and follow-up of dealership to ensure accurate procedures are followed for reporting of products sold.
- Maintain current knowledge about the competition, market threats and challenges, and communicate those concerns with organization and maintain professional and technical knowledge by participating in training; remain active in the industry and in the respective area and trade associations.












The Work Itself











- Manage sales activity and provide essential training in all departments at dealerships relating to Ally Product offerings (Used Vehicle Dept., F&I Dept., Sales, Service, Accounting).
- Analyze statistical reports and data to monitor and manage dealer account performance for maximum efficiency and counsel dealers on best practices to maximize returns in the F&I, Used Car and Service Departments.
- Develop and execute territory plan to include identification of target conquest accounts and target performance accounts for focused activity to acquire new business or drive performance improvements relative to VSC and ancillary products.
- Assist dealership personnel and serve as a resource providing mechanical products and processing training expertise. Serve as a subject matter expert relative to VSC and ancillary products.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- Dealership experience or automotive b2b/outside sales experience.
- Fluently bilingual in French and English.
- Experience working in a dealership in a Business Manager role.
- Successful professional experience in automotive sales management or financial services management.
- P rior experience in promoting a wide range of automotive F&I products such as service contracts (extended warranties), prepaid maintenance, and ancillary products.
- Dealership operations and insurance product knowledge preferred.
- Ability to work independently or in a team setting.
- Ability to develop business relationships with dealer principles and decision makers.
- Excellent communication, interpersonal and organizational skills.
- Well-developed business management, presentation and relationship selling skills.
- Strong problem-solving skills and ability to objectively manage conflicting priorities.
- Demonstrate a high level of accountability, positive attitude, openness to change, self-motivation, and willingness to increase performance.
- Proficient in intermediate MS applications (Word, Excel, Access, PowerPoint and Outlook). #LI-Remote
#DFS












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. But Ally’s total compensation – or total rewards – extends beyond your paycheck and is designed to support and enrich your personal and professional life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 17 paid time off days in addition to 12 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading retirement plan with matching and company contributions, tuition reimbursement and employee stock purchase plan.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: extended health care (paramedical, vision, prescription drug, gender affirmation support), dental, health care spending account funds, life insurance, short and long-term disability coverage, and a well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy, and fertility support as well as parental and caregiver leave, back-up child and adult/elder day care program. Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

But Ally’s total compensation – or total rewards – extends beyond your paycheck and is designed to support and enrich your personal and professional life, including:

 
  • Time Away: Program starts at 17 paid time off days in addition to 12 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading retirement plan with matching and company contributions, tuition reimbursement and employee stock purchase plan.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: extended health care (paramedical, vision, prescription drug, gender affirmation support), dental, health care spending account funds, life insurance, short and long-term disability coverage, and a well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy, and fertility support as well as parental and caregiver leave, back-up child and adult/elder day care program.
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Senior Accountant - Mortgage Accounting


.































General information









Career area



Accounting




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21809




Posted Date



02-27-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










This Senior Accountant will be a member of Ally's Controller's Team. Reporting to the Director or Manager, the position will be responsible for assisting in the preparation of the consolidated financial statements of a public company and key responsibilities include gathering information from the various groups, pulling the information into the quarterly and annual financial statements and working thru the various levels of review from a number of business owners.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself











- Assist in the preparation of the quarterly and year-end financial statements (10-Q/10-K).
- Support qualitative improvements in control operations to reduce /eliminate control deficiencies.
- Analyze and reconcile more complex accounts, researching discrepancies and taking action to ensure accounts are balanced.
- May have supervision responsibility, which would include providing training and development to direct reports and performance evaluation.
- Provide insight into potential process improvements.
- Coordinate with a number of groups across the enterprise in gathering all the necessary materials.
- Participate in gathering the review comments and making any necessary changes to the financial statements.
- Assist in analytical accounting support to the internal business partners.
- Assists in the resolution of accounting problems thru the appropriate channels leaders, to aid in the understanding of accounting activities.
- Drive audit compliance within the areas for which you are responsible.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 3+ years of relevant experience
- Bachelor’s Degree in relevant field(s) of study or equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- Highly preferred: 3+ years previous work experience in Accounting or related field.
- Highly preferred: Bachelor's degree in Accounting, finance, or related. Master's degree, a plus.
- Highly preferred: Advanced MS Excel skills (pivot tables, v-lookups, etc.).
- CPA (or equivalent).
- Workday experience.
- General technical accounting and/or functional skills for area of expertise.
- Exhibit a strong work ethic and high degree of self-motivation.
- Sound oral and written communication skills.
- Ability to organize data, perform independent analysis, and provide explanations/conclusions to management.
- Ability to work with limited supervision while applying previous experience to execute all but the most complex tasks.
- Ability to lead small projects or contribute meaningfully to larger projects.
- Comfortable interacting with other personnel in group or support staff from other departments; may interact with other internal and external personnel (i.e., external auditors, regulators) on specific projects or more complex tasks, usually under supervision. #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.






Base Pay Range : $70000 - $120000 USD An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










This role is Detroit Hybrid preferred; however, remote option possible.

The Senior Actuarial Analyst is responsible for developing and supporting a robust reserving framework at Ally Insurance. The Senior Actuarial Analyst will be expected to independently lead projects from start to finish. This includes brainstorming, accurately executing advanced analytics, and effectively communicating results to Management and Senior Leadership. This role will have high visibility to Leadership and future advancement opportunities. Ideal candidates will have demonstrated leadership, communication, and technical skills. Programming skills a must . SQL/R/Python preferred.













The Work Itself











- Support a reserving framework for insurance business, including both standard actuarial techniques and advanced modeling
- Lead, develop, and communicate advanced analytics to Management and Senior Leadership
- Foster innovation within the team and across the broader organization
- Work collaboratively as a part of the product development, pricing, and/or reserving processes
- Develop reserve indications, produce reserve ranges, and perform advanced analytics
- Support development of themselves and other associates in an actuarial capacity and according to Ally vision and mission statement












The Skills You Bring











- ACAS or near ACAS preferred; 5 or more Casualty Actuarial Society exams required
- 4+ years of experience in actuarial or data science roles; exposure to advanced ratemaking and/or reserving a plus
- Strong communication and relationship building skills
- Programming skills in SQL & R
- Ability to adapt to change and adjust priorities quickly, as necessary and appropriate
- High level of problem solving and critical thinking capabilities
- Well-organized and detail-oriented
- Highly motivated and self-directed
- Minimum of Bachelor’s degree in risk management, finance, economics, actuarial science, mathematics, statistics, or related field
- Must be authorized to work in the US on a permanent basis #LI-Remote #LI-DFS

- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

The Opportunity










This role is Detroit Hybrid preferred, however remote option possible




The Senior Actuarial Analyst is responsible for developing and supporting a robust pricing and/or reserving framework at Ally Insurance. The Senior Actuarial Analyst will be expected to independently lead projects from start to finish. This includes brainstorming, accurately executing advanced analytics, and effectively communicating results to Management and Senior Leadership. The Senior Actuarial Analyst may be expected to train, mentor, and/or develop one entry-level actuarial analyst, overseeing project responsibilities and performance management. This role will have high visibility to Leadership and future advancement opportunities. Ideal candidates will have demonstrated leadership, communication, and technical skills. Programming skills a must. SQL/R/python preferred. Detroit office with a hybrid work schedule or remote.













The Work Itself











- Support a robust pricing and/or reserving framework for insurance business, including both standard actuarial techniques and advanced modeling
- Lead, develop, and communicate advanced analytics to Management and Senior Leadership
- Foster innovation within the team and across the broader organization
- Work collaboratively as a part of the product development, pricing, and/or reserving processes
- Develop rate and/or reserve indications, segmentation analyses, and predictive models
- Support development of themselves and other associates in an actuarial capacity and according to Ally vision and mission statement












The Skills You Bring











- Minimum of Bachelor’s degree in risk management, finance, economics, actuarial science, mathematics, statistics, or related field
- 5 or more Casualty Actuarial Society exams required. ACAS or near ACAS preferred;
- 4+ years of experience in actuarial roles; exposure to advanced ratemaking and/or reserving a plus
- Strong communication and relationship building skills
- Ability to adapt to change quickly and to easily adjust priorities as necessary and appropriate
- High level of problem solving and critical thinking capabilities
- Well-organized and detail-oriented
- Strong work ethic
- Programming skills in SQL/R/python
- Must be authorized to work in the US on a permanent basis

- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.

jobTitle: Senior Analyst - Advanced Business Analytics - Consumer Banking CX
jobDescriptionText:
Senior Analyst - Advanced Business Analytics - Consumer Banking CX


.































General information









Career area



Consumer Analytics




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21667




Posted Date



02-21-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










Ally work locations are Detroit MI or Charlotte NC. In office role with hybrid schedule



At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

Are you passionate about transforming data into insights that drive impactful business decisions? Reporting to the CX Analytics Manager, the Senior Data Analyst will collaboratively work with other analysts on the team and stakeholders to produce performance, control, fulfillment reporting and/or additional project-based analyses to support the Complaints team in creating reporting and analyses to aid in managing customer issues for the Invest, Wealth, Deposits, and Auto Dealer and Consumer servicing teams and matrix partners. In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to shape the way Ally leverages data by establishing routine reporting, leading complex analyses, optimizing data processes, and partnering across departments to deliver actionable insights that impact the customer experience. As a member of the CX Analytics team, you will be required to respond quickly to evolving business opportunities and maintain strong stakeholder relationships within all levels of the organization. As such, the ideal candidate must be passionate about delivering results, willing and eagerness to push boundaries, self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to sort through substantial amounts of information to pinpoint what is relevant.













The Work Itself











- Combine business knowledge and interpretation of data to support a team passionate about delivering insightful reporting and analysis around the customer experience to drive innovation within Ally
- Maintenance and creation of business intelligence, continuously monitor and evaluate reporting processes and tools for opportunities to improve efficiency, accuracy, and usability
- Leveraging advanced technical skills, lead analytic projects in response to stakeholder requests and strategic initiatives
- Apply creative problem solving and innovative thinking when answering business questions
- Provide mentorship on the design, build, and updating of business intelligence platforms to other analysts
- Coordinate with executive leadership, application development teams, product owners, learning & development teams, data engineers, and other matrix partners on data projects
- Partner with Data Governance to establish & maintain data quality monitoring by appropriately documenting completed work.
- Provide data submission support for internal & external audit needs












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 3+ year of relevant experience
- Bachelor's degree in relevant field of study or equivalent Preferred
- 3+ years of professional experience in an analytics based role
- 2+ years of experience in SQL coding, with exposure in Python or R.
- 3+ years of hands-on data architecture, maintenance, and analysis in any or all the subjects below.
- Complaint Management
- Contact Center Performance
- Process Monitoring
- Interaction Monitoring
- Workforce Management Strategies
- Customer Experience
- Audit participation
- Snowflake data architecture and tools, and knowledge in relational database principles preferred.
- Exposure to Salesforce CRM Data management, with an understanding of customer, case, or workflow data preferred.
- Experience working with large datasets and using tools such as Jupyter Notebooks, Excel, Posit (RStudio), GitLab, for version control, and dbt for data transformation and modeling.
- Experience with statistical analysis and quantitative techniques like clustering, decision-trees, linear regression, etc.
- Experience with business intelligence software such as Power BI, Tableau and/or Streamlit is essential
- Ability to transform data insights into business solutions and provide strategic recommendations to stakeholders to inﬂuence decisions with detailed analytical support and accuracy
- Experience working on complex initiatives, navigating cross-functional teams and working productively with varying levels of seniority and leadership styles #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

The Opportunity

Ally work locations are Detroit MI or Charlotte NC. In office role with hybrid schedule

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

Are you passionate about transforming data into insights that drive impactful business decisions? Reporting to the CX Analytics Manager, the Senior Data Analyst will collaboratively work with other analysts on the team and stakeholders to produce performance, control, fulfillment reporting and/or additional project-based analyses to support the Complaints team in creating reporting and analyses to aid in managing customer issues for the Invest, Wealth, Deposits, and Auto Dealer and Consumer servicing teams and matrix partners. In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to shape the way Ally leverages data by establishing routine reporting, leading complex analyses, optimizing data processes, and partnering across departments to deliver actionable insights that impact the customer experience. As a member of the CX Analytics team, you will be required to respond quickly to evolving business opportunities and maintain strong stakeholder relationships within all levels of the organization. As such, the ideal candidate must be passionate about delivering results, willing and eagerness to push boundaries, self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to sort through substantial amounts of information to pinpoint what is relevant. 

The Work Itself

  • Combine business knowledge and interpretation of data to support a team passionate about delivering insightful reporting and analysis around the customer experience to drive innovation within Ally 
  • Maintenance and creation of business intelligence, continuously monitor and evaluate reporting processes and tools for opportunities to improve efficiency, accuracy, and usability 
  • Leveraging advanced technical skills, lead analytic projects in response to stakeholder requests and strategic initiatives
  • Apply creative problem solving and innovative thinking when answering business questions
  • Provide mentorship on the design, build, and updating of business intelligence platforms to other analysts
  • Coordinate with executive leadership, application development teams, product owners, learning & development teams, data engineers, and other matrix partners on data projects
  • Partner with Data Governance to establish & maintain data quality monitoring by appropriately documenting completed work.
  • Provide data submission support for internal & external audit needs

The Skills You Bring

Minimum Qualifications
  • 3+ year of relevant experience 
  • Bachelor's degree in relevant field of study or equivalent
Preferred
  • 3+ years of professional experience in an analytics based role
  • 2+ years of experience in SQL coding, with exposure in Python or R.
  • 3+ years of hands-on data architecture, maintenance, and analysis in any or all the subjects below.
    • Complaint Management
    • Contact Center Performance
    • Process Monitoring
    • Interaction Monitoring
    • Workforce Management Strategies
    • Customer Experience
    • Audit participation
  • Snowflake data architecture and tools, and knowledge in relational database principles preferred.
  • Exposure to Salesforce CRM Data management, with an understanding of customer, case, or workflow data preferred.
  • Experience working with large datasets and using tools such as Jupyter Notebooks, Excel, Posit (RStudio), GitLab, for version control, and dbt for data transformation and modeling.
    • Experience with statistical analysis and quantitative techniques like clustering, decision-trees, linear regression, etc.
  • Experience with business intelligence software such as Power BI, Tableau and/or Streamlit is essential
  • Ability to transform data insights into business solutions and provide strategic recommendations to stakeholders to inﬂuence decisions with detailed analytical support and accuracy
  • Experience working on complex initiatives, navigating cross-functional teams and working productively with varying levels of seniority and leadership styles

In office role, Ally work locations Charlotte NC, Detroit MI, or Lewisville TX At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.
The Senior Director of Data & AI is a cross-functional leader who integrates Consumer Banking objectives with enterprise data and AI strategies. This leader will be responsible for the vision and expansion of our data platforms, and establishing governance standards and resilient data architecture needed to accelerate business outcomes. Beyond platform oversight, they will help drive AI enablement by partnering with enterprise teams to deploy practical AI solutions that align with our risk and compliance framework. They are expected to define success through clear metrics, manage complex cross functional dependencies, and communicate effectively to executive leaders. Ultimately, this leader is expected to cultivate a high performing engineering culture that prioritizes technical excellence and innovation.













The Work Itself











- Serve as subject matter expert for Consumer Banking data and AI platforms.
- Lead the platform roadmap for data and AI; oversee configuration, implementation, and optimization of data engineering solutions to meet priority business use-cases.
- Establish and enforce rigorous data reliability, security, backup, and resilience strategies.
- Create technical documentation and low level design to ensure effective operations/support of the infrastructure solution.
- Collaborate with business stakeholders, analytics teams, IT, and vendors to select appropriate solutions, integration patterns, and configurations that accelerate delivery of business value.
- Manage documentation and design artifacts (reference architectures, data lineage, configuration files, etc) to ensure effective operations, supportability, and knowledge transfer across the organization.
- Define and communicate metrics for platform health, data quality, delivery throughput, and value realization
- Comply with Change Management, Release Management, IT Risk & Compliance processes and standards
- Build and develop a high‑performing data engineering organization; mentor leaders, elevate engineering maturity, and drive continuous improvement in process.

Minimum Qualifications: 12+ years of relevant experience Bachelor's degree in relevant field of study or equivalent Preferred Qualifications:
- 12+ years of professional experience
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering or similar fields Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analysis, Mathematics, Economics, or related discipline
- Experience developing AWS tools, AI/ML framework, data governance platforms, cloud platforms, enterprise BI, SQL/Python/R languages
- Effective and professional oral and written communication skills; ability to translate detailed technical problems/solutions into easy to understand business terms
- Proven track record of providing thought leadership in the technology area -- and applying technology to solve business problems
- Experience leading high performing team, including managing managers
- Excellent problem-solving skills; Ability to ask the right questions and effectively diagnose the root cause of technical issues
- Ability to work with all levels of an organization; Strong communication, analytical and organizational skills
- Collaborative and team focused; Eagerness to learn

jobTitle: Senior Sourcing Analyst – Governance and External Labor Operations
jobDescriptionText:
Senior Sourcing Analyst – Governance and External Labor Operations


.































General information









Career area



Supply Chain




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21791




Posted Date



02-26-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










We are seeking a Senior Sourcing Analyst to support Governance and External Labor Operations as part of the Ally Supply Chain team. They will focus on driving transformative sourcing and category strategies for Ally’s external labor program. The successful candidate will work in close collaboration and partnership with Ally’s business units and enterprise functions to deliver forward-looking, proactive, and best in industry results in contingent labor strategies, practices and hiring activities.
Member of the Ally Supply Chain team are responsible for delivering external third-party labor services to Ally lines of the business through an inhouse vendor management system (VMS) and will be accountable for managing events in the VMS as well as management and oversight of SOW Based Worker resources and suppliers. The Senior Sourcing Analyst will support each Ally business line and corporate function to meet the requirements of the hiring managers and comply with corporate identity management standards. They will be responsible for working with suppliers to ensure worker profiles are entered in the vendor management system in a timely and accurate manner.
This role reports to the Ally Supply Chain Manager – Strategy, Governance, and Operations Office and will participate in driving industry-leading and progressive sourcing, supplier and category strategies for all third-party labor categories in direct support of Ally’s businesses.













The Work Itself











- Responsible for working with Ally businesses and functions to onboard external labor workers in Ally’s vendor management system (VMS) and manage worker life cycle activities in the VMS.
- Support the initiation, negotiation and management of supplier contracts and risk assessments, contract amendments, renewals, and terminations. Supports ongoing administration (e.g., moves, adds and changes) of all external resources, suppliers, and program improvement opportunities.
- Ensure complete, accurate and proactive management of all contract inventories, P2P system/project updates, data quality metrics, service levels, supplier risk assessments, and sourcing events as applicable for external third party labor.
- Oversee all external labor activities including data validations activities.
- Troubleshoot and resolve customer or supplier cases from Ally’s ticketing system.
- Responsible for all reporting and preparing executive level presentations to convey key aspects of external labor and to present improvement ideas to leadership and business line executive sponsors.
- Identify and collaborate with a variety of internal colleagues and workgroups critical to executing sourcing strategies, negotiating contracts, managing third party relationships and onboarding/offboarding supplier partners – e.g., Ally Legal group, Information Security, Third Party Risk Management, HR, Data Privacy, Compliance, Financial Planning and Analysis, CFOs, Supply Chain Shared Services, other Sourcing Managers, Leaders and team members, etc.
- Participate in highly effective fact-based recruiting practices, sourcing processes, and negotiation strategies to maximize business value and risk mitigation.
- Work with business partners to maximize compliance and optimize spend against established and preferred engagement procedures, buying channels, supplier contracts and related programs.
- Support the end-to-end management of critical supplier relationships including constructive handling or resolution of issues and disputes, risk management, contract inventory management, performance and scorecard management, negotiation strategies, etc.
- Support and enable all required supplier risk management activities within the Lines of Business.
- Identify and implement consistent and continuous process, policy, and performance improvement programs across all areas of responsibility.
- Create and maintain an environment of transparency, performance and risk management that encourages innovation, integrity, and teamwork.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 3+ years of experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent
Preferred Qualifications
- Highly preferred: 3+ years of strategic sourcing, negotiation, and other relevant procurement management experience.
- Highly preferred: Deep understanding of key business labor strategies, including objectives and challenges. Experience with financial services category management and sourcing expertise to devise and execute supply chain strategies that support and advance the business strategies is a huge plus.
- Highly preferred: experience with professional services or contingent labor programs and spend for information technology or managed services providers in a business environment, with particular emphasis on sourcing technology, application development and maintenance, finance, accounting, clerical, administration, and project management skills.
- Highly preferred: Experience using Microsoft Excel, PowerBI, or related for financial analysis and/or reporting.
- Bachelor Degree, a plus.
- Proficiency in computer applications such as Microsoft Office (Outlook, PowerPoint, Word, etc.), Vendor Management Systems, Salesforce, or related.
- Experience with supplier selection and management, contract negotiations, conflict management, etc.
- Demonstrated ability to lead a work effort to negotiate price, rates, details, contract terms and conditions.
- Analytical ability to develop and execute strategic and tactical plans.
- Must possess good individual communication skills, ability to multi task, and work independently with a strategic mindset.
- Ability to manage large projects across multiple parties while delivering results. #LI-Hybrid













How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

jobDescriptionHtml:
Senior Sourcing Analyst – Governance and External Labor Operations .

General information

Career area
Supply Chain
Work Location(s)
500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC
Remote?
No
Ref #
21791
Posted Date
02-26-26
Working time
Full time

Ally and Your Career

Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a Senior Sourcing Analyst to support Governance and External Labor Operations as part of the Ally Supply Chain team. They will focus on driving transformative sourcing and category strategies for Ally’s external labor program. The successful candidate will work in close collaboration and partnership with Ally’s business units and enterprise functions to deliver forward-looking, proactive, and best in industry results in contingent labor strategies, practices and hiring activities. 

Member of the Ally Supply Chain team are responsible for delivering external third-party labor services to Ally lines of the business through an inhouse vendor management system (VMS) and will be accountable for managing events in the VMS as well as management and oversight of SOW Based Worker resources and suppliers. The Senior Sourcing Analyst will support each Ally business line and corporate function to meet the requirements of the hiring managers and comply with corporate identity management standards. They will be responsible for working with suppliers to ensure worker profiles are entered in the vendor management system in a timely and accurate manner.

This role reports to the Ally Supply Chain Manager – Strategy, Governance, and Operations Office and will participate in driving industry-leading and progressive sourcing, supplier and category strategies for all third-party labor categories in direct support of Ally’s businesses.

The Work Itself

  • Responsible for working with Ally businesses and functions to onboard external labor workers in Ally’s vendor management system (VMS) and manage worker life cycle activities in the VMS.
  • Support the initiation, negotiation and management of supplier contracts and risk assessments, contract amendments, renewals, and terminations. Supports ongoing administration (e.g., moves, adds and changes) of all external resources, suppliers, and program improvement opportunities.
  • Ensure complete, accurate and proactive management of all contract inventories, P2P system/project updates, data quality metrics, service levels, supplier risk assessments, and sourcing events as applicable for external third party labor.
  • Oversee all external labor activities including data validations activities.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve customer or supplier cases from Ally’s ticketing system.
  • Responsible for all reporting and preparing executive level presentations to convey key aspects of external labor and to present improvement ideas to leadership and business line executive sponsors.
  • Identify and collaborate with a variety of internal colleagues and workgroups critical to executing sourcing strategies, negotiating contracts, managing third party relationships and onboarding/offboarding supplier partners – e.g., Ally Legal group, Information Security, Third Party Risk Management, HR, Data Privacy, Compliance, Financial Planning and Analysis, CFOs, Supply Chain Shared Services, other Sourcing Managers, Leaders and team members, etc.
  • Participate in highly effective fact-based recruiting practices, sourcing processes, and negotiation strategies to maximize business value and risk mitigation.
  • Work with business partners to maximize compliance and optimize spend against established and preferred engagement procedures, buying channels, supplier contracts and related programs.
  • Support the end-to-end management of critical supplier relationships including constructive handling or resolution of issues and disputes, risk management, contract inventory management, performance and scorecard management, negotiation strategies, etc.
  • Support and enable all required supplier risk management activities within the Lines of Business.
  • Identify and implement consistent and continuous process, policy, and performance improvement programs across all areas of responsibility.
  • Create and maintain an environment of transparency, performance and risk management that encourages innovation, integrity, and teamwork.

The Skills You Bring

Minimum Qualifications
  • 3+ years of experience
  • High school Diploma or GED equivalent

Preferred Qualifications
  • Highly preferred: 3+ years of strategic sourcing, negotiation, and other relevant procurement management experience.
  • Highly preferred: Deep understanding of key business labor strategies, including objectives and challenges. Experience with financial services category management and sourcing expertise to devise and execute supply chain strategies that support and advance the business strategies is a huge plus.
  • Highly preferred: experience with professional services or contingent labor programs and spend for information technology or managed services providers in a business environment, with particular emphasis on sourcing technology, application development and maintenance, finance, accounting, clerical, administration, and project management skills.
  • Highly preferred: Experience using Microsoft Excel, PowerBI, or related for financial analysis and/or reporting. 
  • Bachelor Degree, a plus.
  • Proficiency in computer applications such as Microsoft Office (Outlook, PowerPoint, Word, etc.), Vendor Management Systems, Salesforce, or related.
  • Experience with supplier selection and management, contract negotiations, conflict management, etc.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead a work effort to negotiate price, rates, details, contract terms and conditions.
  • Analytical ability to develop and execute strategic and tactical plans.
  • Must possess good individual communication skills, ability to multi task, and work independently with a strategic mindset. 
  • Ability to manage large projects across multiple parties while delivering results. 
#LI-Hybrid

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Base Pay Range: $70000 - $120000 USD
An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer. 
Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

ServiceNow Developer


.































General information









Career area



Technology




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21552




Posted Date



01-22-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










We are seeking (2) ServiceNow Developers to join our ServiceNow Demand Management team in either our Detroit, MI or Charlotte, NC offices. This role bridges business needs and technical solutions—combining analytical skills, process understanding, and platform expertise to design, build, and deliver scalable ServiceNow solutions that align with organizational goals.

The ideal candidates will be equally comfortable engaging with stakeholders to define requirements and working within ServiceNow to configure, script, and deploy enhancements.













The Work Itself










Key Responsibilities:

ServiceNow Development & Delivery


- Configure and develop within ServiceNow (forms, workflows, business rules, client scripts, UI policies, flow designer, integrations).
- Participate in the design and implementation of ServiceNow modules, custom applications, and integrations with third-party systems.
- Contribute to solution design reviews, testing, documentation, and deployment activities.
- Adhere to established governance, development standards, and best practices for the ServiceNow platform.
- Assist in platform upgrades, instance maintenance, and quality assurance activities. Continuous Improvement


- Identify opportunities to leverage ServiceNow capabilities for automation, efficiency, and improved user experience.
- Stay current with the ServiceNow product roadmap, releases, and emerging features.
- Provide input into ServiceNow roadmap planning and platform strategy. Stakeholder Engagement


- Facilitate workshops, demos, and user acceptance testing to ensure solutions meet business needs.
- Support process optimization efforts across ITSM, HRSD, CSM, and other ServiceNow modules as needed.
- Act as a liaison between business teams, technical teams, and platform governance.












The Skills You Bring










Required Qualifications


- Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field (or equivalent experience).
- 1 - 3 years of hands-on ServiceNow development experience (e.g., ITSM, HRSD, SecOps, or custom applications).
- Strong understanding of ServiceNow data models, flow designer, and configuration options.
- Proficiency in JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and ServiceNow’s scripting APIs.
- Ability to create detailed technical documentation, solution designs, and test scripts.
- Strong problem-solving ability and a proactive, collaborative approach. Preferred Qualifications


- ServiceNow Certified System Administrator (CSA); additional certifications (CIS – ITSM, HRSD, CSM, App Engine) are a plus.
- Experience with REST/SOAP integrations and Flow Designer
- Familiarity with CMDB, Discovery, and platform governance.
- Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills. #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. The Opportunity

We are seeking (2) ServiceNow Developers to join our ServiceNow Demand Management team in either our Detroit, MI or Charlotte, NC offices. This role bridges business needs and technical solutions—combining analytical skills, process understanding, and platform expertise to design, build, and deliver scalable ServiceNow solutions that align with organizational goals.

The ideal candidates will be equally comfortable engaging with stakeholders to define requirements and working within ServiceNow to configure, script, and deploy enhancements.

The Work Itself

Key Responsibilities:

ServiceNow Development & Delivery

  • Configure and develop within ServiceNow (forms, workflows, business rules, client scripts, UI policies, flow designer, integrations).
  • Participate in the design and implementation of ServiceNow modules, custom applications, and integrations with third-party systems.
  • Contribute to solution design reviews, testing, documentation, and deployment activities.
  • Adhere to established governance, development standards, and best practices for the ServiceNow platform.
  • Assist in platform upgrades, instance maintenance, and quality assurance activities.

Continuous Improvement

  • Identify opportunities to leverage ServiceNow capabilities for automation, efficiency, and improved user experience.
  • Stay current with the ServiceNow product roadmap, releases, and emerging features.
  • Provide input into ServiceNow roadmap planning and platform strategy.

 Stakeholder Engagement

  • Facilitate workshops, demos, and user acceptance testing to ensure solutions meet business needs.
  • Support process optimization efforts across ITSM, HRSD, CSM, and other ServiceNow modules as needed.
  • Act as a liaison between business teams, technical teams, and platform governance.

The Skills You Bring

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field (or equivalent experience).
  • 1 - 3 years of hands-on ServiceNow development experience (e.g., ITSM, HRSD, SecOps, or custom applications).
  • Strong understanding of ServiceNow data models, flow designer, and configuration options.
  • Proficiency in JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and ServiceNow’s scripting APIs.
  • Ability to create detailed technical documentation, solution designs, and test scripts.
  • Strong problem-solving ability and a proactive, collaborative approach.

 Preferred Qualifications

  • ServiceNow Certified System Administrator (CSA); additional certifications (CIS – ITSM, HRSD, CSM, App Engine) are a plus.
  • Experience with REST/SOAP integrations and Flow Designer
  • Familiarity with CMDB, Discovery, and platform governance.
  • Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.

.

Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position. We are looking for a passionate and dedicated Data Scientist with solid technical skills, good communication, and analytical experience. This person will be responsible for estimating and monitoring various consumer auto originations credit risk metrics and their impact to pricing of consumer products. They will also build tools for analyzing price sensitivities and optimizing pricing strategies. The ideal candidate is familiar with data science techniques and machine learning algorithms and can apply them in a dynamic business environment to support decisions with direct impact to the bottom line. Knowledge of predictive analytics, SQL and big data management, Python (or R) programming are needed as the foundation for this role.













The Work Itself











- Heavy emphasis on advanced coding and ability to create predictive and statistical models from the ground up.
- Incorporates the use of algorithms into analytics and modeling.
- Has knowledge of data and business acumen.
- Utilizes machine learning and experimental design techniques.
- Builds credit risk and pricing predictive and prescriptive analytical tools in support of data driven business decisions.
- Applies optimization algorithms to identify opportunities for achieving specific business objectives, such as minimizing risks and maximizing profitability.
- Streamlines data usage and interfaces information dashboards to create consistency and efficiencies across business processes.
- Learns how to apply best practices and to filter data into meaningful information.












The Skills You Bring











- Minimum 1 year of work experience in analytics is required.
- Bachelor's degree or higher is needed.
- Must be technically proficient in Snowflake SQL, Python, and visualization tools (e.g., Power BI).
- Some experience with credit risk analytics and pricing optimization are preferred.
- Knowledge of practical application of supervised and non-supervised machine learning algorithms is a plus (e.g., logistic regression, linear regression, decision trees, survival analysis, ensemble learning, cluster analysis, linear and non-linear programming).
- Ability to organize and explore large data sets to identify trends/insights as well as translate results into business recommendations.
- Ability to manage multiple projects and assignments, prioritize, and meet deadlines.
- Good communication (written and oral) and interpersonal skills.
- Ability to work independently and to collaborate with team members and business partners on assignments.

- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.

.

The Opportunity










At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

Are you passionate about data management and data analytics? Do you possess first-hand experience in cloud technologies, data warehousing, data integration? Do you believe in modern software development methods, teamwork, creativity, and collaboration within a diverse development environment to produce the next generation of data - analytics platforms and services?

If so, then we are interested in talking to you! Ally is seeking a Sr. Analyst - Data Management within Ally's Data Solutions Support team. The ideal candidate will have personal experience implementing data lakes, data analytics, and master data management solutions, including data ingestion, data homogenization - data preparation, data quality checking and management, structured and unstructured data processing, and analytics engineering.

Experience with modern self-service data capabilities, data management and governance, data modeling architecture is also a plus. Candidates having strong analytical skills, hands on development experience, strong SQL skills, ability to multi-task, and strong verbal and written communication skills will be best suited for the team.













The Work Itself











- Responsible for developing curated semantic layers for the auto business to support analytics engineering and AI product enablement
- Develop and maintain pipelines; partner with data engineering to deploy to Oracle and Snowflake.
- Apply data warehousing principles to design reliable, performant models.
- Extract and reconcile data from traditional and cloud warehouses for executive and operational insights.
- Create clean, tested, well-documented data layers to power AI use cases (Cortex search, agentic models).
- Validate AI outputs; curate a library of “Verified Queries” and enable fuzzy search via Cortex.
- Write and troubleshoot complex SQL; working knowledge of Python and dbt.
- Stay current on industry trends and lead data/analytics platform innovation.
- Collaborate cross-functionally to translate needs into tools, metrics, and visualizations.
- Operate within Agile; define/prioritize technical stories and manage multiple projects with clear status and risk communication.
- Metadata integration, quality checks, tags and other steps required to stand up a complete semantic layer that can be used as input for BI or AI products
- Able to do requirement gathering, documenting business metrics, lineage etc.,












The Skills You Bring











- 3 years of relevant experience required, 5 years of relevant experience preferred.
- High school diploma required, Bachelor's degree preferred. With a preferred field of study in Computer Science, Data Management, Data Science, Management Information System, Business Analytics
- Strong organizational, analytical, and interpersonal skills
- RDBMS – Snowflake, Oracle, SQL Server
- Programming Languages/Tools - SQL, Python, DBT
- Data platforms - AWS, Snowflake, Oracle
- AI Models Claude, ChatGPT
- Data Governance - Alation, Collibra
- Knowledge about Snowflake Semantic, Cortex Products will be a plus
