Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

Are you passionate about data management and data analytics? Do you possess first-hand experience in cloud technologies, data warehousing, data integration? Do you believe in modern software development methods, teamwork, creativity, and collaboration within a diverse development environment to produce the next generation of data - analytics platforms and services?

If so, then we are interested in talking to you! Ally is seeking a Sr. Analyst - Data Management within Ally's Data Solutions Support team. The ideal candidate will have personal experience implementing data lakes, data analytics, and master data management solutions, including data ingestion, data homogenization - data preparation, data quality checking and management, structured and unstructured data processing, and analytics engineering.

Experience with modern self-service data capabilities, data management and governance, data modeling architecture is also a plus.  Candidates having strong analytical skills, hands on development experience, strong SQL skills, ability to multi-task, and strong verbal and written communication skills will be best suited for the team.

The Work Itself

  • Responsible for developing curated semantic layers for the auto business to support analytics engineering and AI product enablement
  • Develop and maintain pipelines; partner with data engineering to deploy to Oracle and Snowflake.
  • Apply data warehousing principles to design reliable, performant models.
  • Extract and reconcile data from traditional and cloud warehouses for executive and operational insights.
  • Create clean, tested, well-documented data layers to power AI use cases (Cortex search, agentic models).
  • Validate AI outputs; curate a library of “Verified Queries” and enable fuzzy search via Cortex.
  • Write and troubleshoot complex SQL; working knowledge of Python and dbt.
  • Stay current on industry trends and lead data/analytics platform innovation.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to translate needs into tools, metrics, and visualizations.
  • Operate within Agile; define/prioritize technical stories and manage multiple projects with clear status and risk communication.
  • Metadata integration, quality checks, tags and other steps required to stand up a complete semantic layer that can be used as input for BI or AI products
  • Able to do requirement gathering, documenting business metrics, lineage etc.,

The Skills You Bring

  • 3 years of relevant experience required, 5 years of relevant experience preferred. 
  • High school diploma required, Bachelor's degree preferred. With a preferred field of study in Computer Science, Data Management, Data Science, Management Information System, Business Analytics
  • Strong organizational, analytical, and interpersonal skills
  • RDBMS – Snowflake, Oracle, SQL Server
  • Programming Languages/Tools - SQL, Python, DBT
  • Data platforms - AWS, Snowflake, Oracle
  • AI Models Claude, ChatGPT 
  • Data Governance - Alation, Collibra
  • Knowledge about Snowflake Semantic, Cortex Products will be a plus

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

jobDescriptionText:
Sr. Analyst - Measurement & Analytics Delivery


.































General information









Career area



Marketing




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21772




Posted Date



02-25-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










Reporting to the MDMA Strategy and Effectiveness Sr Director, this role will drive delivery excellence for marketing analytics initiatives by establishing Agile practices, coordinating cross-functional work, and ensuring timely, high-quality outcomes. You will serve as capacity manager for multiple measurement functions, enabling efficient prioritization, workload execution, stakeholder alignment, and transparent reporting. Your work will increase the impact of Ally’s marketing measurement and attribution efforts by keeping projects on track, removing impediments, and fostering continuous improvement across all business lines.

At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.













The Work Itself











- Serve as Scrum Master for analytics squads: facilitate sprint ceremonies (planning, daily stand-ups, refinement, reviews, retrospectives), uphold Agile principles, and coach team members on best practices.
- Partner with project teams and stakeholders (LOB Leads, Digital Marketing, Technology, Marketing Research, Content & Strategy) to define clear objectives, scope, acceptance criteria, and success metrics for marketing analytics initiatives.
- Own project planning and execution: create and maintain roadmaps, sprint plans, milestones, dependencies, risks, and resource plans across concurrent projects.
- Drive backlog management: ensure well-groomed epics/stories, prioritize work based on business value, and maintain alignment with strategic goals and KPI frameworks.
- Remove impediments: proactively identify and resolve blockers, coordinate with data owners and technology teams, and escalate risks with mitigation plans.
- Standardize processes and documentation: establish templates, playbooks, and operating rhythms for measurement plans, attribution analyses, KPI reporting, and cross-team handoffs.
- Improve delivery performance: track and report Agile and project health metrics (velocity, burn-down, cycle time, on-time delivery, stakeholder satisfaction) and lead retrospectives to implement actionable improvements.
- Coordinate cross-functional dependencies: align campaign timelines, data availability, and analytics deliverables across online/offline channels and platforms.
- Ensure transparent communication: prepare and present status updates, timelines, and dashboards to leadership and partners; maintain project artifacts and decision logs.
- Support change management: enable teams to adopt new tools, workflows, and governance for measurement and attribution; provide training and onboarding as needed.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 3+ years of experience
- High School Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- 3+ years experience in Agile project management, Scrum Mastery, or program management within analytics or technology environments.
- PMP, SAFe, or Agile certifications.
- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Project Management, Marketing, Analytics, or related field
- Demonstrated experience facilitating Agile ceremonies and coaching teams; Scrum Master certification (CSM, PSM).
- Proficiency with Agile/PM tooling such as Jira, Confluence, Trello, Asana, or equivalent; including the ability to build dashboards, workflows, and reports.
- Strong project planning and delivery skills; including scope definition, backlog management, dependency tracking, risk/issue management, and stakeholder communications.
- Familiarity with marketing analytics concepts (conversion funnels, KPIs, attribution, campaign measurement) and the data lifecycle; ability to translate technical work into business outcomes.
- Working knowledge of data and analytics practices; comfort collaborating with analysts and data scientists.
- Excellent verbal and written communication; adept at executive-ready updates and cross-functional facilitation.
- Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent initiatives, prioritize effectively, and drive accountability in ambiguous environments.
- Experience establishing repeatable processes, templates, and governance for analytics delivery.
- Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build trust, influence without authority, and foster a collaborative, continuous-improvement culture.
- Self-starter with an inquisitive, solutions-oriented mindset and creative problem-solving skills.












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

jobDescriptionText:
Summer 2026 Internship – Auto Finance (Cranberry Township, PA)


.































General information









Work Location(s)



2000 Westinghouse, PA




Remote?



No




Ref #



20000




Posted Date



06-02-25




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










The Opportunity
We’re in the business of putting people first and we’ve been doing just that for over 100 years. Our mission isn’t performative either: we show up for our customers when it comes to their financial needs. We’re making banking smarter and simpler while serving our communities. We value and encourage the mantra of working better together. That’s where you come into play. Test drive the tools in your educational toolbox and learn some new skills along the way with an 11-week paid internship with us. Your internship with Ally will move you from educated to experienced.
The Ally Internship Program guides and mentors students with development opportunities and experiences that support your career interests while also driving the business forward. You’ll have the opportunity to connect with diverse teams and functions across the company. You will interact with Ally’s leadership and learn from their personal successes. You will gain the experience to know what it means to be an Ally!
As part of our Internship Program, you will have an opportunity to:
- Have hands on experience and project-based work
- Attend Orientation, Executive Speaking Series, and learning sessions as an Intern cohort
- Participate in Mentoring Program and Giving Back Events
- Network with your peers, your team, and those Allys you find interesting
- Lead an end of assignment presentation
The Details
- Internship Program Timing: May 26 – August 7, 2026
- 40 hours each week with a mix of in-office and remote working days
- Project-based work
- Market competitive hourly compensation and a sign-on bonus, as well as financial housing assistance for those who qualify
- Successful interns will have the opportunity to return to Ally in an Early Talent Program or full-time role, or to a second internship, depending upon graduation timing.












The Work Itself










Areas of Opportunity:

· Commercial Underwriting - responsible for completing the financial analysis and related due diligence for extensions of inventory credit lines and other commercial loans (real estate, working capital, lines of credit) to current and prospective automotive dealership customers.

· Consumer Underwriting - this group evaluates, negotiates, and decisions consumer offerings within Ally's underwriting policies to further grow application and contract volume. They build and maintain a portfolio of dealers and provide best-in-class relationship management for their assigned territories.

· Credit Administration - This group is responsible for providing support directly and indirectly to our dealer customers that include performing daily tasks that support and accurately maintain our dealers wholesale accounts. This includes the onboarding of new retail relationships with automotive dealerships, oversight of collateral audits, funding and payoff of floorplan units, accounting and payment processing of all payments to/from automotive dealerships in the Ally commercial portfolio, and preparation and maintenance of collateral and security documentation.

· Portfolio Management/Dealer Management - facilitate the delivery and management of products and services to automotive dealers, including lines of credit for vehicle inventory, equipment, working capital and real estate loans, and commercial insurance products, to assist in dealership operations. Roles and responsibilities include credit underwriting, new business development, loan closing, payment processing and account management.

· Sales Analytics - Analyze data and trends among a variety of sales functions within the Auto Finance Regional Business Center. Be able to provide to senior leadership industry insights, program updates, and custom reports when necessary. Support sales teams with proofs, identify opportunities, and mitigate risks through data analysis.













The Skills You Bring










The Skills You Bring
Required qualifications.
- Rising Junior or Senior (during the time of internship) in a full-time undergraduate program.
- Major/Course of Study: Business, Finance, or related field preferred.
- Excellent leadership, interpersonal and communication skills.
- Demonstrates Analytical Thinking, Collaboration, Learning Agility.
- Available for the full duration of the internship program.
- Eligible to work in the US for any employer without sponsorship from the company now or in the future.












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).

- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.

The Opportunity

The Opportunity

We’re in the business of putting people first and we’ve been doing just that for over 100 years. Our mission isn’t performative either: we show up for our customers when it comes to their financial needs. We’re making banking smarter and simpler while serving our communities. We value and encourage the mantra of working better together.
That’s where you come into play. Test drive the tools in your educational toolbox and learn some new skills along the way with an 11-week paid internship with us. Your internship with Ally will move you from educated to experienced.

The Ally Internship Program guides and mentors students with development opportunities and experiences that support your career interests while also driving the business forward. You’ll have the opportunity to connect with diverse teams and functions across the company. You will interact with Ally’s leadership and learn from their personal successes. You will gain the experience to know what it means to be an Ally!

As part of our Internship Program, you will have an opportunity to:
  • Have hands on experience and project-based work
  • Attend Orientation, Executive Speaking Series, and learning sessions as an Intern cohort
  • Participate in Mentoring Program and Giving Back Events
  • Network with your peers, your team, and those Allys you find interesting
  • Lead an end of assignment presentation

The Details
  • Internship Program Timing: May 26 – August 7, 2026
  • 40 hours each week with a mix of in-office and remote working days
  • Project-based work
  • Market competitive hourly compensation and a sign-on bonus, as well as financial housing assistance for those who qualify
  • Successful interns will have the opportunity to return to Ally in an Early Talent Program or full-time role, or to a second internship, depending upon graduation timing.

The Work Itself

Areas of Opportunity:

·       Commercial Underwriting - responsible for completing the financial analysis and related due diligence for extensions of inventory credit lines and other commercial loans (real estate, working capital, lines of credit) to current and prospective automotive dealership customers.

·       Consumer Underwriting - this group evaluates, negotiates, and decisions consumer offerings within Ally's underwriting policies to further grow application and contract volume. They build and maintain a portfolio of dealers and provide best-in-class relationship management for their assigned territories.

·       Credit Administration - This group is responsible for providing support directly and indirectly to our dealer customers that include performing daily tasks that support and accurately maintain our dealers wholesale accounts. This includes the onboarding of new retail relationships with automotive dealerships, oversight of collateral audits, funding and payoff of floorplan units, accounting and payment processing of all payments to/from automotive dealerships in the Ally commercial portfolio, and preparation and maintenance of collateral and security documentation. 

·       Portfolio Management/Dealer Management - facilitate the delivery and management of products and services to automotive dealers, including lines of credit for vehicle inventory, equipment, working capital and real estate loans, and commercial insurance products, to assist in dealership operations.  Roles and responsibilities include credit underwriting, new business development, loan closing, payment processing and account management.

·       Sales Analytics - Analyze data and trends among a variety of sales functions within the Auto Finance Regional Business Center.  Be able to provide to senior leadership industry insights, program updates, and custom reports when necessary.  Support sales teams with proofs, identify opportunities, and mitigate risks through data analysis.

The Skills You Bring

The Skills You Bring

Required qualifications.
  • Rising Junior or Senior (during the time of internship) in a full-time undergraduate program.
  • Major/Course of Study: Business, Finance, or related field preferred. 
  • Excellent leadership, interpersonal and communication skills.
  • Demonstrates Analytical Thinking, Collaboration, Learning Agility.
  • Available for the full duration of the internship program.
  • Eligible to work in the US for any employer without sponsorship from the company now or in the future.

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:

  • Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

jobTitle: Supply Chain Transformation: Governance & Change Manager
jobDescriptionText:
Supply Chain Transformation: Governance & Change Manager


.































General information









Career area



Supply Chain




Work Location(s)



500 Woodward Avenue, MI, 601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21699




Posted Date



02-13-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










Join the Supply Chain Transformation (SCT) team to own and elevate our governance and change management disciplines across transformation initiatives. You will steward the SCT Project Governance and Change Management playbooks, frameworks, templates, and calendars; guide teams on project types and requirements; and run formal tollgates and forums to ensure alignment, rigor, and compliance with enterprise standards.
You’ll lead change management for strategic programs—shaping strategy, crafting and reviewing communications, coordinating go-lives and bundled releases, and driving stakeholder adoption—while maintaining a consistent transformation narrative and creative strategy.
You’ll build strong cross-functional relationships, proactively support project owners ahead of releases, and champion an environment of innovation, integrity, and teamwork, continually applying industry best practices to deliver best-in-class results.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.












The Work Itself










Project Governance
- Own, execute, and maintain the SCT Project Governance Playbook & associated project guides and templates
- Drive Governance improvements using best practices and internal Ally Enterprise Requirements
- Ensure SCT Projects comply with Project Governance by reviewing and challenging deliverables
- Advise Supply Chain Transformation team on project type and requirements based on the project’s specific problem statement and impact to the enterprise
- Organize and drive official SCT tollgates and forums including setting agendas, reconciling metrics, communicating results, maintaining charters, presenting applicable updates, and documenting project status results
- Utilize peer Product Owners when applicable to execute technical aspects of transformation project governance Change Management
- Own, execute, and maintain the Change Management Playbook and Framework
- Own and maintain broader SCT Change Management Creative Strategy and map projects to ensure consistent transformation theme usage
- Drive key Change Management activities for strategic projects leading to maximum stakeholder adoption
- Review and challenge Change Management Strategies for projects and opine/provide feedback to ensure full business and enterprise adoption of transformation initiatives
- Provide advice and guidance to project owners and stakeholders at all levels of the organization to support decision-making and facilitate change management
- Own Change Management communication templates, draft and execute key communications & review drafts of all Chance Management communications
- Maintain the SCT project Go Live Calendar and change releases; coordinate bundled releases by audience
- Own SCT communications and other messaging channels showcasing our transformation journey inclusive of goals, wins, and vision, etc.
- Proactively reach out to project owners on upcoming project releases that need Change Management discussion
- Develop and maintain strong working relationships with internal business partners
- Create an environment that encourages innovation, integrity and teamwork
- Stay updated with industry trends, best practices, and emerging technologies to continuously enhance the Change Management strategy













The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High school diploma or GED equivalent
Preferred Qualifications
- Highly preferred: 5+ years of experience leading highly complex and challenging change management programs with best-in-class results leading to strong user adoption. Experience in the financial services industry within the Supply Chain / Procurement function is ideal.
- Highly preferred: Strong understanding of change management principles and strategies as evidenced through change management certifications such as CCMP, Proci, AMPG, or similar
- Highly preferred: 3+ years of experience governing projects against a six sigma DMAIC framework
- Highly preferred: Bachelor's degree; MBA, a plus
- Highly preferred: Experience governing and managing change for Supply Chain / Procurement transformational initiatives, preferably with leading financial services institutions or consulting firms, inclusive of process improvement, technology upgrades, and data transformation using six sigma methodology such as DMAIC framework, value stream mapping, and Kaizen
- Strong written and verbal communication skills with an ability to influence and communicate effectively across business levels (both vertically and horizontally)
- Ability to build strong relationships with internal business partners and experience working with multiple business partners in a collaborative and team-oriented manner
- Ability to support multiple assignments and stakeholders, prioritize workload and coordinate with teammates to meet deadlines
- Strong analytical skills and proficiency with Excel
- Strong presentation skills including proficiency in PowerPoint
- Comfortable working in an evolving and dynamic business environment with ability to seamlessly balance competing priorities and customers
- Flexible and can work independently and in a team environment
- Detail-oriented, energetic, motivated, and action-oriented #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.






Base Pay Range : $90000 - $150000 USD An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the greater Toronto or surrounding area to support the sales territory for this work from home role with local travel. Company vehicle provided.
- Responsible for developing and increasing vehicle remarketing fee revenue within a defined territory to attain territory targets.
- Grow the active auto dealership base through equipping, training, and coaching them on how to solve used vehicle needs utilizing our online vehicle remarketing platform, SmartAuction.
- In-person customer contacts on a daily basis throughout the assigned territory, designed to grow the engaged dealer base and increase utilization.
- Attend meetings and conventions, dealer and remarketing trade shows and managing special projects as requested by management.












The Work Itself











- Increase the buying and selling of used vehicles through SmartAuction.
- Solicit consignor postings.
- Train, equip, and enable dealer customers to utilize SmartAuction.
- Facilitate new dealer introductions, onboarding, training and providing overall sales support as needed.
- Ensure dealer customers are current on all promotions and incentives being offered.
- Daily in-person dealer customer contacts are required.
- Assist or lead Zoom or in-person training for dealers as needed.
- Utilize Salesforce for dealer meeting recaps and planning.












The Skills You Bring

- 3+ years of experience.

- b2b/outside sales experience preferred.
- Bachelor's degree in related field strongly preferred.
- Ability to provide advice/direction in primary areas of expertise.
- Leverage technical expertise and relationships to contribute to strategy and drive business results.
- Contribute to and manage projects. Compensation Range

- Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan, with a target incentive that may range from 7% to 9% of base pay.
- Total Compensation Range: $74,900 - $109,000

  • Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the greater Toronto or surrounding area to support the sales territory for this work from home role with local travel. Company vehicle provided. 
  • Responsible for developing and increasing vehicle remarketing fee revenue within a defined territory to attain territory targets. 
  • Grow the active auto dealership base through equipping, training, and coaching them on how to solve used vehicle needs utilizing our online vehicle remarketing platform, SmartAuction. 
  • In-person customer contacts on a daily basis throughout the assigned territory, designed to grow the engaged dealer base and increase utilization. 
  • Attend meetings and conventions, dealer and remarketing trade shows and managing special projects as requested by management.

The Work Itself

  • Increase the buying and selling of used vehicles through SmartAuction.
  • Solicit consignor postings. 
  • Train, equip, and enable dealer customers to utilize SmartAuction.
  • Facilitate new dealer introductions, onboarding, training and providing overall sales support as needed.
  • Ensure dealer customers are current on all promotions and incentives being offered.
  • Daily in-person dealer customer contacts are required.
  • Assist or lead Zoom or in-person training for dealers as needed.
  • Utilize Salesforce for dealer meeting recaps and planning. 

The Skills You Bring

Minimum Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience.
  • High school Diploma or GED equivalent.

Preferred Qualifications

  • 3+ years in auto sector, preferably vehicle remarketing services industry.
  • b2b/outside sales experience preferred.
  • Bachelor's degree in related field strongly preferred.
  • Ability to provide advice/direction in primary areas of expertise.
  • Leverage technical expertise and relationships to contribute to strategy and drive business results.
  • Contribute to and manage projects.

Compensation Range

  • Base Pay Range: $70,000 - $100,000 CAD
  • Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan, with a target incentive that may range from 7% to 9% of base pay.
  • Total Compensation Range: $74,900 - $109,000
  • An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer. 

#LI-Remote

#DFS

How We'll Have Your Back

Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. But Ally’s total compensation – or total rewards – extends beyond your paycheck and is designed to support and enrich your personal and professional life, including:

 
  • Time Away: Program starts at 17 paid time off days in addition to 12 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading retirement plan with matching and company contributions, tuition reimbursement and employee stock purchase plan.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: extended health care (paramedical, vision, prescription drug, gender affirmation support), dental, health care spending account funds, life insurance, short and long-term disability coverage, and a well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy, and fertility support as well as parental and caregiver leave, back-up child and adult/elder day care program.
 

Who We Are:

 

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

jobDescriptionText:
Analyst - Quant Modeling


.































General information









Career area



Capital Markets




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21777




Posted Date



02-27-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










The Treasury Quantitative Analyst is an outstanding and ambitious role crafted to propel your career to the next level. You will play a pivotal role in centralizing, modernizing, and automating our Treasury data pipeline and analytics. This position demands an outstanding individual who is eager to optimize operational efficiency and elevate data visibility and accessibility in real-time, intraday, and end-of-day scenarios.













The Work Itself











- Build, implement, deploy, maintain, improve, store, and automate end-of-day and real-time processes, model outputs, and centralized data pipelines.
- Collaborate with Treasury leaders and partners to gather requirements and feedback for efficient, agile, tactical, and strategic projects that provide valuable benefits.
- Collaborate with Treasury IT partners to prioritize development, testing, project management, and product ownership in accordance with Ally policies and standards.
- Develop clear and coordinated Python code in line with PEP8 standards and internal guidelines within our strategic Collaborative Python Computing (CPC) platform framework.
- Build Python code for internal and vendor wrappers, data APIs, models, web servers, end-of-day and real-time processes, and dependency scheduling.
- Identify and eliminate manual processes and single points of failure across all of Treasury.
- Self-sufficiently identify defects, gaps, and pitfalls, and rapidly overcome them with robust long-term tactical or strategic solutions requiring minimal assumptions.
- Collaborate and consult with business and IT partners across multiple lines of business, Risk, Accounting, and Treasury.












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 1+ years of experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- An Advanced Degree or equivalent experience in Computational Finance, Financial Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Economics, Finance, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field is preferred. Candidates with a Bachelor's degree and relevant work experience will also be considered.
- 1+ years of Treasury, Capital Markets, Accounting, Balance Sheet, Finance, Risk, or relevant experience.
- 1+ years of development experience in Python.
- 1+ years of data modeling experience.
- 1+ years of data pipeline and IT architecture experience.
- 1+ years process automation and graphical display development experience.
- Strong understanding of SQL, data normalization, indexing, joins, primary and foreign keys.
- Strong Excel VBA skills.
- Familiarity with Dash/Flask, React, Vue, Angular or any other web user interface framework.
- Familiarity with a main-stream source control system.
- Familiarity with distributed systems computing.
- Familiarity with AWS components like ECS, CI/CD pipeline, and cloud capabilities.
- Familiarity with Infrastructure As Code Tools like Terraform.
- Familiarity with Jira Application, Jira API and Jira Data Model.
- Familiarity with AI use case implementation.
- Self-starter, able to produce quickly and independently.
- Strong communication (written, verbal, and interpersonal) skills.
- Integrity, maturity, dependability, a positive forward-looking professional attitude.












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.






Base Pay Range : $65000 - $115000 USD An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

jobDescriptionText:
Treasury Manager - Balance Sheet Management


.































General information









Career area



Treasury




Work Location(s)



601 S. Tryon Street, NC




Remote?



No




Ref #



21820




Posted Date



02-26-26




Working time



Full time












Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










Join Ally’s Balance Sheet Management Asset Forecasting team as a Manager (Individual Contributor). In this role, you will be a key member of the team responsible for developing, maintaining, and communicating high-quality asset forecasts that inform balance sheet strategy, capital and liquidity planning, earnings outlooks, and interest rate risk management. You will partner closely with FP&A and Capital Markets to translate portfolio dynamics, market conditions, and strategic initiatives into robust, explainable projections. This is an ideal opportunity for a finance professional with deep forecasting/modeling expertise, strong business acumen, and the ability to influence through insight.
At this time, Ally will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.












The Work Itself











- Core Responsibilities: Net Financing revenue forecasting, reporting and analysis.
- Lead end-to-end asset forecasting for assigned portfolios (e.g., consumer auto, mortgage, commercial, investment securities), including balances, interest income, yields, prepayments, and product mix.
- Own forecast production cycles for monthly outlooks, quarterly corporate forecasts, annual planning, and stress testing, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and strong controls.
- Develop, enhance, and document forecasting methodologies, overlays, and assumption frameworks; challenge models and inputs to ensure reasonableness and fidelity to business drivers.
- Lead cross-functional projects and model update cycles—defining scope and timelines, coordinating stakeholders (Treasury, Risk, FP&A, Business), executing methodology and assumption changes, managing testing/validation and controls, and delivering clear documentation and easily digestible communications for approvals and implementation.
- Translate complex results into clear narratives, executive-ready materials, and decision support for senior leadership.
- Support governance requirements, including model risk management, controls testing, documentation, and regulatory requests.
- Serve as a subject matter expert for assigned asset classes and mentor peers on best practices and standards.
- Contribute to creation of content for ALCO.
- Identifies significant, unusual and emerging risks.
- Mentoring junior level talent












The Skills You Bring










Minimum Qualifications
- 7+ years of relevant experience or equivalent combination of education and experience
- High school Diploma or GED equivalent Preferred Qualifications
- Highly preferred: 5+ years progressive experience in aligned area/functions (Structured Funding, Funding - Liquidity, Liquidity Risk, Treasury Operations, Capital and Balance Sheet Management, Capital Markets, Economics) in the financial industry.
- Highly preferred: Bachelor's degree in finance or economics.
- Highly preferred: Experience with Quantitative Risk Management (QRM) is a plus
- Possesses solid technical expertise, business and industry knowledge, and process and leadership capabilities.
- Well-developed project management, analytical and judgment skills.
- Familiarity with ALM concepts (NII, EVE, liquidity, hedging) and forecasting processes.
- Exceptional problem solving, attention to detail, and commitment to controls and documentation.
- Proven ability to manage multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment, ownership mentality and intellectual curiosity
- Demonstrated ability to influence others, build consensus, and communicate effectively.
- Strong technical skills including Excel and PowerPoint. #LI-Hybrid












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.






Base Pay Range : $110000 - $180000 USD An individual's position in the range is determined by the specific role, the scope and responsibilities of the role, work experience, education, certification(s), training, and additional qualifications. We review internal pay, the competitive market, and business environment prior to extending an offer.





Incentive Compensation: This position is eligible to participate in our annual incentive plan.

  • Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
  • Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
  • Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
  • Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
  • Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
  • To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf

Ally Financial is a customer-centric, leading digital financial services company with passionate customer service and innovative financial solutions. We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

 

Ally is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic disposition or any other reason protected by law.

 

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with physical or mental disabilities. For accommodation requests, email us at hrpolicy@ally.com. Ally will not discriminate against any qualified individual who is capable of performing the essential functions of the job with or without reasonable accommodation.

Ally and Your Career










Ally Financial only succeeds when its people do - and that’s more than some cliché people put on job postings. We live this stuff! We see our people as, well, people - with interests, families, friends, dreams, and causes that are all important to them. Our focus is on the health and safety of our teammates as well as work-life balance and diversity and inclusion. From generous benefits to a variety of employee resource groups, we strive to build paths that encourage employees to stretch themselves professionally. We want to help you grow, develop, and learn new things. You’re constantly evolving, so shouldn’t your opportunities be, too?
Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity










The Treasury Systems Analyst II serves as a key resource in managing and optimizing access to critical banking and treasury platforms. This role is responsible for ensuring that system users and groups are appropriately configured, supporting secure and compliant operations across multiple applications. The Analyst II leverages strong analytical abilities to monitor system activity, maintain accurate records, and uphold data quality standards. By collaborating with internal teams and business partners, the Treasury Systems Analyst II helps facilitate seamless user experiences and supports ongoing system enhancements. The position also contributes to periodic audits and reporting initiatives, playing an important part in maintaining regulatory compliance and operational excellence within the organization’s treasury functions.












The Work Itself











- Process User Provisioning Requests for Correspondent Banking and Treasury Applications.
- Process Funds Transfer Authority (FTA) Limit requests.
- Perform analytical reviews to ensure data integrity, record retention and compliance with policies and procedures.
- Work with Business Partners to ensure Correspondent Banking and Treasury Application User Groups contain the appropriate system entitlements.
- Maintain User, User Group and other Administrative settings in Correspondent Banking and Treasury Applications.
- Perform Monthly User List (MUL) reporting and Quarterly End User Access Campaign (EAC) for all Correspondent Bank and other applications as needed.
- Assist in testing and deployment of changes to Correspondent Bank and other applications as needed.













The Skills You Bring











- 1+ year of experience in the financial industry required; strong preference to candidates with systems administration experience including user provisioning and access management.
- Bachelor's degree required; degree in business, finance, information systems, or related, preferred.
- Advanced reporting skills in MS Excel (pivot tables, v-lookups, etc.) are required; PowerBI experience is highly preferred.
- Understanding of regulatory requirements and audit processes related to user access, record keeping, and reporting (e.g., Monthly User List, End User Access Campaign).
- Strong organizational and time management skills to handle multiple requests and reporting cycles.
- Demonstrate strong technical, analytical, and project management skills with the ability to work independently on moderately complex tasks.
- Communicate effectively and collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders.
- Exhibit sound judgment, self-motivation, and the ability to build consensus across groups. #LI-Hybrid













How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. Our Total Rewards program includes industry-leading compensation and benefits plus additional incentives that are designed to meet your needs and those of your family so you can get the most out of your career and your life, including:


- Time Away: Program starts at 20 paid time off days in addition to 11 paid holidays and 8 hours of volunteer time off yearly (time off days are prorated based on start date and program varies based on full or part-time status and management level).
- Planning for the Future: plan for the near and long term with an industry-leading 401K retirement savings plan with matching and company contributions, student loan pay downs and 529 educational save up assistance programs, tuition reimbursement, employee stock purchase plan, and financial learning center and financial coach access.
- Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.

.

Career area



Sales

United States




State



Washington

WA

Yes

Work Schedule: Ally designates roles as (1) fully on-site, (2) hybrid, or (3) fully remote. Hybrid roles are generally expected to be in the office a certain number of days per week as indicated by your manager. Your hiring manager will discuss this role's specific work requirements with you during the hiring process. All work requirements are subject to change at any time based on leader discretion and/or business need.












The Opportunity











- Candidate must reside in, or be willing to relocate to, the Greater Seattle, Tacoma, or Olympia, WA area, ideally within a 30-minute drive of the South Puget Sound metro market and Seattle/Tacoma area, requiring local travel throughout the surrounding sales market. Company vehicle provided.
- Successful account executives love to play. Creativity drives them – they throw new ideas against the wall and watch them stick. In this role, you’ll expand product offerings in existing business through cross-selling and increase market share with new clients. You’ll balance your compassion and empathy for everyone involved in the sales process. We see our account executives as problem solvers who are resilient, can face adversity, aren’t afraid of rejection and emerge as champions when times are tough. If you want flexibility, training to improve your skills and a management team that champions you and backs you up, Ally is the place to be.












The Work Itself











- You’ll sell finance & insurance (F&I) product lines to meet targeted goals by explaining corporate programs and service capabilities, while effectively overcoming objections.
- You’ll service current customers by maintaining rapport, making regular visits (there’s a lot of travel involved with this role), evaluating customer's needs for new opportunities, and replying to requests.
- You’ll also identify non-F&I opportunities and coordinate with the Insurance Specialist (identify new customers, estimate growth volume and qualify prospects).
- To ensure customers are fully knowledgeable with Ally’s products, you’ll assist in training F&I Managers within our dealerships.
- To sustain your success, you’ll maintain current knowledge about our competitors, market threats and challenges, maintain professional and technical knowledge by participating in training, and remain involved in the industry, respective area and trade associations.












The Skills You Bring











- 3+ years of experience required.
- High school diploma or GED equivalent required. Bachelors degree preferred.
- Dealership or b2b sales and automotive industry experience.
- The ability to develop relationships with dealer principals and decision makers.
- You have well-developed business management, presentation and relationship selling skills.
- Holding yourself accountable is important to you. Being effective at prioritizing tasks is second nature.
- You’ve branded yourself as a problem solver and you objectively manage conflicting priorities. #LI-Remote
#DFS












How We'll Have Your Back










Ally's compensation program offers market-competitive base pay and pay-for-performance incentives (bonuses) based on achieving personal and company goals. - Supporting your Health & Well-being: flexible health and insurance options including medical, dental and vision, employee, spouse and child life insurance, short- and long-term disability, pre-tax Health Savings Account with employer contributions, Healthcare FSA, critical illness, accident & hospital indemnity insurance, and a total well-being program that helps you and your family stay on track physically, socially, emotionally, and financially.
- Building a Family: adoption, surrogacy and fertility assistance as well as paid parental and caregiver leave, Dependent Day Care FSA back-up child and adult/elder care days and childcare discounts.
- Work-Life Integration: other benefits including Mentally Fit Employee Assistance Program, subsidized and discounted Weight Watchers® program and other employee discount programs.
- Other compensations: depending on the role for which you are considered, you may be eligible for travel allowances, relocation assistance, a signing bonus and/or equity.
- To view more detailed information about Ally’s Total Rewards, please visit this link: https://www.ally.com/content/dam/pdf/corporate/ally-total-rewards-snapshot.pdf Who We Are:

We are relentlessly focused on "Doing it Right" and being a trusted financial-services provider to our consumer, commercial, and corporate customers. For more information, visit www.ally.com.

jobDescriptionText:
Description & Requirements




Entry Level & Licensed Insurance Agents We’re seeking motivated, driven individuals who are eager to launch a rewarding career in the insurance industry. As an AmeriLife Health & Life Insurance Agent, you’ll play a critical role in helping clients protect what matters most—while building a lucrative, long-term career with unlimited growth potential. At AmeriLife, you won’t be limited to just one carrier. Our agents represent 50+ top-rated insurance companies, giving you the flexibility and product breadth to truly serve your clients’ needs with confidence and integrity.
What You’ll Do
- Deliver exceptional customer experiences by educating and guiding clients through informed insurance decisions
- Conduct personalized needs assessments to identify appropriate life, health, and retirement solutions
- Build and maintain strong relationships with both new and existing clients
- Use provided and proven sales tools, marketing resources, and leads to grow your book of business
- Operate with professionalism, integrity, and a client-first mindset
What We’re Looking For
- Self-motivated individuals with a strong desire to succeed
- Entrepreneurial mindset with a passion for personal growth
- A genuine interest in helping others plan for their future
- Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation
- No prior insurance experience required—we provide training
What We Offer
- Comprehensive Training & Development Online coursework, classroom instruction, and hands-on field training to set you up for success
